## Your task

Examine pull request  using the GitHub CLI command.

Ignore any changes to:
- .specstory/history files
- .claude/ files
- .cursor/ files
- ./specstory binary
- CLAUDE.md
- AGENT.md
- changelog.md

Please code review the pull request.

Review line by line and explain to me the purpose of each change. Use file names and line numbers to reference the changes, but also sequentially number every observation so we can easily reference them.

In the line by line review, pay attention to:

- clarity of variable names
- Go lang idiomatic code
- code clarity and simplicity, readable over clever
- "why" comments, not "how" comments
- missing comments
- missing log output
- missing analytics tracking
- single function exit point where possible (immediate guard clauses are OK)
- verify the code has to exist, is actually needed, and is in use
- verify the code is DRY, and doesn't replicate the same or similar code
- for test cases, ensure a data-driven approach is being used rather than lots of repetitive test code

Format your line by line review like this example:

```
  cmd/remote.go

  Line 12 (removed): Removed unused import of pkg/service package
  - (1) ✅ Good - Cleaning up unused imports is proper Go hygiene

  Lines 157-162 (modified): Changed from loading config directly to using RPC client
  // Old: config, err := service.LoadConfig(dir)
  // New: rpcClient, err := client.NewRPCClient(dir)
  - (2) ✅ Good architectural decision - Now operates via daemon RPC instead of direct file access
  - (3) ✅ Proper defer cleanup pattern for RPC client
  - (4) ✅ Variable name rpcClient is clear and follows conventions

  Lines 164-189 (new): Added remote status check before disabling
  - (5) ✅ Good UX - Detects current connection state to provide better feedback
  - (6) ✅ Type assertions use the two-value form (ok pattern) for safety
  - (7) ⚠️ Nested if statements become deeply indented (4 levels) - could be refactored for readability
  - (8) ✅ Variable names wasConnected, oldURL are descriptive
  - (9) ❓ Missing error handling - if remote.status call fails, we continue anyway. Should we?

  ---
  pkg/remote/sync.go
  Lines 260-265 (modified): Success messages now come after RPC call
  - (10) ✅ Comment "config is now saved" is helpful context
  - (11) ⚠️ Misleading comment placement - comment says "config is now saved" but we can't be certain from this code's perspective (that happens in the daemon)
```

In addition to the line by line review, suggest the 2-5 most important improvements for this code review.

In addition to the most important improvements, let's also think about if any of these changes could benefit from new, updated or expanded unit tests. We focus our unit tests on complicated logic, and combinatorial scenarios, not on coverage or completeness for completeness sake. Better to not have a unit test than to have tests that are simplistic or tautological. We also don't test 3rd party library or language features, only our own code.

For any new test cases that were added, confirm the tests are:

- Not trivial - They test real logic in our code and aren't just testing Go lang or 3rd party libraries
- Not tautological - They would catch real bugs
- Well-designed - Each assertion accurately verifies specific logic and complexity in the code being tested
- Reliable - They won't be flaky, don't rely on external state, and don't catch false positives.
- Not repetitive - They test different scenarios, and use data-driven testing rather than repetitive test code.

188	+
189	+// ResumeConfig holds preferences for the `specstory resume` picker. view_mode is a
190	+// user setting; last_agent is written automatically as you resume.
191	+type ResumeConfig struct {
192	+	// ViewMode is the picker layout: "dense" (more sessions) or "sparse" (more detail).

194	+	// LastAgent is the provider id of the agent you last resumed into (default target).

203	+// GetResumeViewMode returns the resume picker view mode, defaulting to "dense".
204	+func (c *Config) GetResumeViewMode() string {

206	+		return "sparse"
207	+	}
208	+	return "dense"
209	+}
210	+
211	+// GetResumeLastAgent returns the provider id of the last-resumed agent, or "".
212	+func (c *Config) GetResumeLastAgent() string {
213	+	return c.Resume.LastAgent
214	+}
215	+
216	+// SaveResumePrefs persists the resume view mode and last-resumed agent to the
217	+// user-level config (~/.specstory/cli/config.toml). It upserts ONLY the [resume]
218	+// section, preserving the rest of the file (comments and other sections), so the
219	+// self-documenting template stays intact. Pass an empty value to leave that key out.
220	+func SaveResumePrefs(viewMode, lastAgent string) error {
221	+	path := getUserConfigPath()
222	+	if path == "" {
223	+		return fmt.Errorf("could not determine user config path")
224	+	}
225	+	if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path), 0o755); err != nil {
226	+		return fmt.Errorf("creating config directory: %w", err)
227	+	}
228	+
229	+	data, err := os.ReadFile(path)
230	+	if err != nil {
231	+		if !os.IsNotExist(err) {
232	+			return fmt.Errorf("reading config: %w", err)
233	+		}
234	+		data = []byte(processTemplate(defaultConfigTemplate, "user"))
235	+	}
236	+
237	+	updated := upsertResumeSection(string(data), viewMode, lastAgent)
238	+	if err := os.WriteFile(path, []byte(updated), 0o644); err != nil {
239	+		return fmt.Errorf("writing config: %w", err)
240	+	}
241	+	return nil
242	+}
243	+
244	+// upsertResumeSection replaces the [resume] section of a TOML document with active
245	+// view_mode/last_agent keys, preserving every other line. If no [resume] section
246	+// exists it is appended. Only the [resume] block is rewritten — other sections and
247	+// their comments are untouched.
248	+func upsertResumeSection(content, viewMode, lastAgent string) string {
249	+	var block strings.Builder
250	+	block.WriteString("[resume]\n")
251	+	if viewMode != "" {
252	+		fmt.Fprintf(&block, "view_mode = %q\n", viewMode)
253	+	}
254	+	if lastAgent != "" {
255	+		fmt.Fprintf(&block, "last_agent = %q\n", lastAgent)
256	+	}
257	+
258	+	lines := strings.Split(content, "\n")
259	+	start := -1
260	+	for i, ln := range lines {
261	+		if strings.TrimSpace(ln) == "[resume]" {
262	+			start = i
263	+			break
264	+		}
265	+	}
266	+
267	+	// No existing [resume] section — append it after a blank-line separator.
268	+	if start == -1 {
269	+		out := strings.TrimRight(content, "\n")
270	+		if out != "" {
271	+			out += "\n\n"
272	+		}
273	+		return out + block.String()
274	+	}
275	+
276	+	// Replace from [resume] up to (but not including) the next top-level section.
277	+	end := len(lines)
278	+	for j := start + 1; j < len(lines); j++ {
279	+		t := strings.TrimSpace(lines[j])
280	+		if strings.HasPrefix(t, "[") && strings.HasSuffix(t, "]") {
281	+			end = j
282	+			break
283	+		}
284	+	}
285	+
286	+	var b strings.Builder
287	+	for i := 0; i < start; i++ {
288	+		b.WriteString(lines[i])
289	+		b.WriteString("\n")
290	+	}
291	+	b.WriteString(block.String())
292	+	for i := end; i < len(lines); i++ {
293	+		b.WriteString(lines[i])
294	+		if i < len(lines)-1 {
295	+			b.WriteString("\n")
296	+		}
297	+	}
298	+	return b.String()
299	+}
300	+

302	 // string if none is configured. The providerID should match a registered
303	 // provider ID (e.g., "claude", "codex", "cursor", "droid", "gemini").
304	diff --git a/specstory-cli/pkg/config/config_test.go b/specstory-cli/pkg/config/config_test.go

309	 		}
310	 	})
311	 }
312	+
313	+func TestUpsertResumeSection_AppendWhenAbsent(t *testing.T) {
314	+	in := "[local_sync]\n# enabled = false\n"
315	+	out := upsertResumeSection(in, "sparse", "codex")
316	+	if !strings.Contains(out, "[local_sync]") || !strings.Contains(out, "# enabled = false") {
317	+		t.Errorf("existing section/comments not preserved:\n%s", out)
318	+	}
319	+	if !strings.Contains(out, "[resume]") || !strings.Contains(out, `view_mode = "sparse"`) || !strings.Contains(out, `last_agent = "codex"`) {
320	+		t.Errorf("resume section not appended:\n%s", out)
321	+	}
322	+	var cfg Config
323	+	if _, err := toml.Decode(out, &cfg); err != nil {
324	+		t.Fatalf("result is not valid TOML: %v", err)
325	+	}
326	+	if cfg.GetResumeViewMode() != "sparse" || cfg.GetResumeLastAgent() != "codex" {
327	+		t.Errorf("round-trip mismatch: %+v", cfg.Resume)
328	+	}
329	+}
330	+
331	+func TestUpsertResumeSection_ReplacePreservingOthers(t *testing.T) {
332	+	in := "[resume]\nview_mode = \"dense\"\nlast_agent = \"claude\"\n\n[providers]\n# claude_cmd = \"claude\"\n"
333	+	out := upsertResumeSection(in, "sparse", "gemini")
334	+	// The other section and its comment must survive untouched.
335	+	if !strings.Contains(out, "[providers]") || !strings.Contains(out, `# claude_cmd = "claude"`) {
336	+		t.Errorf("trailing section clobbered:\n%s", out)
337	+	}
338	+	// Old resume values replaced, not duplicated.
339	+	if strings.Count(out, "[resume]") != 1 {
340	+		t.Errorf("resume section duplicated:\n%s", out)
341	+	}
342	+	var cfg Config
343	+	if _, err := toml.Decode(out, &cfg); err != nil {
344	+		t.Fatalf("invalid TOML: %v", err)
345	+	}
346	+	if cfg.GetResumeViewMode() != "sparse" || cfg.GetResumeLastAgent() != "gemini" {
347	+		t.Errorf("values not replaced: %+v", cfg.Resume)
348	+	}
349	+}
350	+
351	+func TestGetResumeViewModeDefaults(t *testing.T) {
352	+	var c Config
353	+	if c.GetResumeViewMode() != "dense" {
354	+		t.Errorf("default view mode = %q; want dense", c.GetResumeViewMode())
355	+	}
356	+	c.Resume.ViewMode = "sparse"
357	+	if c.GetResumeViewMode() != "sparse" {
358	+		t.Errorf("view mode not honored")
359	+	}
360	+}
361	diff --git a/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/global.go b/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/global.go
362	new file mode 100644
363	index 0000000..205065e
364	--- /dev/null
365	+++ b/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/global.go
366	@@ -0,0 +1,73 @@
367	+package spi
368	+
369	+import "sync/atomic"
370	+
371	+// GlobalSessionRef is a lightweight, project-discovering reference to a single
372	+// native session, returned by Provider.ListAllAgentChatSessions.
373	+//
374	+// Unlike the project-scoped ListAgentChatSessions(projectPath), the project is NOT
375	+// an input here: each ref carries the originating working directory (read from inside
376	+// the session) so the caller — the `specstory reindex` command — can resolve project
377	+// identity with utils.ComputeProjectID. The project is discovered, not supplied.
378	+//
379	+// It is intentionally metadata-only (no full SessionData parse); reindex re-fetches
380	+// full data per ref via GetAgentChatSession(OriginCwd, SessionID) when it needs the
381	+// conversation body. See docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
382	+type GlobalSessionRef struct {
383	+	SessionID  string // native session id (uuid)
384	+	CreatedAt  string // ISO 8601 creation timestamp (first turn), may be empty
385	+	Slug       string // filename-safe slug derived from the first user message
386	+	Name       string // human-readable description (may be empty)
387	+	NativePath string // absolute path the provider opens to read this session
388	+	OriginCwd  string // working directory the session was launched from (-> project_id)
389	+}
390	+
391	+// PathSessionReader is an OPTIONAL capability a Provider may implement: parse a single
392	+// session directly from its already-known native file path, skipping the by-id discovery
393	+// search that GetAgentChatSession performs.
394	+//
395	+// Why: some providers locate a session by scanning their native store. Codex's by-id
396	+// lookup walks the entire ~/.codex/sessions tree on every call, so resolving N sessions
397	+// that way is O(N²). reindex already holds each session's exact file path
398	+// (GlobalSessionRef.NativePath) from enumeration, so a path-keyed parse turns that back
399	+// into O(N). reindex prefers this when a provider implements it and the ref carries a
400	+// NativePath, and falls back to GetAgentChatSession otherwise.
401	+//
402	+// originCwd is the session's originating working directory (GlobalSessionRef.OriginCwd),
403	+// passed through as the workspace root for path normalization — matching what
404	+// GetAgentChatSession receives as projectPath.
405	+type PathSessionReader interface {
406	+	GetAgentChatSessionByPath(nativePath string, originCwd string, debugRaw bool) (*AgentChatSession, error)
407	+}
408	+
409	+// ScanReporter accumulates a provider's enumeration progress (sessions found) so the CLI can
410	+// render a live "scanning" display. It is safe for concurrent use and safe to call on a nil
411	+// receiver (a no-op), so providers can report unconditionally.
412	+type ScanReporter struct {
413	+	found atomic.Int64
414	+}
415	+
416	+// Add records that n more sessions have been found. Files that yield no session (warmup-only or
417	+// sidechain-only transcripts) are deliberately not counted, so the running total reflects real
418	+// sessions rather than raw files.
419	+func (r *ScanReporter) Add(n int) {
420	+	if r != nil {
421	+		r.found.Add(int64(n))
422	+	}
423	+}
424	+
425	+// Found returns the number of sessions found so far.
426	+func (r *ScanReporter) Found() int64 {
427	+	if r == nil {
428	+		return 0
429	+	}
430	+	return r.found.Load()
431	+}
432	+
433	+// ProgressEnumerator is an OPTIONAL Provider capability: enumerate all sessions while reporting
434	+// scan progress into r (which may be nil — ScanReporter is nil-safe, so report unconditionally).
435	+// Providers that don't implement it are enumerated via ListAllAgentChatSessions with no live
436	+// feedback. reindex uses this to render a per-agent "Scanning agents…" display.
437	+type ProgressEnumerator interface {
438	+	ListAllAgentChatSessionsProgress(r *ScanReporter) ([]GlobalSessionRef, error)
439	+}
440	diff --git a/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/provider.go b/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/provider.go
441	index 4386a9b..3b1e267 100644
442	--- a/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/provider.go
443	+++ b/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/provider.go
444	@@ -95,4 +95,31 @@ type Provider interface {
445	 	// sessionCallback: called with AgentChatSession on each update (provider should not block on callback)
446	 	// The implementation should handle its own file watching and session tracking
447	 	WatchAgent(ctx context.Context, projectPath string, debugRaw bool, sessionCallback func(*AgentChatSession)) error
448	+
449	+	// ReconstructSession rebuilds the provider's native session format from the
450	+	// neutral SessionData so the agent can resume the conversation (the reverse of
451	+	// the parse/generate path). It is a pure transform: it returns the native file
452	+	// bytes, a freshly minted native-format session ID, and a suggested filename,
453	+	// but does NOT touch the filesystem — writing into the live agent store is the
454	+	// caller's responsibility.
455	+	//
456	+	// Every provider carries this responsibility; providers that do not yet have a
457	+	// native serializer return ErrReconstructionUnsupported.
458	+	// See docs/SESSION-PORTABILITY.md for the design.
459	+	ReconstructSession(data *schema.SessionData, opts ReconstructOptions) (*ReconstructedSession, error)
460	+
461	+	// NativeSessionPath resolves the absolute path where a reconstructed session
462	+	// file (with the given base filename, from ReconstructedSession.Filename)
463	+	// belongs in this provider's native store for the given project, WITHOUT
464	+	// requiring the directory to exist — the caller creates it and writes the file.
465	+	// Providers without a native serializer return ErrReconstructionUnsupported.
466	+	NativeSessionPath(projectPath string, filename string) (string, error)
467	+
468	+	// ListAllAgentChatSessions enumerates every session in this provider's native
469	+	// store, regardless of project — the inverse of the project-scoped
470	+	// ListAgentChatSessions. Each returned ref carries the originating cwd (read from
471	+	// inside the session) so the caller can resolve project identity; the project is
472	+	// discovered, not supplied. Lightweight: metadata only, no full SessionData parse.
473	+	// Used by `specstory reindex` to (re)build the restore index. See docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
474	+	ListAllAgentChatSessions() ([]GlobalSessionRef, error)
475	 }
476	diff --git a/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/reconstruct.go b/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/reconstruct.go
477	new file mode 100644
478	index 0000000..20f4899
479	--- /dev/null
480	+++ b/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/reconstruct.go
481	@@ -0,0 +1,177 @@
482	+package spi
483	+
484	+import (
485	+	"errors"
486	+	"strings"
487	+
488	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
489	+)
490	+
491	+// ErrReconstructionUnsupported is returned by a provider that carries the
492	+// ReconstructSession responsibility but does not yet have a native serializer.
493	+// Reconstruction is on the Provider interface so every provider implements the
494	+// method, but only some providers (initially Claude Code and Codex CLI) produce
495	+// real native output.
496	+var ErrReconstructionUnsupported = errors.New("session reconstruction not supported by this provider")
497	+
498	+// ReconstructOptions controls how a session is reconstructed into native form.
499	+type ReconstructOptions struct {
500	+	// WorkspaceRoot is the target working directory the reconstructed session
501	+	// should belong to. Native paths/IDs are derived from it. When empty, the
502	+	// provider falls back to the SessionData's own WorkspaceRoot.
503	+	WorkspaceRoot string
504	+
505	+	// MigrationNote, when set, is prepended as a leading agent turn so the agent
506	+	// understands the prior turns were imported from another session. Optional.
507	+	MigrationNote string
508	+}
509	+
510	+// ReconstructedSession is the native output of reconstruction. It is data only;
511	+// the caller decides where (and whether) to write it.
512	+type ReconstructedSession struct {
513	+	SessionID string // freshly minted, native-format session ID
514	+	Filename  string // suggested native filename (base name, not a full path)
515	+	Content   []byte // native session file bytes
516	+}
517	+
518	+// Turn is a single role-tagged text turn in the flattened transcript. The
519	+// reconstruction model collapses everything an agent said, thought, or did into
520	+// plain user/agent text turns (see docs/SESSION-PORTABILITY.md), which each
521	+// provider then serializes into its native format.
522	+type Turn struct {
523	+	Role string // schema.RoleUser or schema.RoleAgent
524	+	Text string
525	+}
526	+
527	+// FlattenSessionData reduces a SessionData into an ordered list of plain
528	+// user/agent text turns. This is the single place the cross-provider flattening
529	+// policy lives, so every provider's serializer consumes the same transcript.
530	+//
531	+// Rules (per the locked design):
532	+//   - user/agent text content -> a turn of that role
533	+//   - thinking content -> folded into the agent turn's text (not dropped)
534	+//   - tool calls -> folded into the agent turn's text via the pre-rendered
535	+//     Summary / FormattedMarkdown captured on the forward pass
536	+//   - model, usage, and path hints -> dropped
537	+//
538	+// migrationNote, when non-empty, becomes a leading agent turn.
539	+func FlattenSessionData(data *schema.SessionData, migrationNote string) []Turn {
540	+	var turns []Turn
541	+
542	+	if strings.TrimSpace(migrationNote) != "" {
543	+		turns = append(turns, Turn{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Text: strings.TrimSpace(migrationNote)})
544	+	}
545	+
546	+	if data == nil {
547	+		return turns
548	+	}
549	+
550	+	for i := range data.Exchanges {
551	+		for j := range data.Exchanges[i].Messages {
552	+			msg := &data.Exchanges[i].Messages[j]
553	+
554	+			// Only user and agent roles produce turns; anything else is skipped.
555	+			if msg.Role != schema.RoleUser && msg.Role != schema.RoleAgent {
556	+				continue
557	+			}
558	+
559	+			text := messageTurnText(msg)
560	+			if strings.TrimSpace(text) == "" {
561	+				continue
562	+			}
563	+
564	+			// Drop synthetic, non-conversational turns (slash-command invocations,
565	+			// local-command output, etc.) so they are not replayed into the resumed
566	+			// session. This is intentionally reconstruction-only — archival markdown
567	+			// stays faithful to the source session.
568	+			if isSyntheticTurnText(text) {
569	+				continue
570	+			}
571	+
572	+			turns = append(turns, Turn{Role: msg.Role, Text: text})
573	+		}
574	+	}
575	+
576	+	return turns
577	+}
578	+
579	+// syntheticTurnMarkers are wrapper tags agents inject for non-conversational
580	+// records — slash-command invocations, their stdout, and guard/caveat notes (e.g.
581	+// Claude Code's <command-name>/<local-command-*> and <TEXTBLOCK> title-generation
582	+// prompts). These are noise — and sometimes actively misleading — when replayed
583	+// into a resumed session, so reconstruction drops any turn whose text contains
584	+// one. Markdown generation does NOT use this filter and stays faithful to source.
585	+var syntheticTurnMarkers = []string{
586	+	"<command-name>",
587	+	"<command-message>",
588	+	"<command-args>",
589	+	"<local-command-stdout>",
590	+	"<local-command-caveat>",
591	+	"<TEXTBLOCK>",
592	+}
593	+
594	+// isSyntheticTurnText reports whether a flattened turn is a synthetic,
595	+// non-conversational artifact that should not be replayed into a resumed session.
596	+func isSyntheticTurnText(text string) bool {
597	+	for _, marker := range syntheticTurnMarkers {
598	+		if strings.Contains(text, marker) {
599	+			return true
600	+		}
601	+	}
602	+	return false
603	+}
604	+
605	+// messageTurnText flattens a single message into its turn text: all text/thinking
606	+// content parts joined, followed by the tool's pre-rendered markdown when present.
607	+func messageTurnText(msg *schema.Message) string {
608	+	var parts []string
609	+
610	+	for _, part := range msg.Content {
611	+		// Both "text" and "thinking" parts carry their content in Text; thinking
612	+		// is intentionally treated as ordinary agent text.
613	+		if strings.TrimSpace(part.Text) != "" {
614	+			parts = append(parts, part.Text)
615	+		}
616	+	}
617	+
618	+	text := strings.Join(parts, "\n\n")
619	+
620	+	if msg.Tool != nil {
621	+		toolText := toolTurnText(msg.Tool)
622	+		if toolText != "" {
623	+			if text != "" {
624	+				text += "\n\n" + toolText
625	+			} else {
626	+				text = toolText
627	+			}
628	+		}
629	+	}
630	+
631	+	return text
632	+}
633	+
634	+// toolTurnText renders a tool call as agent text, reusing the markdown the
635	+// forward pass already produced (Summary + FormattedMarkdown). Falls back to a
636	+// minimal description when neither is available.
637	+func toolTurnText(tool *schema.ToolInfo) string {
638	+	var b strings.Builder
639	+
640	+	if tool.Summary != nil && strings.TrimSpace(*tool.Summary) != "" {
641	+		b.WriteString(strings.TrimSpace(*tool.Summary))
642	+	}
643	+
644	+	if tool.FormattedMarkdown != nil && strings.TrimSpace(*tool.FormattedMarkdown) != "" {
645	+		if b.Len() > 0 {
646	+			b.WriteString("\n\n")
647	+		}
648	+		b.WriteString(strings.TrimRight(*tool.FormattedMarkdown, "\n"))
649	+	}
650	+
651	+	if b.Len() == 0 {
652	+		// Nothing pre-rendered; emit a minimal, non-empty description so the tool
653	+		// activity is at least acknowledged in the transcript.
654	+		b.WriteString("Tool use: " + tool.Name)
655	+	}
656	+
657	+	return b.String()
658	+}

664	@@ -0,0 +1,169 @@
665	+package spi
666	+

668	+	"testing"

672	+
673	+func strptr(s string) *string { return &s }
674	+
675	+func TestFlattenSessionData(t *testing.T) {
676	+	summary := "Tool use: **shell** `ls`"
677	+	formatted := "```\nfile.txt\n```"
678	+	onlyFormatted := "Wrote `hello.c`"
679	+
680	+	data := &schema.SessionData{
681	+		Exchanges: []schema.Exchange{
682	+			{
683	+				Messages: []schema.Message{
684	+					// User text.
685	+					{Role: schema.RoleUser, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "do a thing"}}},
686	+					// Agent text; model/usage must be ignored by flattening.
687	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Model: "gpt-5-codex", Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "on it"}}, Usage: &schema.Usage{InputTokens: 10}},
688	+					// Thinking folds into agent text.
689	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeThinking, Text: "let me reason"}}},
690	+					// Tool with both summary and formatted markdown.
691	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Tool: &schema.ToolInfo{Name: "shell", Type: schema.ToolTypeShell, Summary: strptr(summary), FormattedMarkdown: strptr(formatted)}, PathHints: []string{"x"}},
692	+					// Tool with only formatted markdown.
693	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Tool: &schema.ToolInfo{Name: "Write", Type: schema.ToolTypeWrite, FormattedMarkdown: strptr(onlyFormatted)}},
694	+					// Empty agent message (no content/tool) is skipped.
695	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, PathHints: []string{"y"}},
696	+				},
697	+			},
698	+		},
699	+	}
700	+
701	+	got := FlattenSessionData(data, "")
702	+
703	+	want := []Turn{
704	+		{Role: schema.RoleUser, Text: "do a thing"},
705	+		{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Text: "on it"},
706	+		{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Text: "let me reason"},
707	+		{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Text: summary + "\n\n" + formatted},
708	+		{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Text: onlyFormatted},
709	+	}
710	+
711	+	if len(got) != len(want) {
712	+		t.Fatalf("got %d turns, want %d: %+v", len(got), len(want), got)
713	+	}
714	+	for i := range want {
715	+		if got[i] != want[i] {
716	+			t.Errorf("turn %d: got %+v, want %+v", i, got[i], want[i])
717	+		}
718	+	}
719	+}
720	+

726	+			}},
727	+		},
728	+	}
729	+

731	+	if len(got) != 2 {

733	+	}

760	+func TestFlattenSessionData_DropsSyntheticTurns(t *testing.T) {
761	+	data := &schema.SessionData{
762	+		Exchanges: []schema.Exchange{
763	+			{Messages: []schema.Message{
764	+				{Role: schema.RoleUser, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "real prompt"}}},
765	+				// Claude Code slash-command artifacts captured as user messages.
766	+				{Role: schema.RoleUser, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "<command-name>/exit</command-name>\n<command-message>exit</command-message>\n<command-args></command-args>"}}},
767	+				{Role: schema.RoleUser, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "<local-command-stdout>Catch you later!</local-command-stdout>"}}},
768	+				{Role: schema.RoleUser, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "<local-command-caveat>Caveat: ...DO NOT respond...</local-command-caveat>"}}},
769	+				{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "real answer"}}},
770	+			}},
771	+		},
772	+	}
773	+
774	+	got := FlattenSessionData(data, "")
775	+	want := []Turn{
776	+		{Role: schema.RoleUser, Text: "real prompt"},
777	+		{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Text: "real answer"},
778	+	}
779	+	if len(got) != len(want) {
780	+		t.Fatalf("got %d turns, want %d: %+v", len(got), len(want), got)
781	+	}
782	+	for i := range want {
783	+		if got[i] != want[i] {
784	+			t.Errorf("turn %d: got %+v, want %+v", i, got[i], want[i])
785	+		}
786	+	}
787	+}
788	+
789	+func TestFlattenSessionData_Nil(t *testing.T) {

796	+	data := &schema.SessionData{

800	+				{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: ""}}},
801	+				{Role: schema.RoleUser, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "real"}}},

811	+func TestFlattenSessionData_ToolOnlySession(t *testing.T) {
812	+	// A session whose agent turns are entirely tool calls still produces agent text.

820	+			}},
821	+		},

825	+	if len(got) != len(want) {

827	+	}
828	+	for i := range want {

831	+		}
832	+	}
833	+}

924	+
925	+// caseInsensitiveFilesystem reports whether the host OS treats paths case-insensitively.
926	+// macOS (APFS/HFS+) and Windows (NTFS) are case-insensitive/case-preserving; Linux is
927	+// case-sensitive. It is a var so tests can exercise both behaviors. Windows is included
928	+// even though the app is built only for macOS/Linux today: the in-flight Windows support
929	+// lives on a branch that does not carry this file, so folding case here keeps that branch
930	+// from silently reintroducing case-divergent workspace ids when it merges.
931	+var caseInsensitiveFilesystem = runtime.GOOS == "darwin" || runtime.GOOS == "windows"
932	+
933	+// canonicalizeWorkspacePath normalizes a path before it is hashed into a workspace_id. On
934	+// a case-insensitive filesystem the same directory can be named with different casing,
935	+// which would otherwise hash to different ids for one physical directory; case-folding
936	+// makes the id stable. On case-sensitive filesystems the path is left byte-exact, since
937	+// there two differently-cased paths are genuinely different directories.
938	+func canonicalizeWorkspacePath(path string) string {
939	+	if caseInsensitiveFilesystem {
940	+		return strings.ToLower(path)
941	+	}
942	+	return path
943	 }

998	 	}

1000	 }
1001	+
1002	+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
1003	+// Walk-up identity resolution (shared by ComputeProjectID and the writer).
1004	+//
1005	+// The stored .specstory/.project.json fragments a monorepo: it is written per
1006	+// launch directory, and generateGitID looks for .git at exactly that directory and
1007	+// never walks up — so a session launched from a subdirectory gets no git_id and a
1008	+// path-based workspace_id all its own. The resolver below instead walks UP to the
1009	+// git root and computes identity from there, so every directory inside one repo
1010	+// resolves to a single project. See docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
1011	+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
1012	+
1013	+// originRemoteURLRegex captures the origin remote's url value from a git config file.
1014	+var originRemoteURLRegex = regexp.MustCompile(`\[remote "origin"\][^\[]*url\s*=\s*([^\n\r]+)`)
1015	+
1016	+// readOriginURL returns the origin remote URL from a git config file, or "" if the
1017	+// file is unreadable or has no origin remote.
1018	+func readOriginURL(gitConfigPath string) string {
1019	+	gitConfig, err := os.ReadFile(gitConfigPath)
1020	+	if err != nil {
1021	+		return ""
1022	+	}
1023	+	matches := originRemoteURLRegex.FindSubmatch(gitConfig)
1024	+	if len(matches) < 2 {
1025	+		return ""
1026	+	}
1027	+	return strings.TrimSpace(string(matches[1]))
1028	+}
1029	+
1030	+// findGitRoot walks up from dir to the nearest ancestor containing a .git entry and
1031	+// returns it. The .git entry may be a directory (normal clone) or a FILE (git
1032	+// worktree / submodule); both satisfy the search. Returns false if no .git is found
1033	+// before the filesystem root. The walk-up is what lets a session launched from a
1034	+// monorepo subdirectory resolve to the repo's identity.
1035	+func findGitRoot(dir string) (string, bool) {
1036	+	if abs, err := filepath.Abs(dir); err == nil {
1037	+		dir = abs
1038	+	}
1039	+	dir = filepath.Clean(dir)
1040	+	for {
1041	+		if _, err := os.Stat(filepath.Join(dir, ".git")); err == nil {
1042	+			return dir, true
1043	+		}
1044	+		parent := filepath.Dir(dir)
1045	+		if parent == dir {
1046	+			return "", false // reached the filesystem root without finding .git
1047	+		}
1048	+		dir = parent
1049	+	}
1050	+}
1051	+
1052	+// resolveGitConfigPath returns the path to the git config file that holds a root's
1053	+// remotes, handling both a normal `.git` directory and a `.git` FILE (worktrees /
1054	+// submodules, whose private gitdir points at a commondir that holds the shared
1055	+// config). Returns "" when it cannot be resolved.
1056	+func resolveGitConfigPath(gitRoot string) string {
1057	+	gitPath := filepath.Join(gitRoot, ".git")
1058	+	info, err := os.Stat(gitPath)
1059	+	if err != nil {
1060	+		return ""
1061	+	}
1062	+	if info.IsDir() {
1063	+		return filepath.Join(gitPath, "config")
1064	+	}
1065	+
1066	+	// `.git` is a file of the form "gitdir: <path>" pointing at the worktree's private
1067	+	// git dir; the shared config lives in the common dir, not that private dir.
1068	+	data, err := os.ReadFile(gitPath)
1069	+	if err != nil {
1070	+		return ""
1071	+	}
1072	+	content := strings.TrimSpace(string(data))
1073	+	if !strings.HasPrefix(content, "gitdir:") {
1074	+		return ""
1075	+	}
1076	+	gitDir := strings.TrimSpace(strings.TrimPrefix(content, "gitdir:"))
1077	+	if gitDir == "" {
1078	+		return ""
1079	+	}
1080	+	if !filepath.IsAbs(gitDir) {
1081	+		gitDir = filepath.Join(gitRoot, gitDir)
1082	+	}
1083	+	commonDir := gitDir
1084	+	if cd, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(gitDir, "commondir")); err == nil {
1085	+		c := strings.TrimSpace(string(cd))
1086	+		if !filepath.IsAbs(c) {
1087	+			c = filepath.Join(gitDir, c)
1088	+		}
1089	+		commonDir = filepath.Clean(c)
1090	+	}
1091	+	return filepath.Join(commonDir, "config")
1092	+}
1093	+
1094	+// resolveIdentity computes a project's identity from a starting directory by walking
1095	+// up to the git root. It reads only git metadata — never any .specstory/.project.json
1096	+// — so it cannot be fooled by the per-directory identity files that fragment a
1097	+// monorepo. Returns the git_id (empty when there is no resolvable origin remote), the
1098	+// workspace_id (hash of the resolved root), the project name, and the resolved root.
1099	+func resolveIdentity(startDir string) (gitID, workspaceID, name, root string) {
1100	+	root = startDir
1101	+	if gr, ok := findGitRoot(startDir); ok {
1102	+		root = gr
1103	+	}
1104	+
1105	+	// A zero-state manager rooted at the resolved root reuses the existing hashing /
1106	+	// normalization / repo-name helpers (they do not depend on manager state).
1107	+	rooted := &ProjectIdentityManager{projectRoot: root}
1108	+	workspaceID = rooted.generateWorkspaceID()
1109	+	if cfg := resolveGitConfigPath(root); cfg != "" {
1110	+		if originURL := readOriginURL(cfg); originURL != "" {
1111	+			gitID = rooted.createHash(rooted.normalizeGitURL(originURL))
1112	+			name = rooted.parseGitRemoteURL(originURL)
1113	+		}
1114	+	}
1115	+	if name == "" {
1116	+		name = filepath.Base(root)
1117	+	}
1118	+	return gitID, workspaceID, name, root
1119	+}
1120	+
1121	+// ComputeProjectID resolves the restore-index project identity for a session's
1122	+// working directory. It walks up to the git root and computes identity fresh,
1123	+// ignoring any stored .specstory/.project.json (which fragments a monorepo) and
1124	+// never writing one. Returns the project id (git_id when a remote is resolvable,
1125	+// else the path-based workspace_id) and the project name.
1126	+func ComputeProjectID(cwd string) (id, name string, err error) {
1127	+	if strings.TrimSpace(cwd) == "" {
1128	+		return "", "", fmt.Errorf("cannot resolve project id: empty working directory")
1129	+	}
1130	+	gitID, workspaceID, name, _ := resolveIdentity(cwd)
1131	+
1132	+	// Prefer a resolvable git_id: it hashes the normalized remote URL, not a path, so it is
1133	+	// immune to the case divergence handled below and never fragments a monorepo.
1134	+	if gitID != "" {
1135	+		return gitID, name, nil
1136	+	}
1137	+
1138	+	// No git remote → use the path-based workspace_id. Prefer an id already persisted in the
1139	+	// launch dir's .project.json so every consumer (reindex, resume, cloud sync) agrees on a
1140	+	// single value, even for projects whose file predates path canonicalization. Only when no
1141	+	// file exists (e.g. a portable session whose recorded cwd is not present locally) do we
1142	+	// fall back to the freshly computed — and now canonical — hash.
1143	+	if stored := persistedWorkspaceID(cwd); stored != "" {
1144	+		return stored, name, nil
1145	+	}
1146	+	if workspaceID == "" {
1147	+		return "", "", fmt.Errorf("cannot resolve project id for %q", cwd)
1148	+	}
1149	+	return workspaceID, name, nil
1150	+}
1151	+
1152	+// persistedWorkspaceID returns the workspace_id recorded in dir's .specstory/.project.json,
1153	+// or "" when there is no readable file or it carries no workspace_id. The file is read from
1154	+// the launch directory (where the writer puts it), not the resolved git root.
1155	+func persistedWorkspaceID(dir string) string {
1156	+	m := &ProjectIdentityManager{projectRoot: dir}
1157	+	identity, err := m.ReadProjectIdentity()
1158	+	if err != nil || identity == nil {
1159	+		return ""
1160	+	}
1161	+	return identity.WorkspaceID
1162	+}

1	diff --git a/specstory-cli/pkg/sessionindex/store.go b/specstory-cli/pkg/sessionindex/store.go
2	new file mode 100644
3	index 0000000..95dfff6
4	--- /dev/null
5	+++ b/specstory-cli/pkg/sessionindex/store.go
6	@@ -0,0 +1,526 @@
7	+// Package sessionindex implements sessions.db — the machine-level index of every coding-agent
8	+// session SpecStory knows about, across all projects and providers. It backs the
9	+// `specstory resume` selection UX and is (re)built by `specstory reindex`.
10	+//
11	+// sessions.db is a DERIVED CACHE over the native session stores: it can be deleted and
12	+// fully rebuilt at any time. See docs/SESSIONS-DB.md for the design and schema.
13	+package sessionindex
14	+
15	+import (
16	+	"context"
17	+	"database/sql"
18	+	"fmt"
19	+	"os"
20	+	"path/filepath"
21	+	"sort"
22	+	"strings"
23	+
24	+	_ "modernc.org/sqlite" // SQLite driver (pure Go), same as pkg/provenance
25	+)
26	+
27	+// Connection-pool sizing. Writes (reindex) must serialize on a single connection to
28	+// avoid SQLITE_BUSY/deadlocks. The interactive browse path (resume/search) only reads,
29	+// so it opens several connections — WAL permits many concurrent readers, which keeps a
30	+// single slow full-text query (a broad prefix can take seconds) from starving the UI.
31	+const (
32	+	writerConns = 1
33	+	readerConns = 4
34	+)
35	+
36	+// Session is one row of the restore index (the `sessions` table), plus Body, which is
37	+// indexed into the FTS table rather than stored on the row. See docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
38	+type Session struct {
39	+	ProjectID    string // resolved git_id (else workspace_id)
40	+	ProjectName  string
41	+	Agent        string // provider id: claude, codex, gemini, droid, deepseek, cursor
42	+	SessionID    string // native session id (uuid)
43	+	CreatedAt    string // ISO 8601, first turn
44	+	UpdatedAt    string // ISO 8601, last activity (last turn, else file mtime)
45	+	UserTurns    int    // count of user prompts
46	+	TotalTurns   int    // count of all messages
47	+	Slug         string
48	+	Name         string
49	+	NativePath   string // absolute path the provider opens to read this session
50	+	OriginCwd    string // working directory the session was launched from
51	+	Size         int64  // native file size, bytes — part of the freshness fingerprint
52	+	Mtime        int64  // native file mtime, epoch ms — part of the freshness fingerprint
53	+	IndexVersion int    // reindex logic version that wrote this row — part of the fingerprint
54	+	IndexedAt    string // ISO 8601, when reindex last wrote this row
55	+
56	+	// Body is the full conversation text, indexed into sessions_fts (not persisted on
57	+	// the sessions row). Left empty on rows read back out of the index.
58	+	Body string
59	+
60	+	// IsNew is a write-time hint, not a persisted column: when true, the caller has
61	+	// determined this (agent, session_id) has no existing index row, so upsertTx can skip
62	+	// the delete-before-insert on the standalone FTS table (which otherwise scans the whole
63	+	// FTS, since session_id/agent are UNINDEXED). Leave false when unsure — the delete is
64	+	// then kept, which is always correct.
65	+	IsNew bool
66	+}
67	+
68	+// Fingerprint identifies an indexed session's freshness: the native file's size and
69	+// mtime, plus the reindex logic version that produced the row. reindex skips a session
70	+// whose fingerprint is unchanged. See docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
71	+type Fingerprint struct {
72	+	Size    int64
73	+	Mtime   int64
74	+	Version int
75	+}
76	+
77	+// Store is a handle to sessions.db.
78	+type Store struct {
79	+	db *sql.DB
80	+}
81	+
82	+// DefaultPath returns ~/.specstory/sessions.db.
83	+func DefaultPath() (string, error) {
84	+	home, err := os.UserHomeDir()
85	+	if err != nil {
86	+		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to resolve home directory: %w", err)
87	+	}
88	+	return filepath.Join(home, ".specstory", "sessions.db"), nil
89	+}
90	+
91	+// Open opens (or creates) sessions.db for writing — a single serialized connection,
92	+// applying WAL + performance pragmas (matching pkg/provenance) and ensuring the schema
93	+// exists. Use OpenReader for the read-only interactive browse path.
94	+func Open(path string) (*Store, error) {
95	+	return openWith(path, writerConns)
96	+}
97	+
98	+// OpenReader opens sessions.db for the interactive browse path (resume/search), which
99	+// only reads. It allows several concurrent connections so one slow full-text query can't
100	+// starve the UI (WAL permits many simultaneous readers). Never write through this handle.
101	+func OpenReader(path string) (*Store, error) {
102	+	return openWith(path, readerConns)
103	+}
104	+
105	+func openWith(path string, maxConns int) (*Store, error) {
106	+	if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path), 0o755); err != nil {
107	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("creating database directory: %w", err)
108	+	}
109	+
110	+	dsn := fmt.Sprintf("file:%s?_pragma=busy_timeout=15000&_pragma=journal_mode(WAL)", filepath.ToSlash(path))
111	+	db, err := sql.Open("sqlite", dsn)
112	+	if err != nil {
113	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("opening sessions.db: %w", err)
114	+	}
115	+
116	+	db.SetMaxOpenConns(maxConns)
117	+	db.SetMaxIdleConns(maxConns)
118	+
119	+	for _, p := range []string{
120	+		"PRAGMA synchronous=NORMAL",
121	+		"PRAGMA cache_size = -64000",
122	+		"PRAGMA temp_store = MEMORY",
123	+		"PRAGMA mmap_size = 268435456",
124	+		"PRAGMA page_size = 8192",
125	+	} {
126	+		if _, err := db.Exec(p); err != nil {
127	+			_ = db.Close()
128	+			return nil, fmt.Errorf("setting pragma %q: %w", p, err)
129	+		}
130	+	}
131	+
132	+	s := &Store{db: db}
133	+	if err := s.ensureSchema(); err != nil {
134	+		_ = db.Close()
135	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("ensuring schema: %w", err)
136	+	}
137	+	return s, nil
138	+}
139	+
140	+// Close closes the database connection.
141	+func (s *Store) Close() error {
142	+	return s.db.Close()
143	+}
144	+
145	+// sessionColumns is the canonical sessions column list, shared by every SELECT so the
146	+// scan order in scanSession stays in lockstep with it.
147	+const sessionColumns = `project_id, project_name, agent, session_id, created_at, updated_at,
148	+	user_turns, total_turns, slug, name, native_path, origin_cwd, size, mtime, index_version, indexed_at`
149	+
150	+func (s *Store) ensureSchema() error {
151	+	schema := `
152	+	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sessions (
153	+		project_id   TEXT NOT NULL,
154	+		project_name TEXT,
155	+		agent        TEXT NOT NULL,
156	+		session_id   TEXT NOT NULL,
157	+		created_at   TEXT,
158	+		updated_at   TEXT,
159	+		user_turns   INTEGER,
160	+		total_turns  INTEGER,
161	+		slug         TEXT,
162	+		name         TEXT,
163	+		native_path  TEXT,
164	+		origin_cwd   TEXT,
165	+		size          INTEGER,
166	+		mtime         INTEGER,
167	+		index_version INTEGER,
168	+		indexed_at    TEXT,
169	+		PRIMARY KEY (agent, session_id)
170	+	);
171	+	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_sessions_project ON sessions(project_id);
172	+
173	+	-- Standalone FTS5 index over the conversation body + name. session_id/agent ride
174	+	-- along UNINDEXED as join keys back to the sessions row. See docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
175	+	CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sessions_fts USING fts5(
176	+		session_id UNINDEXED,
177	+		agent UNINDEXED,
178	+		name,
179	+		body
180	+	);
181	+	`
182	+	if _, err := s.db.Exec(schema); err != nil {
183	+		return err
184	+	}
185	+	// Migration for indexes created before index_version existed (the column is part of
186	+	// the freshness fingerprint). Ignore the error when it already exists.
187	+	_, _ = s.db.Exec(`ALTER TABLE sessions ADD COLUMN index_version INTEGER DEFAULT 0`)
188	+	return nil
189	+}
190	+
191	+// Fingerprints returns the freshness fingerprint of every indexed session, keyed by
192	+// fingerprintKey(agent, session_id). reindex loads this once and skips any session
193	+// whose native file is unchanged (same size + mtime) and was indexed by the current
194	+// logic version.
195	+func (s *Store) Fingerprints() (map[string]Fingerprint, error) {
196	+	rows, err := s.db.Query(`SELECT agent, session_id, size, mtime, index_version FROM sessions`)
197	+	if err != nil {
198	+		return nil, err
199	+	}
200	+	defer func() { _ = rows.Close() }()
201	+
202	+	out := make(map[string]Fingerprint)
203	+	for rows.Next() {
204	+		var agent, sessionID string
205	+		var fp Fingerprint
206	+		if err := rows.Scan(&agent, &sessionID, &fp.Size, &fp.Mtime, &fp.Version); err != nil {
207	+			return nil, err
208	+		}
209	+		out[FingerprintKey(agent, sessionID)] = fp
210	+	}
211	+	return out, rows.Err()
212	+}
213	+
214	+// FingerprintKey is the map key for a session's fingerprint: agent + NUL + session_id.
215	+func FingerprintKey(agent, sessionID string) string {
216	+	return agent + "\x00" + sessionID
217	+}
218	+
219	+// sessionUpsertStmts are the per-row statements UpsertBatch prepares once and reuses across
220	+// every row in a transaction, so the SQL is parsed once per batch instead of once per row.
221	+type sessionUpsertStmts struct {
222	+	insSession *sql.Stmt
223	+	delFTS     *sql.Stmt
224	+	insFTS     *sql.Stmt
225	+}
226	+
227	+// upsertOne writes one session's row and full-text row using the batch's prepared statements.
228	+// FTS5 standalone tables are not auto-synced, so the FTS row is replaced by hand — except for
229	+// brand-new sessions (sess.IsNew), where the delete is skipped: it can't use an index
230	+// (session_id/agent are UNINDEXED) so it scans the whole FTS, and a new session has no row to
231	+// delete anyway. At 25k+ sessions that skip is what keeps a from-scratch build out of O(N²).
232	+func upsertOne(st sessionUpsertStmts, sess Session) error {
233	+	if _, err := st.insSession.Exec(
234	+		sess.ProjectID, sess.ProjectName, sess.Agent, sess.SessionID, sess.CreatedAt, sess.UpdatedAt,
235	+		sess.UserTurns, sess.TotalTurns, sess.Slug, sess.Name, sess.NativePath, sess.OriginCwd,
236	+		sess.Size, sess.Mtime, sess.IndexVersion, sess.IndexedAt); err != nil {
237	+		return fmt.Errorf("upsert session row: %w", err)
238	+	}
239	+	if !sess.IsNew {
240	+		if _, err := st.delFTS.Exec(sess.Agent, sess.SessionID); err != nil {
241	+			return fmt.Errorf("clear fts row: %w", err)
242	+		}
243	+	}
244	+	if _, err := st.insFTS.Exec(sess.SessionID, sess.Agent, sess.Name, sess.Body); err != nil {
245	+		return fmt.Errorf("insert fts row: %w", err)
246	+	}
247	+	return nil
248	+}
249	+
250	+// prepareUpsert prepares the three per-row statements on tx and returns them with a closer.
251	+func prepareUpsert(tx *sql.Tx) (sessionUpsertStmts, func(), error) {
252	+	insSession, err := tx.Prepare(`INSERT OR REPLACE INTO sessions (` + sessionColumns + `)
253	+		VALUES (?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?)`)
254	+	if err != nil {
255	+		return sessionUpsertStmts{}, func() {}, fmt.Errorf("prepare session upsert: %w", err)
256	+	}
257	+	delFTS, err := tx.Prepare(`DELETE FROM sessions_fts WHERE agent = ? AND session_id = ?`)
258	+	if err != nil {
259	+		_ = insSession.Close()
260	+		return sessionUpsertStmts{}, func() {}, fmt.Errorf("prepare fts delete: %w", err)
261	+	}
262	+	insFTS, err := tx.Prepare(`INSERT INTO sessions_fts (session_id, agent, name, body) VALUES (?,?,?,?)`)
263	+	if err != nil {
264	+		_ = insSession.Close()
265	+		_ = delFTS.Close()
266	+		return sessionUpsertStmts{}, func() {}, fmt.Errorf("prepare fts insert: %w", err)
267	+	}
268	+	closer := func() {
269	+		_ = insSession.Close()
270	+		_ = delFTS.Close()
271	+		_ = insFTS.Close()
272	+	}
273	+	return sessionUpsertStmts{insSession: insSession, delFTS: delFTS, insFTS: insFTS}, closer, nil
274	+}
275	+
276	+// Upsert inserts or replaces a single session row and its full-text row, atomically. A
277	+// re-index of the same session (same agent + session_id) replaces both in lockstep.
278	+func (s *Store) Upsert(sess Session) error {
279	+	return s.UpsertBatch([]Session{sess})
280	+}
281	+
282	+// UpsertBatch writes many sessions in one transaction — the write path for `reindex`,
283	+// which batches to avoid thousands of tiny WAL commits.
284	+func (s *Store) UpsertBatch(sessions []Session) error {
285	+	if len(sessions) == 0 {
286	+		return nil
287	+	}
288	+	tx, err := s.db.Begin()
289	+	if err != nil {
290	+		return fmt.Errorf("begin upsert: %w", err)
291	+	}
292	+	committed := false
293	+	defer func() {
294	+		if !committed {
295	+			_ = tx.Rollback()
296	+		}
297	+	}()
298	+
299	+	st, closeStmts, err := prepareUpsert(tx)
300	+	if err != nil {
301	+		return err
302	+	}
303	+	defer closeStmts()
304	+
305	+	for _, sess := range sessions {
306	+		if err := upsertOne(st, sess); err != nil {
307	+			return err
308	+		}
309	+	}
310	+
311	+	if err := tx.Commit(); err != nil {
312	+		return fmt.Errorf("commit upsert: %w", err)
313	+	}
314	+	committed = true
315	+	return nil
316	+}
317	+
318	+// Count returns the number of indexed sessions.
319	+func (s *Store) Count() (int, error) {
320	+	var n int
321	+	if err := s.db.QueryRow(`SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sessions`).Scan(&n); err != nil {
322	+		return 0, err
323	+	}
324	+	return n, nil
325	+}
326	+
327	+// ProjectCount returns the number of distinct attributed projects in the index
328	+// (excluding the unknownID bucket).
329	+func (s *Store) ProjectCount(unknownID string) (int, error) {
330	+	var n int
331	+	err := s.db.QueryRow(`SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT project_id) FROM sessions WHERE project_id != ?`, unknownID).Scan(&n)
332	+	return n, err
333	+}
334	+
335	+// UnattributedCount returns the number of sessions in the unknownID bucket.
336	+func (s *Store) UnattributedCount(unknownID string) (int, error) {
337	+	var n int
338	+	err := s.db.QueryRow(`SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sessions WHERE project_id = ?`, unknownID).Scan(&n)
339	+	return n, err
340	+}
341	+
342	+// ListByProject returns a project's sessions, newest activity first. Body is not
343	+// populated (it lives only in the FTS index). Used by the `specstory resume` picker.
344	+func (s *Store) ListByProject(projectID string) ([]Session, error) {
345	+	rows, err := s.db.Query(`SELECT `+sessionColumns+`
346	+		FROM sessions WHERE project_id = ? ORDER BY updated_at DESC, created_at DESC`, projectID)
347	+	if err != nil {
348	+		return nil, err
349	+	}
350	+	defer func() { _ = rows.Close() }()
351	+	return scanSessions(rows)
352	+}
353	+
354	+// ProjectSummary is a rolled-up view of one project for the all-projects picker.
355	+type ProjectSummary struct {
356	+	ProjectID    string
357	+	ProjectName  string
358	+	Sessions     int            // total sessions in the project
359	+	LastActivity string         // most recent updated_at across the project
360	+	AgentCounts  map[string]int // sessions per agent (claude, codex, …)
361	+}
362	+
363	+// ListProjects returns one rolled-up summary per project, most recently active first.
364	+// Used by the all-projects view (date-bucketed). The unknown-project bucket is included;
365	+// the caller decides how to present it.
366	+func (s *Store) ListProjects() ([]ProjectSummary, error) {
367	+	rows, err := s.db.Query(`SELECT project_id, project_name, agent, COUNT(*), MAX(updated_at)
368	+		FROM sessions GROUP BY project_id, agent`)
369	+	if err != nil {
370	+		return nil, err
371	+	}
372	+	defer func() { _ = rows.Close() }()
373	+
374	+	byID := map[string]*ProjectSummary{}
375	+	for rows.Next() {
376	+		var pid, pname, agent, last string
377	+		var n int
378	+		if err := rows.Scan(&pid, &pname, &agent, &n, &last); err != nil {
379	+			return nil, err
380	+		}
381	+		ps, ok := byID[pid]
382	+		if !ok {
383	+			ps = &ProjectSummary{ProjectID: pid, ProjectName: pname, AgentCounts: map[string]int{}}
384	+			byID[pid] = ps
385	+		}
386	+		ps.Sessions += n
387	+		ps.AgentCounts[agent] += n
388	+		if ps.ProjectName == "" {
389	+			ps.ProjectName = pname
390	+		}
391	+		if last > ps.LastActivity {
392	+			ps.LastActivity = last
393	+		}
394	+	}
395	+	if err := rows.Err(); err != nil {
396	+		return nil, err
397	+	}
398	+
399	+	out := make([]ProjectSummary, 0, len(byID))
400	+	for _, ps := range byID {
401	+		out = append(out, *ps)
402	+	}
403	+	sort.Slice(out, func(i, j int) bool { return out[i].LastActivity > out[j].LastActivity })
404	+	return out, nil
405	+}
406	+
407	+// SessionBody returns the full-text conversation body for a session (for the preview
408	+// pane), or "" if the session has no indexed body (e.g. Cursor, metadata-only).
409	+func (s *Store) SessionBody(agent, sessionID string) (string, error) {
410	+	var body string
411	+	err := s.db.QueryRow(`SELECT body FROM sessions_fts WHERE agent = ? AND session_id = ?`,
412	+		agent, sessionID).Scan(&body)
413	+	if err == sql.ErrNoRows {
414	+		return "", nil
415	+	}
416	+	return body, err
417	+}
418	+
419	+// Search runs a full-text query and returns matching sessions, most recent first.
420	+// projectID scopes the search to one project; "" searches across all projects.
421	+func (s *Store) Search(query, projectID string) ([]Session, error) {
422	+	return s.SearchContext(context.Background(), query, projectID)
423	+}
424	+
425	+// SearchContext is Search bound to a context. The interactive search cancels the context
426	+// when a newer keystroke arrives, which aborts a slow in-flight query and frees its
427	+// connection. Snippets are deliberately fetched separately for only the visible rows:
428	+// FTS5 snippet generation over hundreds of full transcripts dominates broad searches.
429	+func (s *Store) SearchContext(ctx context.Context, query, projectID string) ([]Session, error) {
430	+	q := `SELECT ` + prefixed("s", sessionColumns) + `
431	+		FROM sessions_fts
432	+		JOIN sessions s ON s.agent = sessions_fts.agent AND s.session_id = sessions_fts.session_id
433	+		WHERE sessions_fts MATCH ?`
434	+	args := []any{query}
435	+	if projectID != "" {
436	+		q += ` AND s.project_id = ?`
437	+		args = append(args, projectID)
438	+	}
439	+	// Order by recency, not FTS5's BM25 rank. For full-conversation transcripts BM25 is
440	+	// noisy (length-normalization dominates) and a single-term query gets no IDF signal,
441	+	// so relevance order looks arbitrary; newest-first is what's useful when browsing your
442	+	// own history. It also makes LIMIT keep the 500 most RECENT matches (vs the 500 densest)
443	+	// and skips BM25 scoring entirely. updated_at is ISO 8601, so TEXT sort = chronological.
444	+	q += ` ORDER BY s.updated_at DESC LIMIT 500`
445	+
446	+	rows, err := s.db.QueryContext(ctx, q, args...)
447	+	if err != nil {
448	+		return nil, err
449	+	}
450	+	defer func() { _ = rows.Close() }()
451	+
452	+	return scanSessions(rows)
453	+}
454	+
455	+// Snippets returns highlighted match snippets for the provided sessions. The result is
456	+// keyed by FingerprintKey(agent, session_id). Matched terms are wrapped in \x02 and \x03
457	+// (STX/ETX) so callers can highlight without colliding with conversation text.
458	+func (s *Store) Snippets(query string, sessions []Session) (map[string]string, error) {
459	+	return s.SnippetsContext(context.Background(), query, sessions)
460	+}
461	+
462	+// SnippetsContext is Snippets bound to a context. Callers should pass only the currently
463	+// visible rows; snippet() is intentionally lazy because it is the expensive part of FTS
464	+// search on long transcripts.
465	+func (s *Store) SnippetsContext(ctx context.Context, query string, sessions []Session) (map[string]string, error) {
466	+	out := map[string]string{}
467	+	if query == "" || len(sessions) == 0 {
468	+		return out, nil
469	+	}
470	+
471	+	clauses := make([]string, 0, len(sessions))
472	+	args := []any{query}
473	+	seen := map[string]bool{}
474	+	for _, sess := range sessions {
475	+		key := FingerprintKey(sess.Agent, sess.SessionID)
476	+		if seen[key] {
477	+			continue
478	+		}
479	+		seen[key] = true
480	+		clauses = append(clauses, `(agent = ? AND session_id = ?)`)
481	+		args = append(args, sess.Agent, sess.SessionID)
482	+	}
483	+	if len(clauses) == 0 {
484	+		return out, nil
485	+	}
486	+
487	+	q := `SELECT agent, session_id, snippet(sessions_fts, 3, char(2), char(3), '…', 12)
488	+		FROM sessions_fts
489	+		WHERE sessions_fts MATCH ? AND (` + strings.Join(clauses, ` OR `) + `)`
490	+	rows, err := s.db.QueryContext(ctx, q, args...)
491	+	if err != nil {
492	+		return nil, err
493	+	}
494	+	defer func() { _ = rows.Close() }()
495	+
496	+	for rows.Next() {
497	+		var agent, sessionID, snippet string
498	+		if err := rows.Scan(&agent, &sessionID, &snippet); err != nil {
499	+			return nil, err
500	+		}
501	+		out[FingerprintKey(agent, sessionID)] = snippet
502	+	}
503	+	return out, rows.Err()
504	+}
505	+
506	+// prefixed qualifies each comma-separated column in cols with the given table alias,
507	+// e.g. prefixed("s", "a, b") -> "s.a, s.b". Used to disambiguate the sessions columns
508	+// in the FTS join (where session_id/agent also exist on the FTS table).
509	+func prefixed(alias, cols string) string {
510	+	parts := strings.Split(cols, ",")
511	+	for i, p := range parts {
512	+		parts[i] = alias + "." + strings.TrimSpace(p)
513	+	}
514	+	return strings.Join(parts, ", ")
515	+}
516	+
517	+// scanSessions scans rows selected with sessionColumns (in order) into Sessions.
518	+func scanSessions(rows *sql.Rows) ([]Session, error) {
519	+	var out []Session
520	+	for rows.Next() {
521	+		var s Session
522	+		if err := rows.Scan(
523	+			&s.ProjectID, &s.ProjectName, &s.Agent, &s.SessionID, &s.CreatedAt, &s.UpdatedAt,
524	+			&s.UserTurns, &s.TotalTurns, &s.Slug, &s.Name, &s.NativePath, &s.OriginCwd,
525	+			&s.Size, &s.Mtime, &s.IndexVersion, &s.IndexedAt,
526	+		); err != nil {
527	+			return nil, err
528	+		}
529	+		out = append(out, s)
530	+	}
531	+	return out, rows.Err()
532	+}

548	+	return Session{
549	+		ProjectID:   projectID,
550	+		ProjectName: "widgets",
551	+		Agent:       agent,
552	+		SessionID:   id,
553	+		CreatedAt:   "2026-06-18T10:00:00Z",
554	+		UpdatedAt:   "2026-06-18T11:00:00Z",
555	+		UserTurns:   3,
556	+		TotalTurns:  9,
557	+		Slug:        "do-the-thing",
558	+		Name:        name,
559	+		NativePath:  "/store/" + id + ".jsonl",
560	+		OriginCwd:   "/repo",
561	+		Size:        1234,
562	+		Mtime:       1700000000000,
563	+		IndexedAt:   "2026-06-18T12:00:00Z",
564	+		Body:        body,
565	+	}
566	+}

568	+func openTemp(t *testing.T) *Store {
569	+	t.Helper()
570	+	s, err := Open(filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "sessions.db"))
571	+	if err != nil {
572	+		t.Fatalf("Open: %v", err)
573	+	}
574	+	t.Cleanup(func() { _ = s.Close() })
575	+	return s
576	+}

578	+func TestUpsertAndListByProject(t *testing.T) {
579	+	s := openTemp(t)
580	+
581	+	if err := s.Upsert(newSession("claude", "c1", "proj-a", "Auth refactor", "rewrote the login flow")); err != nil {
582	+		t.Fatal(err)
583	+	}
584	+	if err := s.Upsert(newSession("codex", "x1", "proj-a", "Billing migration", "moved billing to stripe")); err != nil {
585	+		t.Fatal(err)
586	+	}
587	+	if err := s.Upsert(newSession("claude", "c2", "proj-b", "Other project", "unrelated work")); err != nil {
588	+		t.Fatal(err)
589	+	}
590	+
591	+	if n, err := s.Count(); err != nil || n != 3 {
592	+		t.Fatalf("Count = %d, %v; want 3", n, err)
593	+	}
594	+
595	+	got, err := s.ListByProject("proj-a")
596	+	if err != nil {
597	+		t.Fatal(err)
598	+	}
599	+	if len(got) != 2 {
600	+		t.Fatalf("ListByProject(proj-a) = %d rows; want 2", len(got))
601	+	}
602	+	for _, sess := range got {
603	+		if sess.ProjectID != "proj-a" {
604	+			t.Errorf("got project_id %q; want proj-a", sess.ProjectID)
605	+		}
606	+		if sess.UserTurns != 3 || sess.TotalTurns != 9 {
607	+			t.Errorf("turn counts not round-tripped: user=%d total=%d", sess.UserTurns, sess.TotalTurns)
608	+		}
609	+	}
610	+}

612	+func TestSearchFTS(t *testing.T) {
613	+	s := openTemp(t)
614	+	mustUpsert(t, s, newSession("claude", "c1", "proj-a", "Auth refactor", "rewrote the login flow with oauth"))
615	+	mustUpsert(t, s, newSession("codex", "x1", "proj-a", "Billing migration", "moved billing to stripe webhooks"))
616	+
617	+	// Body match
618	+	hits, err := s.Search("oauth", "")
619	+	if err != nil {
620	+		t.Fatal(err)
621	+	}
622	+	if len(hits) != 1 || hits[0].SessionID != "c1" {
623	+		t.Fatalf("Search(oauth) = %+v; want only c1", hits)
624	+	}
625	+
626	+	// Name match
627	+	hits, err = s.Search("billing", "")
628	+	if err != nil {
629	+		t.Fatal(err)
630	+	}
631	+	if len(hits) != 1 || hits[0].SessionID != "x1" {
632	+		t.Fatalf("Search(billing) = %+v; want only x1", hits)
633	+	}
634	+}
635	+
636	+func TestUpsertIsIdempotent(t *testing.T) {
637	+	s := openTemp(t)
638	+	mustUpsert(t, s, newSession("claude", "c1", "proj-a", "First name", "first body text"))
639	+	// Re-index the same (agent, session_id) with changed content.
640	+	mustUpsert(t, s, newSession("claude", "c1", "proj-a", "Renamed", "second body text"))
641	+
642	+	if n, _ := s.Count(); n != 1 {
643	+		t.Fatalf("Count = %d after re-upsert; want 1 (replaced, not duplicated)", n)
644	+	}
645	+	// Old FTS content must be gone; new content searchable.
646	+	if hits, _ := s.Search("first", ""); len(hits) != 0 {
647	+		t.Errorf("stale FTS row survived re-upsert: %+v", hits)
648	+	}
649	+	if hits, _ := s.Search("second", ""); len(hits) != 1 {
650	+		t.Errorf("new FTS content not searchable after re-upsert")
651	+	}
652	+}
653	+
654	+func mustUpsert(t *testing.T, s *Store, sess Session) {
655	+	t.Helper()
656	+	if err := s.Upsert(sess); err != nil {
657	+		t.Fatalf("Upsert(%s): %v", sess.SessionID, err)
658	+	}
659	+}
660	+
661	+func TestFingerprintsRoundTrip(t *testing.T) {
662	+	s := openTemp(t)
663	+	a := newSession("claude", "c1", "proj-a", "Auth", "body")
664	+	a.Size, a.Mtime, a.IndexVersion = 111, 222, 3

681	+	}
682	+}

684	+func TestProjectAndUnattributedCounts(t *testing.T) {
685	+	s := openTemp(t)
686	+	mustUpsert(t, s, newSession("claude", "c1", "proj-a", "a", "b"))
687	+	mustUpsert(t, s, newSession("codex", "x1", "proj-a", "a", "b")) // same project
688	+	mustUpsert(t, s, newSession("claude", "c2", "proj-b", "a", "b"))
689	+	mustUpsert(t, s, newSession("cursor", "z1", "unknown", "a", "b"))
690	+	mustUpsert(t, s, newSession("cursor", "z2", "unknown", "a", "b"))
691	+
692	+	if n, err := s.ProjectCount("unknown"); err != nil || n != 2 {
693	+		t.Errorf("ProjectCount = %d, %v; want 2 (proj-a, proj-b)", n, err)
694	+	}
695	+	if n, err := s.UnattributedCount("unknown"); err != nil || n != 2 {
696	+		t.Errorf("UnattributedCount = %d, %v; want 2", n, err)
697	+	}
698	+}
699	+
700	+func TestListProjectsRollup(t *testing.T) {
701	+	s := openTemp(t)
702	+	a := newSession("claude", "c1", "proj-a", "n", "b")
703	+	a.UpdatedAt = "2026-06-18T10:00:00Z"

707	+	mustUpsert(t, s, b)

709	+	c.UpdatedAt = "2026-06-17T09:00:00Z"
710	+	mustUpsert(t, s, c)
711	+
712	+	projs, err := s.ListProjects()

714	+		t.Fatal(err)
715	+	}
716	+	if len(projs) != 2 {
717	+		t.Fatalf("got %d projects; want 2", len(projs))
718	+	}
719	+	// proj-a is most recently active (codex at 12:00) → first.
720	+	if projs[0].ProjectID != "proj-a" {
721	+		t.Errorf("expected proj-a first (most recent), got %s", projs[0].ProjectID)
722	+	}
723	+	if projs[0].Sessions != 2 || projs[0].AgentCounts["claude"] != 1 || projs[0].AgentCounts["codex"] != 1 {
724	+		t.Errorf("proj-a rollup wrong: sessions=%d agents=%v", projs[0].Sessions, projs[0].AgentCounts)
725	+	}

728	+	}
729	+}

37	+

39	+	reindexWriteBatch = 200
40	+
41	+	// reindexVersion is the logic version stamped on every indexed row. Bump it whenever
42	+	// the parse/flatten/derivation logic changes so the next reindex re-parses everything
43	+	// even when files are unchanged. It is part of the freshness fingerprint.
44	+	//   2: skip slash-command noise when choosing a Claude session title (fixes UUID titles)
45	+	//   3: also skip "[Request interrupted by user…]" markers
46	+	//   4: canonicalize (case-fold) the workspace_id path hash + prefer a persisted
47	+	//      .project.json workspace_id, so a project's id no longer varies with path casing
48	+	//   5: parse Codex bodies from the enumerated NativePath (was a by-id tree search), so a
49	+	//      session's body and its freshness fingerprint now derive from the same file
50	+	reindexVersion = 5
