52	+
53	+// CreateReindexCommand builds the `specstory reindex` command: a full, from-scratch
54	+// rebuild of the restore index (~/.specstory/sessions.db) of every coding-agent session
55	+// SpecStory can find, across all projects and providers. See docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
56	+func CreateReindexCommand() *cobra.Command {
57	+	cmd := &cobra.Command{
58	+		Use:   "reindex",
59	+		Short: "Rebuild the restore index of all known agent sessions",
60	+		Long: `Rebuild the restore index used by 'specstory resume'.
61	+
62	+'reindex' enumerates every session across all installed agents and projects and writes a
63	+searchable index to ~/.specstory/sessions.db. It is incremental: a session whose native
64	+file is unchanged since it was last indexed is skipped, so re-runs are fast. Use --force to
65	+re-index everything regardless. The index is a derived cache: it is safe to delete.`,
66	+		Args: cobra.NoArgs,
67	+		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
68	+			force, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("force")
69	+			return runReindex(force)
70	+		},
71	+	}
72	+	cmd.Flags().Bool("force", false, "re-index every session even if unchanged")
73	+	return cmd
74	+}
75	+
76	+// reindexItem is one unit of work: a session to parse and index. size/mtime are the
77	+// native file's stat, captured during the freshness check and reused (no re-stat).
78	+type reindexItem struct {
79	+	agent string
80	+	prov  spi.Provider
81	+	ref   spi.GlobalSessionRef
82	+	size  int64
83	+	mtime int64
84	+	isNew bool // no existing index row → writer can skip the standalone-FTS delete
85	+}
86	+
87	+func runReindex(force bool) error {
88	+	start := time.Now()
89	+
90	+	// Graceful Ctrl+C: stop feeding work, flush what's written, exit cleanly.
91	+	ctx, cancel := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), os.Interrupt, syscall.SIGTERM)
92	+	defer cancel()
93	+
94	+	dbPath, err := sessionindex.DefaultPath()
95	+	if err != nil {
96	+		return err
97	+	}
98	+	store, err := sessionindex.Open(dbPath)
99	+	if err != nil {
100	+		return fmt.Errorf("opening restore index: %w", err)
101	+	}
102	+	defer func() { _ = store.Close() }()
103	+
104	+	registry := factory.GetRegistry()
105	+
106	+	// ---- Phase 1: enumerate every provider concurrently, then dedup ----
107	+	ids, provs, perProvider := enumerateAll(registry, true)
108	+
109	+	// Existing fingerprints, so unchanged sessions can be skipped (the incremental path).
110	+	fingerprints := map[string]sessionindex.Fingerprint{}
111	+	if !force {
112	+		if fps, err := store.Fingerprints(); err != nil {
113	+			slog.Warn("reindex: could not load fingerprints, re-indexing all", "error", err)
114	+		} else {
115	+			fingerprints = fps
116	+		}
117	+	}
118	+
119	+	best, order, foundPerAgent := dedupRefs(ids, provs, perProvider)
120	+	found := len(order)
121	+	cache := &projectIDCache{m: map[string]projectIDName{}}
122	+	work, totals, unchanged := selectWork(order, best, fingerprints, "", force, cache)
123	+
124	+	if found == 0 {
125	+		fprintln(os.Stderr, "No agent sessions found to index.")
126	+		return nil
127	+	}
128	+	fprintf(os.Stderr, "✓   Found %d sessions  ·  %s\n", found, summarizeCounts(ids, foundPerAgent))
129	+	if len(work) == 0 {
130	+		fprintf(os.Stderr, "✓   All %d sessions already up to date.  (%.1fs)\n", found, time.Since(start).Seconds())
131	+		return nil
132	+	}
133	+	if unchanged > 0 {
134	+		fprintf(os.Stderr, "    %d unchanged · indexing %d…\n\n", unchanged, len(work))
135	+	} else {
136	+		fprintln(os.Stderr, "")
137	+	}
138	+
139	+	// ---- Phase 2+3: parse (worker pool) → write (single goroutine), with live progress ----
140	+	prog := newReindexProgress(ids, totals)
141	+	prog.begin()
142	+	indexedAt := start.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
143	+	writeErr := processWork(ctx, store, work, cache, indexedAt, min(runtime.NumCPU(), 12), prog)
144	+	prog.end()
145	+
146	+	if writeErr != nil {
147	+		return fmt.Errorf("writing restore index: %w", writeErr)
148	+	}
149	+
150	+	// ---- Summary (counts reflect the WHOLE index, not just this run's work) ----
151	+	elapsed := time.Since(start)
152	+	projects, _ := store.ProjectCount(unknownProjectID)
153	+	unattributed, _ := store.UnattributedCount(unknownProjectID)
154	+	printReindexSummary(ids, foundPerAgent, dbPath, elapsed, prog.totalDone(), unchanged, projects, unattributed)
155	+	analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventReindexCompleted, analytics.Properties{
156	+		"sessions":     len(work),
157	+		"unchanged":    unchanged,
158	+		"projects":     projects,
159	+		"unattributed": unattributed,
160	+		"duration_ms":  elapsed.Milliseconds(),
161	+	})
162	+	return nil
163	+}
164	+
165	+// ---- reindex engine (shared by the foreground command and the background warm) ----
166	+
167	+// enumerateAll concurrently lists every installed provider's sessions. The returned slices
168	+// are index-aligned: provs[i]/perProvider[i] correspond to ids[i] (a nil provs[i] marks a
169	+// provider that failed to load). When visible, it renders a live "Scanning agents…" line
170	+// (foreground reindex); the background warm passes visible=false and stays silent.
171	+func enumerateAll(registry *factory.Registry, visible bool) (ids []string, provs []spi.Provider, perProvider [][]spi.GlobalSessionRef) {
172	+	ids = registry.ListIDs()
173	+	perProvider = make([][]spi.GlobalSessionRef, len(ids))
174	+	provs = make([]spi.Provider, len(ids))
175	+
176	+	var scan *scanProgress
177	+	if visible {
178	+		scan = newScanProgress(ids)
179	+		scan.begin()
180	+		defer scan.end()
181	+	}
182	+
183	+	var ewg sync.WaitGroup
184	+	for i, id := range ids {
185	+		prov, err := registry.Get(id)
186	+		if err != nil {
187	+			continue
188	+		}
189	+		provs[i] = prov
190	+		reporter := scan.reporterFor(id)
191	+		ewg.Add(1)
192	+		go func(i int, id string, prov spi.Provider, reporter *spi.ScanReporter) {
193	+			defer ewg.Done()
194	+			var refs []spi.GlobalSessionRef
195	+			var err error
196	+			// Providers that can report progress (Codex, the long pole) tick the live line; the
197	+			// rest snap to their final count via markDone when they finish.
198	+			if pe, ok := prov.(spi.ProgressEnumerator); ok {
199	+				refs, err = pe.ListAllAgentChatSessionsProgress(reporter)
200	+			} else {
201	+				refs, err = prov.ListAllAgentChatSessions()
202	+			}
203	+			if err != nil {
204	+				slog.Warn("reindex: enumeration failed", "provider", id, "error", err)
205	+			}
206	+			perProvider[i] = refs
207	+			scan.markDone(id, len(refs))
208	+		}(i, id, prov, reporter)
209	+	}
210	+	ewg.Wait()
211	+	return ids, provs, perProvider
212	+}
213	+
214	+// dedupRefs collapses enumerated refs by (agent, session_id), keeping the freshest file. A
215	+// resumed session can span MULTIPLE native files sharing one id; they all map to one
216	+// (agent, session_id) row, so without dedup the duplicates ping-pong — each run, one file's
217	+// stat never matches the fingerprint the other file just stored, re-indexing both forever.
218	+// Keeping the freshest by mtime makes the fingerprint stable. Sessions with no native id are
219	+// dropped (unresumable, and would collide on the PK). order is first-seen order (deterministic
220	+// processing); foundPerAgent is the distinct-session count per agent (for the "Found" line).
221	+func dedupRefs(ids []string, provs []spi.Provider, perProvider [][]spi.GlobalSessionRef) (best map[string]reindexItem, order []string, foundPerAgent map[string]int) {
222	+	best = map[string]reindexItem{}
223	+	foundPerAgent = map[string]int{}
224	+	for i, id := range ids {
225	+		if provs[i] == nil {
226	+			continue
227	+		}
228	+		for _, ref := range perProvider[i] {
229	+			if strings.TrimSpace(ref.SessionID) == "" {
230	+				slog.Debug("reindex: skipping session with no id", "agent", id, "path", ref.NativePath)
231	+				continue
232	+			}
233	+			size, mtime := statNative(ref.NativePath)
234	+			item := reindexItem{agent: id, prov: provs[i], ref: ref, size: size, mtime: mtime}
235	+			key := sessionindex.FingerprintKey(id, ref.SessionID)
236	+			if cur, ok := best[key]; ok {
237	+				if mtime > cur.mtime {
238	+					best[key] = item
239	+				}
240	+				continue
241	+			}
242	+			best[key] = item
243	+			order = append(order, key)
244	+		}
245	+	}
246	+	for _, key := range order {
247	+		foundPerAgent[best[key].agent]++
248	+	}
249	+	return best, order, foundPerAgent
250	+}
251	+
252	+// selectWork applies the incremental fingerprint skip (and an optional project filter) to the
253	+// deduped set. projectFilter "" means all projects; otherwise only sessions whose originating
254	+// cwd resolves to that project id are kept (used by the background warm's current-project
255	+// pass). totals is the per-agent to-do count (drives the foreground progress bars).
256	+func selectWork(order []string, best map[string]reindexItem, fingerprints map[string]sessionindex.Fingerprint, projectFilter string, force bool, cache *projectIDCache) (work []reindexItem, totals map[string]int, unchanged int) {
257	+	totals = map[string]int{}
258	+	for _, key := range order {
259	+		item := best[key]
260	+		if projectFilter != "" {
261	+			if pid, _ := cache.resolve(item.ref.OriginCwd); pid != projectFilter {
262	+				continue
263	+			}
264	+		}
265	+		fp, ok := fingerprints[key]
266	+		if ok && fp.Size == item.size && fp.Mtime == item.mtime && fp.Version == reindexVersion {
267	+			unchanged++
268	+			continue // already indexed and unchanged
269	+		}
270	+		// A session absent from the fingerprint set has no sessions row, hence no sessions_fts
271	+		// row either (the pair is written atomically) — so the writer can skip the O(rows)
272	+		// standalone-FTS delete. Under --force the fingerprint map is deliberately empty, so
273	+		// absence proves nothing; keep the delete to avoid duplicate FTS rows.
274	+		item.isNew = !force && !ok
275	+		work = append(work, item)
276	+		totals[item.agent]++
277	+	}
278	+	return work, totals, unchanged
279	+}
280	+
281	+// reindexReporter receives per-session progress from processWork. The foreground command
282	+// passes *reindexProgress (live bars); the background warm passes nopReporter (silent).
283	+type reindexReporter interface {
284	+	observe(projectID string)
285	+	inc(agent string)
286	+}
287	+
288	+// nopReporter is the silent reporter used by the background warm.
289	+type nopReporter struct{}
290	+
291	+func (nopReporter) observe(string) {}
292	+func (nopReporter) inc(string)     {}
293	+
294	+// processWork parses each work item (worker pool) and writes the results to the store via a
295	+// single writer goroutine, reporting progress through rep. It stops feeding work when ctx is
296	+// cancelled; the writer flushes what it already received. Returns the writer's error, if any.
297	+func processWork(ctx context.Context, store *sessionindex.Store, work []reindexItem, cache *projectIDCache, indexedAt string, workers int, rep reindexReporter) error {
298	+	if len(work) == 0 {
299	+		return nil
300	+	}
301	+	if workers < 1 {
302	+		workers = 1
303	+	}
304	+
305	+	// Schedule the largest sessions first. A few huge Codex files (hundreds of MB) would
306	+	// otherwise be end-of-run stragglers that leave most worker cores idle; starting them
307	+	// first overlaps them with the long tail of small sessions. Order does not affect the
308	+	// resulting index.
309	+	sort.SliceStable(work, func(i, j int) bool { return work[i].size > work[j].size })
310	+
311	+	sessionsCh := make(chan sessionindex.Session, 256)
312	+	var writeErr error
313	+	writerDone := make(chan struct{})
314	+	go func() {
315	+		defer close(writerDone)
316	+		writeErr = drainToStore(store, sessionsCh)
317	+	}()
318	+
319	+	workCh := make(chan reindexItem)
320	+	var wwg sync.WaitGroup
321	+	for w := 0; w < workers; w++ {
322	+		wwg.Add(1)
323	+		go func() {
324	+			defer wwg.Done()
325	+			for item := range workCh {
326	+				sess := buildSession(item, cache, indexedAt)
327	+				rep.observe(sess.ProjectID)
328	+				sessionsCh <- sess
329	+				rep.inc(item.agent)
330	+			}
331	+		}()
332	+	}
333	+
334	+	for _, item := range work {
335	+		select {
336	+		case <-ctx.Done():
337	+			goto closing
338	+		case workCh <- item:
339	+		}
340	+	}
341	+closing:
342	+	close(workCh)
343	+	wwg.Wait()
344	+	close(sessionsCh)
345	+	<-writerDone
346	+	return writeErr
347	+}
348	+
349	+// warmIndexInBackground keeps ~/.specstory/sessions.db warm as a side effect of resume/search.
350	+// It runs two SILENT incremental passes over its own writer handle: the current project first
351	+// (so its rows refresh quickly, and the open TUI re-queries via onProjectWarmed), then the full
352	+// corpus. It enumerates once and reuses the result for both passes. It stops promptly when ctx
353	+// is cancelled (the TUI exited); an abandoned write batch is safe under WAL. Errors are logged
354	+// at debug and never surfaced — warmth is best-effort.
355	+func warmIndexInBackground(ctx context.Context, dbPath, currentProjectID string, onProjectWarmed func()) {
356	+	store, err := sessionindex.Open(dbPath)
357	+	if err != nil {
358	+		slog.Debug("warm: could not open index", "error", err)
359	+		return
360	+	}
361	+	defer func() { _ = store.Close() }()
362	+
363	+	registry := factory.GetRegistry()
364	+	ids, provs, perProvider := enumerateAll(registry, false)
365	+	if ctx.Err() != nil {
366	+		return
367	+	}
368	+	best, order, _ := dedupRefs(ids, provs, perProvider)
369	+	cache := &projectIDCache{m: map[string]projectIDName{}}
370	+	indexedAt := time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
371	+
372	+	// Gentle: fewer workers than a foreground reindex, since the user is interacting.
373	+	workers := min(runtime.NumCPU(), 4)
374	+
375	+	// Pass 1 — the current project, so its rows are fresh almost immediately.
376	+	fps, _ := store.Fingerprints()
377	+	work, _, _ := selectWork(order, best, fps, currentProjectID, false, cache)
378	+	if err := processWork(ctx, store, work, cache, indexedAt, workers, nopReporter{}); err != nil {
379	+		slog.Debug("warm: current-project pass failed", "error", err)
380	+	}
381	+	if ctx.Err() != nil {
382	+		return
383	+	}
384	+	if onProjectWarmed != nil {
385	+		onProjectWarmed() // let the open TUI re-query the (now fresh) current project
386	+	}
387	+
388	+	// Pass 2 — everything else. Reload fingerprints so pass-1's writes now register as
389	+	// unchanged and are skipped, leaving only the rest of the corpus to parse.
390	+	fps, _ = store.Fingerprints()
391	+	work, _, _ = selectWork(order, best, fps, "", false, cache)
392	+	if err := processWork(ctx, store, work, cache, indexedAt, workers, nopReporter{}); err != nil {
393	+		slog.Debug("warm: full pass failed", "error", err)
394	+	}
395	+}
396	+
397	+// ---- real-time indexing (watch / run / resume launch) ----
398	+
399	+// LiveIndexer keeps ~/.specstory/sessions.db current in real time, alongside the markdown
400	+// files that watch/run/resume already write as a session changes. It holds one writer handle
401	+// for the command's lifetime and upserts a single row per detected update, reusing the same
402	+// content derivation as reindex. The project is resolved once (a watch/run has a fixed cwd).
403	+//
404	+// Everything is best-effort: real-time indexing must never disrupt the agent or the markdown
405	+// write, so construction failures yield a nil *LiveIndexer (whose methods are no-ops) and
406	+// per-update errors are logged at debug. A live row carries no native-file fingerprint
407	+// (Size/Mtime/NativePath are unknown without enumerating), so the next reindex re-verifies and
408	+// rewrites it with a stable fingerprint — the live row is correct and queryable in the interim.
409	+type LiveIndexer struct {
410	+	store       *sessionindex.Store
411	+	cwd         string
412	+	projectID   string
413	+	projectName string
414	+	indexedAt   string
415	+
416	+	mu       sync.Mutex
417	+	lastSeen map[string]string // session id -> last indexed UpdatedAt, to skip no-op upserts
418	+}
419	+
420	+// NewLiveIndexer opens the index for writing and resolves the project for cwd. It returns nil
421	+// (not an error) when the index can't be opened, so the caller keeps running with real-time
422	+// indexing simply disabled. The caller owns Close.
423	+func NewLiveIndexer(cwd string) *LiveIndexer {
424	+	dbPath, err := sessionindex.DefaultPath()
425	+	if err != nil {
426	+		slog.Debug("live index: no db path; real-time indexing disabled", "error", err)
427	+		return nil
428	+	}
429	+	store, err := sessionindex.Open(dbPath)
430	+	if err != nil {
431	+		slog.Debug("live index: cannot open index; real-time indexing disabled", "error", err)
432	+		return nil
433	+	}
434	+	projectID, projectName, err := utils.ComputeProjectID(cwd)
435	+	if err != nil || projectID == "" {
436	+		projectID, projectName = unknownProjectID, ""
437	+	}
438	+	return &LiveIndexer{
439	+		store:       store,
440	+		cwd:         cwd,
441	+		projectID:   projectID,
442	+		projectName: projectName,
443	+		indexedAt:   time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
444	+		lastSeen:    map[string]string{},
445	+	}
446	+}
447	+
448	+// Record upserts the live session into the index. agentID is the provider's registry id (e.g.
449	+// "claude"). It skips the write when the session's last activity hasn't advanced since the
450	+// previous record (the watcher can fire repeatedly without new content). Safe on a nil
451	+// receiver, and serialized so concurrent provider watchers don't contend on the single writer.
452	+func (li *LiveIndexer) Record(agentID string, sess *spi.AgentChatSession) {
453	+	if li == nil || sess == nil || sess.SessionData == nil {
454	+		return
455	+	}
456	+	data := sess.SessionData
457	+
458	+	updatedAt := sess.CreatedAt
459	+	if ts := lastTimestamp(data); ts != "" {
460	+		updatedAt = ts
461	+	}
462	+
463	+	li.mu.Lock()
464	+	defer li.mu.Unlock()
465	+
466	+	// Nothing advanced since the last write → skip the (FTS-heavy) upsert.
467	+	if updatedAt != "" && li.lastSeen[sess.SessionID] == updatedAt {
468	+		return
469	+	}
470	+
471	+	userTurns, totalTurns := countTurns(data)
472	+	row := sessionindex.Session{
473	+		ProjectID:    li.projectID,
474	+		ProjectName:  li.projectName,
475	+		Agent:        agentID,
476	+		SessionID:    sess.SessionID,
477	+		CreatedAt:    sess.CreatedAt,
478	+		UpdatedAt:    updatedAt,
479	+		UserTurns:    userTurns,
480	+		TotalTurns:   totalTurns,
481	+		Slug:         sess.Slug,
482	+		OriginCwd:    li.cwd,
483	+		IndexVersion: reindexVersion,
484	+		IndexedAt:    li.indexedAt,
485	+		Body:         flattenBody(data),
486	+	}
487	+	if err := li.store.Upsert(row); err != nil {
488	+		slog.Debug("live index: upsert failed", "sessionId", sess.SessionID, "error", err)
489	+		return
490	+	}
491	+	li.lastSeen[sess.SessionID] = updatedAt
492	+}
493	+
494	+// Close releases the writer handle. Safe on a nil receiver.
495	+func (li *LiveIndexer) Close() {
496	+	if li == nil {
497	+		return
498	+	}
499	+	if err := li.store.Close(); err != nil {
500	+		slog.Debug("live index: close failed", "error", err)
501	+	}
502	+}
503	+
504	+// buildSession turns one enumeration ref into a fully-built index row. Every session
505	+// yields a row from its metadata; the body + turn counts + last-activity time are
506	+// ENRICHED via a full parse only when the originating cwd lets us re-locate the
507	+// session (so Cursor, whose cwd is unknown for now, lands metadata-only). Nothing is
508	+// dropped.
509	+func buildSession(item reindexItem, cache *projectIDCache, indexedAt string) sessionindex.Session {
510	+	ref := item.ref
511	+	projectID, projectName := cache.resolve(ref.OriginCwd)
512	+
513	+	sess := sessionindex.Session{
514	+		ProjectID:    projectID,
515	+		ProjectName:  projectName,
516	+		Agent:        item.agent,
517	+		SessionID:    ref.SessionID,
518	+		CreatedAt:    ref.CreatedAt,
519	+		UpdatedAt:    ref.CreatedAt, // default; overwritten by last-turn timestamp below
520	+		Slug:         ref.Slug,
521	+		Name:         ref.Name,
522	+		NativePath:   ref.NativePath,
523	+		OriginCwd:    ref.OriginCwd,
524	+		Size:         item.size, // captured during the freshness check (no re-stat)
525	+		Mtime:        item.mtime,
526	+		IndexVersion: reindexVersion,
527	+		IndexedAt:    indexedAt,
528	+		IsNew:        item.isNew,
529	+	}
530	+
531	+	if ref.OriginCwd == "" {
532	+		return sess // cannot re-locate without a cwd → metadata-only
533	+	}
534	+	full, err := parseFullSession(item.prov, ref)
535	+	if err != nil || full == nil || full.SessionData == nil {
536	+		slog.Debug("reindex: full parse unavailable, indexing metadata only",
537	+			"agent", item.agent, "session", ref.SessionID, "error", err)
538	+		return sess
539	+	}
540	+
541	+	data := full.SessionData
542	+	sess.Body = flattenBody(data)
543	+	sess.UserTurns, sess.TotalTurns = countTurns(data)
544	+	if ts := lastTimestamp(data); ts != "" {
545	+		sess.UpdatedAt = ts
546	+	}
547	+	return sess
548	+}
549	+
550	+// parseFullSession reads a session's full data for indexing. It prefers a provider's
551	+// path-keyed parse (spi.PathSessionReader) using the ref's already-known NativePath, which
552	+// avoids the provider's by-id discovery search — for Codex that search walks the entire
553	+// ~/.codex/sessions tree, so this is the difference between an O(N) and an O(N²) reindex.
554	+// Providers without the capability (or refs lacking a NativePath) fall back to the by-id
555	+// lookup. Parsing the known NativePath is also more consistent: the row's freshness
556	+// fingerprint is stat'd from NativePath, so the body now comes from that same file.
557	+func parseFullSession(prov spi.Provider, ref spi.GlobalSessionRef) (*spi.AgentChatSession, error) {
558	+	if pr, ok := prov.(spi.PathSessionReader); ok && ref.NativePath != "" {
559	+		return pr.GetAgentChatSessionByPath(ref.NativePath, ref.OriginCwd, false)
560	+	}
561	+	return prov.GetAgentChatSession(ref.OriginCwd, ref.SessionID, false)
562	+}
563	+
564	+// drainToStore writes parsed sessions to the index in batched transactions.
565	+func drainToStore(store *sessionindex.Store, ch <-chan sessionindex.Session) error {
566	+	batch := make([]sessionindex.Session, 0, reindexWriteBatch)
567	+	flush := func() error {
568	+		if err := store.UpsertBatch(batch); err != nil {
569	+			return err
570	+		}
571	+		batch = batch[:0]
572	+		return nil
573	+	}
574	+	for sess := range ch {
575	+		batch = append(batch, sess)
576	+		if len(batch) >= reindexWriteBatch {
577	+			if err := flush(); err != nil {
578	+				return err
579	+			}
580	+		}
581	+	}
582	+	return flush()
583	+}
584	+
585	+// ---- session field derivation ----
586	+
587	+// flattenBody renders SessionData to plain user/agent text for full-text indexing,
588	+// reusing the reconstruction flattener (synthetic noise already stripped). No
589	+// migration note is prepended — this is index content, not a resumed transcript.
590	+func flattenBody(data *schema.SessionData) string {
591	+	turns := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, "")
592	+	var b strings.Builder
593	+	for _, t := range turns {
594	+		if t.Text == "" {
595	+			continue
596	+		}
597	+		if b.Len() > 0 {
598	+			b.WriteString("\n\n")
599	+		}
600	+		b.WriteString(t.Text)
601	+	}
602	+	return b.String()
603	+}
604	+
605	+// countTurns returns (user prompts, all messages) across the session's exchanges.
606	+func countTurns(data *schema.SessionData) (userTurns, totalTurns int) {
607	+	for _, ex := range data.Exchanges {
608	+		for _, m := range ex.Messages {
609	+			totalTurns++
610	+			if m.Role == schema.RoleUser {
611	+				userTurns++
612	+			}
613	+		}
614	+	}
615	+	return userTurns, totalTurns
616	+}
617	+
618	+// lastTimestamp returns the last non-empty message timestamp in the session, or "".
619	+func lastTimestamp(data *schema.SessionData) string {
620	+	last := ""
621	+	for _, ex := range data.Exchanges {
622	+		for _, m := range ex.Messages {
623	+			if m.Timestamp != "" {
624	+				last = m.Timestamp
625	+			}
626	+		}
627	+	}
628	+	return last
629	+}
630	+
631	+// statNative returns the native file's size (bytes) and mtime (epoch ms), or zeros.
632	+func statNative(path string) (size, mtimeMs int64) {
633	+	info, err := os.Stat(path)
634	+	if err != nil {
635	+		return 0, 0
636	+	}
637	+	return info.Size(), info.ModTime().UnixMilli()
638	+}
639	+
640	+// ---- project identity cache (cwd -> project id/name) ----
641	+
642	+type projectIDName struct{ id, name string }
643	+
644	+// projectIDCache memoizes ComputeProjectID by cwd, since many sessions share a cwd and
645	+// each resolution walks the filesystem to the git root.
646	+type projectIDCache struct {
647	+	mu sync.Mutex
648	+	m  map[string]projectIDName
649	+}
650	+
651	+func (c *projectIDCache) resolve(cwd string) (string, string) {
652	+	if strings.TrimSpace(cwd) == "" {
653	+		return unknownProjectID, ""
654	+	}
655	+	c.mu.Lock()
656	+	defer c.mu.Unlock()
657	+	if v, ok := c.m[cwd]; ok {
658	+		return v.id, v.name
659	+	}
660	+	id, name, err := utils.ComputeProjectID(cwd)
661	+	if err != nil || id == "" {
662	+		id, name = unknownProjectID, ""
663	+	}
664	+	c.m[cwd] = projectIDName{id, name}
665	+	return id, name
666	+}
667	+
668	+// ---- helpers ----
669	+
670	+// summarizeCounts renders "claude 683 · codex 70 · …" in registry order, omitting zeros.
671	+func summarizeCounts(ids []string, totals map[string]int) string {
672	+	var parts []string
673	+	for _, id := range ids {
674	+		if totals[id] > 0 {
675	+			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("%s %d", id, totals[id]))
676	+		}
677	+	}
678	+	return strings.Join(parts, " · ")
679	+}
680	+
681	+// ---- live progress (per-agent bars) ----
682	+
683	+// reindexProgress renders live per-agent progress. Workers bump atomic per-agent
684	+// counters and observe project ids; a single render goroutine redraws the block in
685	+// place on a TTY (ANSI cursor-up), or prints periodic plain lines otherwise. No worker
686	+// draws directly. See docs/SESSIONS-DB.md "Reindex Progress UX".
687	+type reindexProgress struct {
688	+	agents []string          // agents with >0 sessions, in registry order
689	+	totals map[string]int    // per-agent session totals
690	+	done   map[string]*int64 // per-agent completed counters (atomic)
691	+	tty    bool
692	+
693	+	mu       sync.Mutex
694	+	projects map[string]struct{} // distinct non-unknown project ids seen
695	+
696	+	stopCh     chan struct{}
697	+	doneCh     chan struct{}
698	+	linesDrawn int
699	+}
700	+
701	+func newReindexProgress(ids []string, totals map[string]int) *reindexProgress {
702	+	done := make(map[string]*int64)
703	+	var agents []string
704	+	for _, id := range ids {
705	+		if totals[id] > 0 {
706	+			agents = append(agents, id)
707	+			done[id] = new(int64)
708	+		}
709	+	}
710	+	return &reindexProgress{
711	+		agents:   agents,
712	+		totals:   totals,
713	+		done:     done,
714	+		tty:      isTerminal(os.Stderr),
715	+		projects: map[string]struct{}{},
716	+		stopCh:   make(chan struct{}),
717	+		doneCh:   make(chan struct{}),
718	+	}
719	+}
720	+
721	+func (p *reindexProgress) begin() {
722	+	go p.loop()
723	+}
724	+
725	+func (p *reindexProgress) end() {
726	+	close(p.stopCh)
727	+	<-p.doneCh
728	+	p.draw(true) // final frame at 100%
729	+}
730	+
731	+func (p *reindexProgress) inc(agent string) {
732	+	if c := p.done[agent]; c != nil {
733	+		atomic.AddInt64(c, 1)
734	+	}
735	+}
736	+
737	+func (p *reindexProgress) observe(projectID string) {
738	+	// Unknown-project sessions are not counted toward the live "projects" total (the
739	+	// final unattributed count comes from the DB at summary time).
740	+	if projectID == unknownProjectID {
741	+		return
742	+	}
743	+	p.mu.Lock()
744	+	p.projects[projectID] = struct{}{}
745	+	p.mu.Unlock()
746	+}
747	+
748	+func (p *reindexProgress) projectCount() int {
749	+	p.mu.Lock()
750	+	defer p.mu.Unlock()
751	+	return len(p.projects)
752	+}
753	+
754	+func (p *reindexProgress) totalDone() int {
755	+	var n int64
756	+	for _, c := range p.done {
757	+		n += atomic.LoadInt64(c)
758	+	}
759	+	return int(n)
760	+}
761	+
762	+func (p *reindexProgress) grandTotal() int {
763	+	n := 0
764	+	for _, a := range p.agents {
765	+		n += p.totals[a]
766	+	}
767	+	return n
768	+}
769	+
770	+func (p *reindexProgress) loop() {
771	+	defer close(p.doneCh)
772	+	interval := 100 * time.Millisecond
773	+	if !p.tty {
774	+		interval = 1500 * time.Millisecond
775	+	}
776	+	ticker := time.NewTicker(interval)
777	+	defer ticker.Stop()
778	+	for {
779	+		select {
780	+		case <-p.stopCh:
781	+			return
782	+		case <-ticker.C:
783	+			p.draw(false)
784	+		}
785	+	}
786	+}
787	+
788	+func (p *reindexProgress) draw(final bool) {
789	+	if !p.tty {
790	+		if !final { // the final state is covered by the summary line
791	+			fprintf(os.Stderr, "indexed %d/%d…\n", p.totalDone(), p.grandTotal())
792	+		}
793	+		return
794	+	}
795	+
796	+	lines := p.frameLines()
797	+	var b strings.Builder
798	+	if p.linesDrawn > 0 {
799	+		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "\033[%dA", p.linesDrawn) // move cursor up to the block's top
800	+	}
801	+	for _, ln := range lines {
802	+		b.WriteString("\033[2K") // clear the line, then redraw it
803	+		b.WriteString(ln)
804	+		b.WriteString("\n")
805	+	}
806	+	fprint(os.Stderr, b.String())
807	+	p.linesDrawn = len(lines)
808	+}
809	+
810	+func (p *reindexProgress) frameLines() []string {
811	+	doneN, totalN := p.totalDone(), p.grandTotal()
812	+	lines := []string{
813	+		fmt.Sprintf("Indexing  %d/%d · %d projects", doneN, totalN, p.projectCount()),
814	+		"",
815	+	}
816	+
817	+	nameW := 0
818	+	for _, a := range p.agents {
819	+		if len(a) > nameW {
820	+			nameW = len(a)
821	+		}
822	+	}
823	+	for _, a := range p.agents {
824	+		d := atomic.LoadInt64(p.done[a])
825	+		t := int64(p.totals[a])
826	+		check := ""
827	+		if d >= t {
828	+			check = " ✓"
829	+		}
830	+		lines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf("  %-*s %s %d/%d%s", nameW, a, progressBar(d, t), d, t, check))
831	+	}
832	+	return lines
833	+}
834	+
835	+// progressBar renders a fixed-width unicode bar, e.g. ▕███████░░░░░░░▏.
836	+func progressBar(done, total int64) string {
837	+	const w = 14
838	+	filled := 0
839	+	if total > 0 {
840	+		filled = int(done * int64(w) / total)
841	+	}
842	+	if filled > w {
843	+		filled = w
844	+	}
845	+	return "▕" + strings.Repeat("█", filled) + strings.Repeat("░", w-filled) + "▏"
846	+}
847	+
848	+// isTerminal reports whether f is a character device (an interactive terminal),
849	+// using only the standard library (no isatty dependency).
850	+func isTerminal(f *os.File) bool {
851	+	info, err := f.Stat()
852	+	if err != nil {
853	+		return false
854	+	}
855	+	return info.Mode()&os.ModeCharDevice != 0
856	+}
857	+
858	+// ---- scan progress (live "Scanning agents…" line) ----
859	+
860	+// scanSpinner is the braille spinner cycled while enumerating.
861	+var scanSpinner = []string{"⠋", "⠙", "⠹", "⠸", "⠼", "⠴", "⠦", "⠧", "⠇", "⠏"}
862	+
863	+// scanAgent holds one provider's live enumeration counter.
864	+type scanAgent struct {
865	+	id string
866	+	r  *spi.ScanReporter
867	+}
868	+
869	+// scanProgress renders one live line while enumerateAll scans providers in parallel:
870	+//
871	+//	🔍  Scanning agents…  ⠹  codex 18420 · claude 3195 · cursor 10
872	+//
873	+// Codex and Claude (the long poles) report sessions-found via spi.ProgressEnumerator; every
874	+// provider snaps to its final count via markDone when it finishes. Counts are sessions, not raw
875	+// files (warmup/sidechain transcripts are excluded). On a non-TTY it prints periodic plain lines.
876	+// A nil *scanProgress is fully silent — the background warm passes nil.
877	+type scanProgress struct {
878	+	agents []*scanAgent
879	+	byID   map[string]*scanAgent
880	+	tty    bool
881	+	spin   int
882	+
883	+	stopCh     chan struct{}
884	+	doneCh     chan struct{}
885	+	linesDrawn int
886	+}
887	+
888	+func newScanProgress(ids []string) *scanProgress {
889	+	agents := make([]*scanAgent, 0, len(ids))
890	+	byID := make(map[string]*scanAgent, len(ids))
891	+	for _, id := range ids {
892	+		a := &scanAgent{id: id, r: &spi.ScanReporter{}}
893	+		agents = append(agents, a)
894	+		byID[id] = a
895	+	}
896	+	return &scanProgress{
897	+		agents: agents,
898	+		byID:   byID,
899	+		tty:    isTerminal(os.Stderr),
900	+		stopCh: make(chan struct{}),
901	+		doneCh: make(chan struct{}),
902	+	}
903	+}
904	+
905	+// reporterFor returns a provider's live reporter (nil-safe: a nil scanProgress yields nil, which
906	+// providers treat as "do not report").
907	+func (s *scanProgress) reporterFor(id string) *spi.ScanReporter {
908	+	if s == nil {
909	+		return nil
910	+	}
911	+	if a := s.byID[id]; a != nil {
912	+		return a.r
913	+	}
914	+	return nil
915	+}
916	+
917	+// markDone reconciles a provider's running count to its final session count — a no-op for the
918	+// reporting providers (Codex/Claude), and the only count for providers that never report
919	+// (Cursor/Gemini/etc., which appear the moment they finish).
920	+func (s *scanProgress) markDone(id string, final int) {
921	+	if s == nil {
922	+		return
923	+	}
924	+	if a := s.byID[id]; a != nil {
925	+		if delta := final - int(a.r.Found()); delta != 0 {
926	+			a.r.Add(delta)
927	+		}
928	+	}
929	+}
930	+
931	+func (s *scanProgress) begin() {
932	+	if s == nil {
933	+		return
934	+	}
935	+	if !s.tty {
936	+		fprintln(os.Stderr, "\n🔍  Scanning agents…")
937	+	} else {
938	+		fprintln(os.Stderr) // blank line above the live line
939	+		s.draw()            // show immediately, before the first tick
940	+	}
941	+	go s.loop()
942	+}
943	+
944	+func (s *scanProgress) end() {
945	+	if s == nil {
946	+		return
947	+	}
948	+	close(s.stopCh)
949	+	<-s.doneCh
950	+	if s.tty && s.linesDrawn > 0 {
951	+		// Erase the live line so the caller's "✓ Found…" line takes its place.
952	+		fprint(os.Stderr, "\033[1A\033[2K")
953	+	}
954	+}
955	+
956	+func (s *scanProgress) loop() {
957	+	defer close(s.doneCh)
958	+	interval := 100 * time.Millisecond
959	+	if !s.tty {
960	+		interval = 1500 * time.Millisecond
961	+	}
962	+	t := time.NewTicker(interval)
963	+	defer t.Stop()
964	+	for {
965	+		select {
966	+		case <-s.stopCh:
967	+			return
968	+		case <-t.C:
969	+			s.spin++
970	+			if s.tty {
971	+				s.draw()
972	+			} else {
973	+				fprintf(os.Stderr, "    scanning… %s\n", s.counts())
974	+			}
975	+		}
976	+	}
977	+}
978	+
979	+func (s *scanProgress) draw() {
980	+	var b strings.Builder
981	+	if s.linesDrawn > 0 {
982	+		b.WriteString("\033[1A") // up to the live line
983	+	}
984	+	b.WriteString("\033[2K") // clear it, then redraw
985	+	b.WriteString(s.line())
986	+	b.WriteString("\n")
987	+	fprint(os.Stderr, b.String())
988	+	s.linesDrawn = 1
989	+}
990	+
991	+func (s *scanProgress) line() string {
992	+	return fmt.Sprintf("🔍  Scanning agents…  %s  %s", scanSpinner[s.spin%len(scanSpinner)], s.counts())
993	+}
994	+
995	+func (s *scanProgress) counts() string {
996	+	var parts []string
997	+	for _, a := range s.agents {
998	+		found := a.r.Found()
999	+		if found == 0 {
1000	+			continue // not started, or finished with no sessions
1001	+		}
1002	+		// Plain integers (no thousands separators), matching the "Found …" summary line.
1003	+		parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("%s %d", a.id, found))
1004	+	}
1005	+	if len(parts) == 0 {
1006	+		return "…"
1007	+	}
1008	+	return strings.Join(parts, " · ")
1009	+}
1010	+
1011	+// ---- summary ----
1012	+
1013	+func printReindexSummary(ids []string, perAgent map[string]int, dbPath string, elapsed time.Duration, indexed, unchanged, projects, unattributed int) {
1014	+	w := os.Stderr
1015	+	if unchanged > 0 {
1016	+		fprintf(w, "\n✓   Indexed %d sessions (%d unchanged) into %s  (%.1fs)\n\n", indexed, unchanged, prettyPath(dbPath), elapsed.Seconds())
1017	+	} else {
1018	+		fprintf(w, "\n✓   Indexed %d sessions into %s  (%.1fs)\n\n", indexed, prettyPath(dbPath), elapsed.Seconds())
1019	+	}
1020	+	fprintf(w, "      %s\n\n", summarizeCounts(ids, perAgent))
1021	+	if unattributed > 0 {
1022	+		fprintf(w, "      %d projects  ·  %d unattributed\n", projects, unattributed)
1023	+	} else {
1024	+		fprintf(w, "      %d projects\n", projects)
1025	+	}
1026	+	fprintf(w, "\n")
1027	+}
1028	+
1029	+// prettyPath abbreviates the user's home directory to ~ for display.
1030	+func prettyPath(p string) string {
1031	+	if home, err := os.UserHomeDir(); err == nil && strings.HasPrefix(p, home) {
1032	+		return "~" + p[len(home):]
1033	+	}
1034	+	return p
1035	+}

63	+// CreateResumeCommand builds the `specstory resume` command. It opens the interactive
64	+// picker (a Bubble Tea TUI over the sessions.db index — see docs/RESUME-TUI.md): browse
65	+// the current project's sessions across all agents, pick one, choose a target agent, then
66	+// reconstruct (cross-agent) or native-resume (same agent) and launch with auto-save.
67	+func CreateResumeCommand(cloudURL *string, localTimeZone bool, debugDir string) *cobra.Command {
68	+	longDesc := `Resume a past coding-agent session — in the same agent, or a different one.
69	+
70	+'resume' opens an interactive picker of the sessions in the current project across all
71	+agents. Pick one, choose which installed agent to continue it in, and go. Resuming into a
72	+different agent reconstructs the conversation into that agent's native format first.
73	+
74	+Pass an agent to set the resume target up front, e.g. 'specstory resume codex' — the picker
75	+then skips the target-selection step and resumes straight into that agent. The agent must be
76	+a known, installed provider, or the command errors.`
77	+
78	+	resumeCmd := &cobra.Command{
79	+		Use:   "resume [agent]",
80	+		Short: "Resume a past session, optionally in a different agent",
81	+		Long:  longDesc,
82	+		Args:  cobra.MaximumNArgs(1),
83	+		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
84	+			config.EnsureDefaultProjectConfig()
85	+			slog.Info("Running in resume mode")
86	+
87	+			registry := factory.GetRegistry()
88	+
89	+			// Optional positional arg pre-selects the target agent (e.g. `resume codex`).
90	+			// An unknown agent name should fail the run rather than be silently ignored, so
91	+			// validate it against the registry up front. When set and valid, the picker skips
92	+			// the target-selection step entirely (see selectResumeViaTUI / beginResume); whether
93	+			// the named agent is actually installed is checked there, where the installed set
94	+			// is known.
95	+			presetTarget := ""
96	+			if len(args) == 1 {
97	+				presetTarget = strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(args[0]))
98	+				if _, err := registry.Get(presetTarget); err != nil {
99	+					return utils.ValidationError{Message: fmt.Sprintf(
100	+						"unknown agent %q. Valid agents: %s", presetTarget, registry.GetProviderList())}
101	+				}
102	+			}
103	+
104	+			// Read flags (subset mirroring run/watch).
105	+			debugRaw, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("debug-raw")
106	+			useLocalTimezone, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("local-time-zone")
107	+			useUTC := !useLocalTimezone
108	+			outputDir, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("output-dir")
109	+			flagDebugDir, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("debug-dir")
110	+			noCloudSync, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-cloud-sync")
111	+			onlyCloudSync, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("only-cloud-sync")
112	+			provenanceEnabled, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("provenance")
113	+			noTelemetryPrompts, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-telemetry-prompts")
114	+
115	+			if flagDebugDir != "" {
116	+				spi.SetDebugBaseDir(flagDebugDir)
117	+			}
118	+
119	+			cwd, err := os.Getwd()
120	+			if err != nil {
121	+				slog.Error("Failed to get current working directory", "error", err)
122	+				return err
123	+			}
124	+
125	+			// Open (building if needed) the session index and run the interactive picker.
126	+			// builtFresh = the index was just rebuilt in the foreground, so the picker skips
127	+			// the background index warm (it would be redundant).
128	+			store, builtFresh, err := openOrBuildResumeIndex()
129	+			if err != nil {
130	+				return err
131	+			}
132	+			defer func() { _ = store.Close() }()
133	+
134	+			projectID, projectName, idErr := utils.ComputeProjectID(cwd)
135	+			if idErr != nil || projectID == "" {
136	+				projectID, projectName = unknownProjectID, filepath.Base(cwd)
137	+			}

139	+			plan, err := selectResumeViaTUI(registry, store, projectID, projectName, presetTarget, builtFresh)
140	+			if err != nil {
141	+				return err
142	+			}

146	+			}
147	+

178	+// `search` (whose `r` action resumes a found session through the same path).

190	+// launchResume reconstructs (cross-agent) or natively resumes the planned session and
191	+// runs the agent with auto-save + provenance — the shared tail of `resume` and `search`.
192	+func launchResume(plan *resumePlan, cwd string, o resumeLaunchOpts) error {
193	+	// Setup output configuration and project identity (needed for auto-save + cloud).
194	+	outConfig, err := utils.SetupOutputConfig(o.outputDir, o.flagDebugDir)
195	+	if err != nil {
196	+		return err
197	+	}
198	+	if err := utils.EnsureHistoryDirectoryExists(outConfig); err != nil {
199	+		return err
200	+	}
201	+	if _, err := utils.NewProjectIdentityManager(cwd).EnsureProjectIdentity(); err != nil {
202	+		slog.Error("Failed to ensure project identity", "error", err)
203	+	}
204	+
205	+	CheckAndWarnAuthentication(o.noCloudSync)
206	+	if o.onlyCloudSync && !cloud.IsAuthenticated() {
207	+		return utils.ValidationError{Message: "--only-cloud-sync requires authentication. Please run 'specstory login' first"}
208	+	}
209	+
210	+	analytics.SetAgentProviders([]string{plan.to.Name()})
211	+	analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventResumeActivated, analytics.Properties{
212	+		"from_provider": plan.fromID,
213	+		"to_provider":   plan.toID,
214	+		"cross_agent":   plan.fromID != plan.toID,
215	+	})
216	+
217	+	resumeSessionID, err := prepareResumeTarget(plan, cwd, os.Stdout)
218	+	if err != nil {
219	+		return err
220	+	}
221	+
222	+	// Context for graceful cancellation (Ctrl+C).
223	+	ctx, cancel := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), os.Interrupt, syscall.SIGTERM)
224	+	defer cancel()
225	+
226	+	// Provenance infrastructure (mirrors run/watch).
227	+	provenanceEngine, provenanceCleanup, err := provenance.StartEngine(o.provenanceEnabled)
228	+	if err != nil {
229	+		return err
230	+	}
231	+	defer provenanceCleanup()
232	+	fsCleanup, err := provenance.StartFSWatcher(ctx, provenanceEngine, cwd)
233	+	if err != nil {
234	+		return err
235	+	}
236	+	defer fsCleanup()
237	+
238	+	// Keep sessions.db current in real time alongside the markdown writes (nil/no-op if the
239	+	// index can't be opened — never block the resumed agent on it).
240	+	liveIndex := NewLiveIndexer(cwd)
241	+	defer liveIndex.Close()
242	+
243	+	// Auto-save callback: identical behavior to run/watch.
244	+	sessionCallback := func(sess *spi.AgentChatSession) {
245	+		if sess == nil {
246	+			return
247	+		}
248	+		_, perr := session.ProcessSingleSession(context.Background(), sess, outConfig, session.ProcessingOptions{
249	+			OnlyCloudSync:      o.onlyCloudSync,
250	+			IsAutosave:         true,
251	+			DebugRaw:           o.debugRaw,
252	+			UseUTC:             o.useUTC,
253	+			NoTelemetryPrompts: o.noTelemetryPrompts,
254	+		})
255	+		if perr != nil {
256	+			slog.Error("Failed to process session update", "sessionId", sess.SessionID, "error", perr)
257	+		}
258	+		// Mirror the markdown write into the restore index (agent we resumed INTO).
259	+		liveIndex.Record(plan.toID, sess)
260	+		provenance.ProcessEvents(ctx, provenanceEngine, sess)
261	+	}
262	+
263	+	slog.Info("Launching resume", "provider", plan.to.Name(), "resumeSessionID", resumeSessionID)
264	+	if err := plan.to.ExecAgentAndWatch(cwd, "", resumeSessionID, o.debugRaw, sessionCallback); err != nil {
265	+		slog.Error("Agent resume failed", "provider", plan.to.Name(), "error", err)
266	+		return err
267	+	}
268	+	return nil
269	+}
270	+
271	+// prepareResumeTarget produces the native session ID to hand to ExecAgentAndWatch.
272	+// Same-agent resume reuses the existing session; cross-agent reconstructs the
273	+// source SessionData into the target's native store and returns the fresh ID.
274	+func prepareResumeTarget(plan *resumePlan, cwd string, out io.Writer) (string, error) {
275	+	// Same agent: native resume on the existing session, no transform.
276	+	if plan.fromID == plan.toID {
277	+		fprintf(out, "\nResuming %s session %s in place...\n", plan.from.Name(), shortID(plan.sessionID))
278	+		return plan.sessionID, nil
279	+	}
280	+
281	+	// Cross-agent: load source SessionData, reconstruct, write into target store.
282	+	// The source must be read from the directory it was originally launched in, not the
283	+	// user's current cwd — a session picked from another project (all-projects browser)
284	+	// lives elsewhere, and providers like Codex locate it by its recorded cwd. The
285	+	// reconstructed session is still written and launched under the current cwd below.
286	+	fromCwd := plan.fromCwd
287	+	if fromCwd == "" {
288	+		fromCwd = cwd
289	+	}
290	+	fromSession, err := plan.from.GetAgentChatSession(fromCwd, plan.sessionID, false)
291	+	if err != nil {
292	+		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to load source session: %w", err)
293	+	}
294	+	if fromSession == nil || fromSession.SessionData == nil {
295	+		return "", fmt.Errorf("source session %s has no data to reconstruct", plan.sessionID)
296	+	}
297	+
298	+	note := fmt.Sprintf("Resumed from a %s session via SpecStory.", plan.from.Name())
299	+	rec, err := plan.to.ReconstructSession(fromSession.SessionData, spi.ReconstructOptions{
300	+		WorkspaceRoot: cwd,
301	+		MigrationNote: note,
302	+	})
303	+	if err != nil {
304	+		if errors.Is(err, spi.ErrReconstructionUnsupported) {
305	+			return "", utils.ValidationError{Message: fmt.Sprintf(
306	+				"%s can't yet be a cross-agent resume target. Choose Claude Code or Codex CLI (or resume in %s itself).",
307	+				plan.to.Name(), plan.from.Name())}
308	+		}
309	+		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to reconstruct session for %s: %w", plan.to.Name(), err)
310	+	}
311	+
312	+	path, err := plan.to.NativeSessionPath(cwd, rec.Filename)
313	+	if err != nil {
314	+		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to resolve target session path: %w", err)
315	+	}
316	+	if err := writeReconstructedSession(path, rec.Content); err != nil {
317	+		return "", err
318	+	}
319	+	// The agent runs in a separate process and resolves the session by reading this file the
320	+	// instant it starts. On a filesystem without strong close-to-open coherence the freshly
321	+	// written file can lag, so confirm it is actually readable before handing the agent a
322	+	// --resume it would otherwise reject as "session not found".
323	+	if err := waitForSessionFileVisible(path, sessionFileVisibleTimeout); err != nil {
324	+		return "", fmt.Errorf("reconstructed %s session is not ready to resume: %w", plan.to.Name(), err)
325	+	}
326	+
327	+	slog.Info("resume: wrote reconstructed session", "path", path, "newID", rec.SessionID)
328	+	fprintf(out, "\nReconstructed %s session into %s as %s.\n", plan.from.Name(), plan.to.Name(), shortID(rec.SessionID))
329	+	return rec.SessionID, nil
330	+}
331	+
332	+// shortID abbreviates a session ID for display.
333	+func shortID(id string) string {
334	+	if len(id) <= 13 {
335	+		return id
336	+	}
337	+	return id[:5] + "..." + id[len(id)-5:]
338	+}
339	+
340	+// sessionFileVisibleTimeout bounds how long resume waits for a just-written reconstructed
341	+// session file to become readable to other processes before launching the agent. It is
342	+// generous enough to absorb the propagation lag of a weak-coherence filesystem (network or
343	+// roaming home, cloud-synced folder, VM/container shared mount) yet short enough not to hang
344	+// an interactive command — on a local disk the very first check passes, so it never waits.
345	+const sessionFileVisibleTimeout = 10 * time.Second
346	+
347	+// writeReconstructedSession durably and atomically writes a reconstructed session file. A
348	+// plain os.WriteFile leaves the data in the page cache and the new directory entry possibly
349	+// non-durable; on a filesystem without strong close-to-open coherence the separately-spawned
350	+// agent process can then fail to see a (large) file and report the session as missing. To
351	+// avoid that, the content is written to a temp file in the SAME directory (so the rename is
352	+// atomic rather than cross-device), fsync'd, renamed into place, and the parent directory is
353	+// fsync'd so the entry itself is durable.
354	+func writeReconstructedSession(path string, content []byte) (err error) {
355	+	dir := filepath.Dir(path)
356	+	if mkErr := os.MkdirAll(dir, 0o755); mkErr != nil {
357	+		return fmt.Errorf("failed to create target session directory: %w", mkErr)
358	+	}
359	+
360	+	tmp, err := os.CreateTemp(dir, ".resume-*.tmp")
361	+	if err != nil {
362	+		return fmt.Errorf("failed to create temp session file: %w", err)
363	+	}
364	+	tmpPath := tmp.Name()
365	+	// Best-effort cleanup of the temp file on any early return; a no-op once it is renamed away.
366	+	defer func() { _ = os.Remove(tmpPath) }()
367	+
368	+	// Preserve the world-readable mode the previous os.WriteFile(…, 0o644) produced
369	+	// (CreateTemp makes the file 0o600).
370	+	if chErr := tmp.Chmod(0o644); chErr != nil {
371	+		_ = tmp.Close()
372	+		return fmt.Errorf("failed to set session file mode: %w", chErr)
373	+	}
374	+	if _, wErr := tmp.Write(content); wErr != nil {
375	+		_ = tmp.Close()
376	+		return fmt.Errorf("failed to write temp session file: %w", wErr)
377	+	}
378	+	// Flush the bytes to the backing store before the rename so a reader cannot observe a
379	+	// renamed-but-empty file.
380	+	if syncErr := tmp.Sync(); syncErr != nil {
381	+		_ = tmp.Close()
382	+		return fmt.Errorf("failed to flush temp session file: %w", syncErr)
383	+	}
384	+	if closeErr := tmp.Close(); closeErr != nil {
385	+		return fmt.Errorf("failed to close temp session file: %w", closeErr)
386	+	}
387	+
388	+	if rnErr := os.Rename(tmpPath, path); rnErr != nil {
389	+		return fmt.Errorf("failed to move session file into place: %w", rnErr)
390	+	}
391	+
392	+	// Fsync the directory so the new entry is durable and propagates. Best-effort: not all
393	+	// platforms permit fsync on a directory handle, and it is a negligible cost on local disks.
394	+	if d, openErr := os.Open(dir); openErr == nil {
395	+		_ = d.Sync()
396	+		_ = d.Close()
397	+	}
398	+	return nil
399	+}
400	+
401	+// waitForSessionFileVisible polls until path is readable — present AND with at least its
402	+// first line available, since on a weak-coherence filesystem a stat can succeed before the
403	+// content propagates — or the timeout elapses, in which case it returns a diagnostic error
404	+// so the caller can fail clearly rather than launch a --resume the agent would reject.
405	+func waitForSessionFileVisible(path string, timeout time.Duration) error {
406	+	deadline := time.Now().Add(timeout)
407	+	for {
408	+		readable, checkErr := sessionFileReadable(path)
409	+		if readable {
410	+			return nil
411	+		}
412	+		if time.Now().After(deadline) {
413	+			return fmt.Errorf("session file %q did not become readable within %s "+
414	+				"(the target filesystem may lack close-to-open coherence): %w", path, timeout, checkErr)
415	+		}
416	+		time.Sleep(50 * time.Millisecond)
417	+	}
418	+}
419	+
420	+// sessionFileReadable reports whether path exists and at least its first line can be read.
421	+// A reconstructed session always has at least one record, so an empty read means the content
422	+// has not propagated yet even though the directory entry exists.
423	+func sessionFileReadable(path string) (bool, error) {
424	+	f, err := os.Open(path)
425	+	if err != nil {
426	+		return false, err
427	+	}
428	+	defer func() { _ = f.Close() }()
429	+
430	+	line, err := bufio.NewReader(f).ReadString('\n')
431	+	if len(line) > 0 {
432	+		return true, nil
433	+	}
434	+	if err == io.EOF || err == nil {
435	+		return false, fmt.Errorf("session file is empty")
436	+	}
437	+	return false, err
438	+}

460	+func TestFTSQuery(t *testing.T) {
461	+	cases := []struct{ name, in, want string }{
462	+		{"empty", "", ""},
463	+		{"single word is a prefix", "thank", "thank*"},
464	+		{"bare words are independent prefixes", "thank you", "thank* you*"},
465	+		{"punctuation in a bare word splits into an adjacency phrase", "max-cpu!", "max + cpu*"},
466	+		{"a bare filename is an adjacency phrase with prefix last", "poem.txt", "poem + txt*"},
467	+		{"a quoted filename is a committed phrase, no prefix", `"poem.txt"`, `"poem txt"`},
468	+		{"an open-quoted filename keeps the last token a prefix", `"poem.txt`, "poem + txt*"},
469	+		{"closed phrase is exact adjacency", `"thank you"`, `"thank you"`},
470	+		{"closed single-word phrase has no prefix", `"thank"`, `"thank"`},
471	+		{"open phrase keeps last word a prefix", `"thank yo`, "thank + yo*"},
472	+		{"open single-word phrase is a prefix", `"thank`, "thank*"},
473	+		{"phrase plus trailing bare word", `"thank you" now`, `"thank you" now*`},
474	+		{"bare word before a closed phrase", `please "thank you"`, `please* "thank you"`},
475	+		{"whitespace inside a phrase is collapsed", `"thank    you"`, `"thank you"`},
476	+	}
477	+	for _, c := range cases {
478	+		t.Run(c.name, func(t *testing.T) {
479	+			if got := ftsQuery(c.in); got != c.want {
480	+				t.Errorf("ftsQuery(%q) = %q, want %q", c.in, got, c.want)
481	+			}
482	+		})
483	+	}
484	+}
485	+
486	+func TestQueryReady(t *testing.T) {
487	+	cases := []struct {
488	+		in   string
489	+		want bool
490	+	}{
491	+		{"", false},
492	+		{"a", false},   // one alnum char is below minQueryLen
493	+		{`"a"`, false}, // quotes don't count toward the threshold
494	+		{"ab", true},   // two alnum chars
495	+		{"a!", false},  // punctuation doesn't count
496	+		{"a b", true},  // two alnum chars across words
497	+		{`"hi"`, true}, // alnum inside quotes counts
498	+		{"日本", true},   // letters in other scripts count
499	+	}
500	+	for _, c := range cases {
501	+		if got := queryReady(c.in); got != c.want {
502	+			t.Errorf("queryReady(%q) = %v, want %v", c.in, got, c.want)
503	+		}
504	+	}
505	+}
506	+
507	+func TestShortID(t *testing.T) {
508	+	cases := []struct{ in, want string }{
509	+		{"short", "short"},
510	+		{"1234567890abc", "1234567890abc"}, // <= 13 chars: unchanged
511	+		{"1234567890abcdef", "12345...bcdef"},
512	+	}
513	+	for _, c := range cases {
514	+		if got := shortID(c.in); got != c.want {
515	+			t.Errorf("shortID(%q) = %q, want %q", c.in, got, c.want)
516	+		}
517	+	}
518	+}

520	+func TestWriteReconstructedSession(t *testing.T) {
521	+	dir := t.TempDir()
522	+	path := filepath.Join(dir, "sub", "session.jsonl")
523	+	content := []byte(`{"type":"user"}` + "\n")
524	+
525	+	if err := writeReconstructedSession(path, content); err != nil {
526	+		t.Fatalf("writeReconstructedSession: %v", err)
527	+	}
528	+
529	+	got, err := os.ReadFile(path)
530	+	if err != nil {
531	+		t.Fatalf("reading written file: %v", err)
532	+	}
533	+	if string(got) != string(content) {
534	+		t.Errorf("content = %q, want %q", got, content)
535	+	}
536	+
537	+	// The atomic-write temp file must not be left behind in the target directory.
538	+	entries, err := os.ReadDir(filepath.Dir(path))
539	+	if err != nil {
540	+		t.Fatal(err)
541	+	}
542	+	for _, e := range entries {
543	+		if e.Name() != "session.jsonl" {
544	+			t.Errorf("unexpected leftover file in target dir: %q", e.Name())
545	+		}
546	+	}
547	+}

549	+func TestSessionFileReadable(t *testing.T) {
550	+	dir := t.TempDir()
551	+
552	+	missing := filepath.Join(dir, "nope.jsonl")
553	+	if ok, _ := sessionFileReadable(missing); ok {
554	+		t.Error("missing file reported readable")
555	+	}
556	+
557	+	empty := filepath.Join(dir, "empty.jsonl")
558	+	if err := os.WriteFile(empty, nil, 0o644); err != nil {
559	+		t.Fatal(err)
560	+	}
561	+	if ok, _ := sessionFileReadable(empty); ok {
562	+		t.Error("empty file reported readable")
563	+	}
564	+
565	+	full := filepath.Join(dir, "full.jsonl")
566	+	if err := os.WriteFile(full, []byte(`{"type":"user"}`+"\n"), 0o644); err != nil {
567	+		t.Fatal(err)
568	+	}
569	+	if ok, err := sessionFileReadable(full); !ok {
570	+		t.Errorf("file with content reported not readable: %v", err)
571	+	}
572	+}

574	+func TestWaitForSessionFileVisible(t *testing.T) {
575	+	dir := t.TempDir()
576	+
577	+	present := filepath.Join(dir, "present.jsonl")
578	+	if err := os.WriteFile(present, []byte(`{"type":"user"}`+"\n"), 0o644); err != nil {
579	+		t.Fatal(err)
580	+	}
581	+	if err := waitForSessionFileVisible(present, time.Second); err != nil {
582	+		t.Errorf("expected an already-present file to be visible: %v", err)
583	+	}
584	+
585	+	// A file that never appears must time out with a diagnostic error rather than block forever.
586	+	if err := waitForSessionFileVisible(filepath.Join(dir, "never.jsonl"), 100*time.Millisecond); err == nil {
587	+		t.Error("expected timeout error for a file that never becomes visible")
588	+	}
589	+}

591	+// TestBeginResumeWithPresetSkipsTargetStep verifies the `resume <agent>` contract: when a
592	+// target agent was pre-selected, choosing a session resumes immediately into that agent
593	+// rather than prompting for a target.
594	+func TestBeginResumeWithPresetSkipsTargetStep(t *testing.T) {
595	+	sess := &sessionindex.Session{SessionID: "s1", Agent: "codex"}
596	+	m := sessionTUI{presetTo: "claude"}
597	+
598	+	next, cmd := m.beginResume(sess)
599	+	rm := next.(sessionTUI)
600	+
601	+	if cmd == nil {
602	+		t.Error("expected an immediate quit command when a target is preset")
603	+	}
604	+	if rm.mode == modeTarget {
605	+		t.Error("a preset target must skip the target-selection step")
606	+	}
607	+	if rm.result.session != sess {
608	+		t.Errorf("result session = %v, want the chosen session", rm.result.session)
609	+	}
610	+	if rm.result.targetID != "claude" {
611	+		t.Errorf("result target = %q, want %q", rm.result.targetID, "claude")
612	+	}
613	+}

616	+// choosing a session advances to the target-selection step (no immediate resume).
617	+func TestBeginResumeWithoutPresetEntersTargetStep(t *testing.T) {
618	+	sess := &sessionindex.Session{SessionID: "s1", Agent: "codex"}

626	+	}

629	+	}
630	+	if rm.chosen != sess {
631	+		t.Error("chosen session must be recorded before target selection")
632	+	}
633	+}

678	+// TestPrepareResumeTargetLoadsSourceFromOriginCwd guards the cross-project resume fix:
679	+// the source session must be loaded from the directory it was launched in (fromCwd),
680	+// not the user's current cwd, while the reconstructed file is still written under the
681	+// current cwd. Regression test for "source session ... has no data to reconstruct" when
682	+// resuming a session picked from another project via the all-projects browser.
683	+func TestPrepareResumeTargetLoadsSourceFromOriginCwd(t *testing.T) {
684	+	nativeDir := t.TempDir()
685	+	from := &fakeProvider{name: "Codex CLI"}
686	+	to := &fakeProvider{name: "Claude Code", nativeDir: nativeDir}
687	+
688	+	const originCwd = "/Users/jake/dev/tmp/blog-site"
689	+	const currentCwd = "/Users/jake/dev/specstory-website"
690	+
691	+	plan := &resumePlan{
692	+		from:      from,
693	+		fromID:    "codex",
694	+		sessionID: "019c4cdd-917a-74e3-9b2e-fdb45e9eddc5",
695	+		fromCwd:   originCwd,
696	+		to:        to,
697	+		toID:      "claude",
698	+	}
699	+
700	+	newID, err := prepareResumeTarget(plan, currentCwd, io.Discard)
701	+	if err != nil {
702	+		t.Fatalf("prepareResumeTarget returned error: %v", err)
703	+	}
704	+	if newID != "new-session-id" {
705	+		t.Errorf("resume target id = %q, want %q", newID, "new-session-id")
706	+	}
707	+	if from.gotLoadPath != originCwd {
708	+		t.Errorf("source loaded from %q, want origin cwd %q (current cwd %q must not be used)",
709	+			from.gotLoadPath, originCwd, currentCwd)
710	+	}
711	+}
712	+
713	+// TestPrepareResumeTargetFallsBackToCurrentCwd verifies that when the index row carries
714	+// no origin cwd (older rows), the source load falls back to the current cwd rather than
715	+// loading from an empty path.
716	+func TestPrepareResumeTargetFallsBackToCurrentCwd(t *testing.T) {
717	+	nativeDir := t.TempDir()
718	+	from := &fakeProvider{name: "Codex CLI"}
719	+	to := &fakeProvider{name: "Claude Code", nativeDir: nativeDir}
720	+
721	+	const currentCwd = "/Users/jake/dev/blog-site"
722	+
723	+	plan := &resumePlan{
724	+		from:      from,
725	+		fromID:    "codex",
726	+		sessionID: "sid",
727	+		fromCwd:   "", // older index row: no origin cwd recorded
728	+		to:        to,
729	+		toID:      "claude",
730	+	}
731	+
732	+	if _, err := prepareResumeTarget(plan, currentCwd, io.Discard); err != nil {
733	+		t.Fatalf("prepareResumeTarget returned error: %v", err)
734	+	}
735	+	if from.gotLoadPath != currentCwd {
736	+		t.Errorf("source loaded from %q, want fallback to current cwd %q", from.gotLoadPath, currentCwd)
737	+	}
738	+}
739	+
740	+// TestLiveIndexerRecord exercises real-time indexing (watch/run/resume): a live session is
741	+// upserted into the index, its FTS body is searchable, repeat records with no new activity are
742	+// skipped, and new activity updates the same row in place.
743	+func TestLiveIndexerRecord(t *testing.T) {
744	+	store, err := sessionindex.Open(filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "sessions.db"))
745	+	if err != nil {
746	+		t.Fatalf("open index: %v", err)
747	+	}
748	+	defer func() { _ = store.Close() }()
749	+
750	+	li := &LiveIndexer{
751	+		store:       store,
752	+		cwd:         "/work/proj",
753	+		projectID:   "proj-1",
754	+		projectName: "proj",
755	+		indexedAt:   "2026-06-25T00:00:00Z",
756	+		lastSeen:    map[string]string{},
757	+	}
758	+
759	+	msg := func(role, ts, text string) schema.Message {
760	+		return schema.Message{
761	+			Role:      role,
762	+			Timestamp: ts,
763	+			Content:   []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: text}},
764	+		}
765	+	}
766	+	sess := func(msgs ...schema.Message) *spi.AgentChatSession {
767	+		return &spi.AgentChatSession{
768	+			SessionID: "sess-1",
769	+			CreatedAt: "2026-06-25T00:00:00Z",
770	+			Slug:      "hello-world",
771	+			SessionData: &schema.SessionData{
772	+				Provider:  schema.ProviderInfo{Name: "Claude Code"},
773	+				Exchanges: []schema.Exchange{{Messages: msgs}},
774	+			},
775	+		}
776	+	}
777	+	user1 := msg(schema.RoleUser, "2026-06-25T00:01:00Z", "index this please")
778	+	agent1 := msg(schema.RoleAgent, "2026-06-25T00:02:00Z", "done indexing")
779	+
780	+	// First record: one user + one agent message → one fully-derived row.
781	+	li.Record("claude", sess(user1, agent1))
782	+	rows, err := store.ListByProject("proj-1")
783	+	if err != nil {
784	+		t.Fatalf("list: %v", err)
785	+	}
786	+	if len(rows) != 1 {
787	+		t.Fatalf("want 1 row, got %d", len(rows))
788	+	}
789	+	r := rows[0]
790	+	if r.Agent != "claude" || r.SessionID != "sess-1" || r.OriginCwd != "/work/proj" {
791	+		t.Errorf("identity wrong: agent=%q session=%q cwd=%q", r.Agent, r.SessionID, r.OriginCwd)
792	+	}
793	+	if r.UserTurns != 1 || r.TotalTurns != 2 {
794	+		t.Errorf("turns = %d/%d, want 1/2", r.UserTurns, r.TotalTurns)
795	+	}
796	+	if r.UpdatedAt != "2026-06-25T00:02:00Z" {
797	+		t.Errorf("updatedAt = %q, want last message timestamp", r.UpdatedAt)
798	+	}
799	+
800	+	// The conversation body is full-text searchable immediately.
801	+	if hits, _ := store.Search(ftsQuery("indexing"), "proj-1"); len(hits) != 1 {
802	+		t.Errorf("search for body word returned %d hits, want 1", len(hits))
803	+	}
804	+
805	+	// Dedup guard: re-recording with no new activity is a no-op.
806	+	li.Record("claude", sess(user1, agent1))
807	+	if rows, _ := store.ListByProject("proj-1"); len(rows) != 1 {
808	+		t.Errorf("dedup: want 1 row, got %d", len(rows))
809	+	}
810	+
811	+	// New activity (a later message) updates the same row in place.
812	+	li.Record("claude", sess(user1, agent1, msg(schema.RoleUser, "2026-06-25T00:03:00Z", "more")))
813	+	rows, _ = store.ListByProject("proj-1")
814	+	if len(rows) != 1 {
815	+		t.Fatalf("want 1 row after update, got %d", len(rows))
816	+	}
817	+	if rows[0].UserTurns != 2 || rows[0].TotalTurns != 3 || rows[0].UpdatedAt != "2026-06-25T00:03:00Z" {
818	+		t.Errorf("after update: turns=%d/%d updatedAt=%q, want 2/3 and advanced timestamp",
819	+			rows[0].UserTurns, rows[0].TotalTurns, rows[0].UpdatedAt)
820	+	}
821	+
822	+	// A nil indexer (index failed to open) is safe to use.
823	+	var nilLI *LiveIndexer
824	+	nilLI.Record("claude", sess(user1))
825	+	nilLI.Close()
826	+}

847	+)
848	+
849	+// CreateSearchCommand builds `specstory search [query…]`. It is the same interactive UI as
850	+// `specstory resume` (see sessionTUI), entered straight into the all-projects full-text
851	+// search with the input focused: type to search, `space` previews a match (glamour-rendered),
852	+// and `r` resumes it through the same launch path as `resume`. See docs/SESSION-SEARCH.md.
853	+func CreateSearchCommand() *cobra.Command {
854	+	return &cobra.Command{
855	+		Use:   "search [query…]",
856	+		Short: "Search and read your past coding-agent sessions",
857	+		Long: `Full-text search across every session SpecStory has indexed, then read the match.
858	+
859	+'search' opens an interactive search of your session history. Type to search, press space to
860	+preview a session (rendered for the terminal), and press 'r' to resume it in an agent. Anything
861	+after the command pre-seeds the query, e.g. 'specstory search max cpu'.`,
862	+		Args: cobra.ArbitraryArgs,
863	+		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
864	+			registry := factory.GetRegistry()
865	+			initialQuery := strings.TrimSpace(strings.Join(args, " "))
866	+
867	+			cwd, err := os.Getwd()
868	+			if err != nil {
869	+				return err
870	+			}
871	+
872	+			store, builtFresh, err := openOrBuildResumeIndex()
873	+			if err != nil {
874	+				return err
875	+			}
876	+			defer func() { _ = store.Close() }()
877	+
878	+			if total, _ := store.Count(); total == 0 {
879	+				fprintln(os.Stderr, "\nNo agent sessions indexed yet. Run an agent, then try again (or `specstory reindex`).")
880	+				return nil
881	+			}
882	+
883	+			homeID, homeName, idErr := utils.ComputeProjectID(cwd)
884	+			if idErr != nil || homeID == "" {
885	+				homeID, homeName = unknownProjectID, filepath.Base(cwd)
886	+			}
887	+			homeSessions, _ := store.ListByProject(homeID)
888	+
889	+			agents := map[string]agentMeta{}
890	+			var installed []agentChoice
891	+			for _, id := range registry.ListIDs() {
892	+				prov, err := registry.Get(id)
893	+				if err != nil {
894	+					continue
895	+				}
896	+				agents[id] = agentMeta{name: prov.Name(), accent: colorForAgent(id)}
897	+				if prov.Check("").Success {
898	+					installed = append(installed, agentChoice{id: id, provider: prov})
899	+				}
900	+			}
901	+
902	+			viewMode, lastAgent := "dense", ""
903	+			if cfg, _ := config.Load(nil); cfg != nil {
904	+				viewMode = cfg.GetResumeViewMode()
905	+				lastAgent = cfg.GetResumeLastAgent()
906	+			}
907	+
908	+			// `search` is the same TUI as `resume`, entered straight into the all-projects
909	+			// FTS with the input focused. See newSessionTUI / sessionTUIOpts.
910	+			model := newSessionTUI(store, registry, homeID, homeName, homeSessions, agents, installed, sessionTUIOpts{
911	+				title:         "SpecStory Search",
912	+				lastAgent:     lastAgent,
913	+				viewMode:      viewMode,
914	+				initialQuery:  initialQuery,
915	+				startInSearch: true,
916	+			})
917	+			p := tea.NewProgram(model)
918	+			cancelWarm := startIndexWarm(p, homeID, builtFresh)
919	+			final, err := p.Run()
920	+			cancelWarm() // stop warming the moment search exits (never block on it)
921	+			if err != nil {
922	+				return fmt.Errorf("search failed: %w", err)
923	+			}
924	+			rm := final.(sessionTUI)
925	+			if rm.result.cancelled || rm.result.session == nil {
926	+				return nil // cancelled: nothing to launch
927	+			}
928	+
929	+			// The user asked to resume a found session — launch via the shared path.
930	+			fromProv, err := registry.Get(rm.result.session.Agent)
931	+			if err != nil {
932	+				return fmt.Errorf("unknown source agent %q: %w", rm.result.session.Agent, err)
933	+			}
934	+			toProv, err := registry.Get(rm.result.targetID)
935	+			if err != nil {
936	+				return fmt.Errorf("unknown target agent %q: %w", rm.result.targetID, err)
937	+			}
938	+			_ = config.SaveResumePrefs(viewMode, rm.result.targetID)
939	+
940	+			plan := &resumePlan{
941	+				from:      fromProv,
942	+				fromID:    rm.result.session.Agent,
943	+				sessionID: rm.result.session.SessionID,
944	+				// A search hit can be from another project; resume must load the source from
945	+				// its original cwd, not the current directory.
946	+				fromCwd: rm.result.session.OriginCwd,
947	+				to:      toProv,
948	+				toID:    rm.result.targetID,
949	+			}
950	+			return launchResume(plan, cwd, resumeLaunchOpts{useUTC: true})
951	+		},
952	+	}
953	+}
954

9	+// projectDirNameRegex matches any character that is not alphanumeric or a dash.
10	+// Claude Code replaces each such character with a dash to map a working directory
11	+// to a project folder name under ~/.claude/projects.
12	+var projectDirNameRegex = regexp.MustCompile(`[^a-zA-Z0-9-]`)
13	+
14	+// encodeProjectDirName converts a real (symlink-resolved) path into Claude Code's
15	+// project directory name: non-alphanumeric/dash characters become dashes, with a
16	+// guaranteed leading dash. Example: "/Users/sean/app" -> "-Users-sean-app".
17	+func encodeProjectDirName(realPath string) string {
18	+	name := projectDirNameRegex.ReplaceAllString(realPath, "-")
19	+	if !strings.HasPrefix(name, "-") {
20	+		name = "-" + name
21	+	}
22	+	return name
23	+}

30	+func resolveClaudeProjectDir(projectPath string) (string, error) {

33	+		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get user home directory: %v", err)
34	+	}
35	+

40	+			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get current working directory: %v", err)

43	+
44	+	// Resolve symlinks to match Claude Code's behavior; fall back to the raw path
45	+	// if resolution fails (e.g., a path component does not exist yet).
46	+	realPath, err := filepath.EvalSymlinks(cwd)
47	+	if err != nil {
48	+		realPath = cwd
49	+	}
50	+
51	+	return filepath.Join(homeDir, ".claude", "projects", encodeProjectDirName(realPath)), nil
52	+}

249	+// extractCommandName pulls the slash command from a Claude Code command record, e.g.
250	+// "<command-name>/code-review</command-name>…" -> "/code-review". Falls back to the
251	+// bare command-message name (prefixed with "/"). Returns "" when none is present.
252	+func extractCommandName(content string) string {
253	+	if c := between(content, "<command-name>", "</command-name>"); c != "" {
254	+		return c
255	+	}
256	+	if c := between(content, "<command-message>", "</command-message>"); c != "" {
257	+		return "/" + c
258	+	}
259	+	return ""
260	+}
261	+
262	+// between returns the text between the first open/close markers, trimmed, or "".
263	+func between(s, openTag, closeTag string) string {
264	+	_, after, ok := strings.Cut(s, openTag)
265	+	if !ok {
266	+		return ""
267	+	}
268	+	inner, _, ok := strings.Cut(after, closeTag)
269	+	if !ok {
270	+		return ""
271	+	}
272	+	return strings.TrimSpace(inner)
273	+}

302	+// ListAllAgentChatSessions enumerates every Claude Code session across all projects. It is the
303	+// no-progress form of ListAllAgentChatSessionsProgress.
304	+func (p *Provider) ListAllAgentChatSessions() ([]spi.GlobalSessionRef, error) {
305	+	return p.ListAllAgentChatSessionsProgress(nil)
306	+}
307	+
308	+// ListAllAgentChatSessionsProgress enumerates every Claude Code session across all projects by
309	+// walking ~/.claude/projects/*/ for *.jsonl (the originating cwd comes from inside each session;
310	+// the project directory name is a lossy, irreversible encoding of the path), reporting scan
311	+// progress into r (nil-safe). Headers are scanned in parallel across CPUs; output order is
312	+// irrelevant (reindex dedups and sorts later). Implements spi.ProgressEnumerator. See
313	+// docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
314	+func (p *Provider) ListAllAgentChatSessionsProgress(r *spi.ScanReporter) ([]spi.GlobalSessionRef, error) {
315	+	projectsDir, err := GetClaudeCodeProjectsDir()
316	+	if err != nil {
317	+		// No projects directory yet → nothing to enumerate (not an error).
318	+		return []spi.GlobalSessionRef{}, nil
319	+	}
320	+
321	+	// Phase 1: collect file paths (dirents only — no opens).
322	+	var paths []string
323	+	walkErr := filepath.WalkDir(projectsDir, func(path string, d os.DirEntry, err error) error {
324	+		if err != nil {
325	+			return nil // skip unreadable entries rather than abort the whole sweep
326	+		}
327	+		if d.IsDir() || !strings.HasSuffix(path, ".jsonl") {
328	+			return nil
329	+		}
330	+		paths = append(paths, path)
331	+		return nil
332	+	})
333	+	if walkErr != nil {
334	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to walk claude projects: %w", walkErr)
335	+	}
336	+
337	+	// Phase 2: scan session headers in parallel — each file's read+parse is independent and
338	+	// CPU-bound, so this scales across cores.
339	+	workers := min(runtime.NumCPU(), 12)
340	+	if workers < 1 {
341	+		workers = 1
342	+	}
343	+	pathCh := make(chan string, workers)
344	+	var refs []spi.GlobalSessionRef
345	+	var mu sync.Mutex
346	+	var wg sync.WaitGroup
347	+	for i := 0; i < workers; i++ {
348	+		wg.Add(1)
349	+		go func() {
350	+			defer wg.Done()
351	+			for path := range pathCh {
352	+				scan, scanErr := scanClaudeSession(path)
353	+				if scanErr != nil {
354	+					slog.Warn("reindex: failed to scan claude session", "file", path, "error", scanErr)
355	+					continue
356	+				}
357	+				if !scan.foundRealMessage {
358	+					continue // warmup-only or sidechain-only transcript (not a session)
359	+				}
360	+				ref := spi.GlobalSessionRef{
361	+					SessionID:  scan.sessionID,
362	+					CreatedAt:  scan.timestamp,
363	+					Slug:       spi.GenerateFilenameFromUserMessage(scan.firstUserMessage),
364	+					Name:       spi.GenerateReadableName(scan.firstUserMessage),
365	+					NativePath: path,
366	+					OriginCwd:  scan.cwd,
367	+				}
368	+				mu.Lock()
369	+				refs = append(refs, ref)
370	+				mu.Unlock()
371	+				r.Add(1) // count sessions found, not files scanned (warmup/sidechain files yield none)
372	+			}
373	+		}()
374	+	}
375	+	for _, path := range paths {
376	+		pathCh <- path
377	+	}
378	+	close(pathCh)
379	+	wg.Wait()
380	+	return refs, nil
381	+}

432	new file mode 100644
433	index 0000000..a57010d
434	--- /dev/null
435	+++ b/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/claudecode/reconstruct.go
436	@@ -0,0 +1,127 @@
437	+package claudecode
438	+
439	+import (
440	+	"bytes"
441	+	"encoding/json"
442	+	"fmt"
443	+	"path/filepath"
444	+	"time"
445	+
446	+	"github.com/google/uuid"
447	+
448	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
449	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
450	+)
451	+
452	+const (
453	+	// reconstructedClaudeModel is a placeholder model label for reconstructed
454	+	// assistant records. It is only a historical display value — on resume Claude
455	+	// Code uses its own configured model for the next turn.
456	+	reconstructedClaudeModel = "claude-opus-4-8"
457	+
458	+	// reconstructedClaudeVersion mirrors the `version` field Claude Code writes on
459	+	// its records. A concrete value matching observed sessions maximizes the
460	+	// chance the loader accepts a synthesized file.
461	+	reconstructedClaudeVersion = "2.1.178"
462	+)
463	+
464	+// ReconstructSession rebuilds a Claude Code native JSONL session from the neutral
465	+// SessionData so `claude --resume <id>` can continue the conversation.
466	+//
467	+// The forward parser collapses Claude's parentUuid DAG and drops the API
468	+// message.id and thinking signatures, so reconstruction synthesizes a fresh,
469	+// linear parentUuid chain of plain user/assistant text records (tool calls and
470	+// thinking are already flattened into agent text by FlattenSessionData). This is
471	+// the structure validated by the Stage 1 spike. See docs/SESSION-PORTABILITY.md.
472	+func (p *Provider) ReconstructSession(data *schema.SessionData, opts spi.ReconstructOptions) (*spi.ReconstructedSession, error) {
473	+	if data == nil {
474	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("cannot reconstruct nil session data")
475	+	}
476	+
477	+	// Prefer the explicit target workspace; fall back to the session's own root.
478	+	cwd := opts.WorkspaceRoot
479	+	if cwd == "" {
480	+		cwd = data.WorkspaceRoot
481	+	}
482	+
483	+	turns := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, opts.MigrationNote)
484	+	if len(turns) == 0 {
485	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("session has no content to reconstruct")
486	+	}
487	+
488	+	newID := uuid.NewString()
489	+	base := time.Now().UTC()
490	+
491	+	var buf bytes.Buffer
492	+	enc := json.NewEncoder(&buf)
493	+	// Preserve <, >, & in conversation/markdown text rather than \u-escaping them.
494	+	enc.SetEscapeHTML(false)
495	+
496	+	var prevUUID string
497	+	for i, turn := range turns {
498	+		recUUID := uuid.NewString()
499	+		ts := base.Add(time.Duration(i) * time.Second).Format("2006-01-02T15:04:05.000Z")
500	+
501	+		rec := map[string]interface{}{
502	+			"isSidechain": false,
503	+			"userType":    "external",
504	+			"cwd":         cwd,
505	+			"sessionId":   newID,
506	+			"version":     reconstructedClaudeVersion,
507	+			"gitBranch":   "",
508	+			"uuid":        recUUID,
509	+			"timestamp":   ts,
510	+		}
511	+
512	+		// Linear chain: the first record roots the DAG (parentUuid null) and
513	+		// carries provenance back to the source session.
514	+		if prevUUID == "" {
515	+			rec["parentUuid"] = nil
516	+			rec["specstorySourceSessionId"] = data.SessionID
517	+		} else {
518	+			rec["parentUuid"] = prevUUID
519	+		}
520	+
521	+		if turn.Role == schema.RoleUser {
522	+			rec["type"] = "user"
523	+			rec["message"] = map[string]interface{}{
524	+				"role":    "user",
525	+				"content": turn.Text,
526	+			}
527	+		} else {
528	+			rec["type"] = "assistant"
529	+			rec["message"] = map[string]interface{}{
530	+				"id":          "msg_" + recUUID,
531	+				"type":        "message",
532	+				"role":        "assistant",
533	+				"model":       reconstructedClaudeModel,
534	+				"content":     []map[string]interface{}{{"type": "text", "text": turn.Text}},
535	+				"stop_reason": "end_turn",
536	+				"usage":       map[string]interface{}{"input_tokens": 1, "output_tokens": 1},
537	+			}
538	+		}
539	+
540	+		if err := enc.Encode(rec); err != nil {
541	+			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode reconstructed record: %w", err)
542	+		}
543	+
544	+		prevUUID = recUUID
545	+	}
546	+
547	+	return &spi.ReconstructedSession{
548	+		SessionID: newID,
549	+		Filename:  newID + ".jsonl",
550	+		Content:   buf.Bytes(),
551	+	}, nil
552	+}
553	+
554	+// NativeSessionPath returns where a reconstructed session file belongs in Claude
555	+// Code's store: ~/.claude/projects/<cwd-encoded>/<filename>. The directory is not
556	+// required to exist (the caller creates it).
557	+func (p *Provider) NativeSessionPath(projectPath string, filename string) (string, error) {
558	+	dir, err := resolveClaudeProjectDir(projectPath)
559	+	if err != nil {
560	+		return "", err
561	+	}
562	+	return filepath.Join(dir, filename), nil
563	+}
