584	+func strptr(s string) *string { return &s }
585	+
586	+// reconstructSampleData returns a SessionData exercising user text, agent text,
587	+// thinking, and a tool call (all of which flatten to user/agent text turns).
588	+func reconstructSampleData() *schema.SessionData {
589	+	fm := "Wrote `hello.c`:\n\n```c\n#include <stdio.h>\nint main(void){ printf(\"Hello, World!\\n\"); }\n```"
590	+	return &schema.SessionData{
591	+		SchemaVersion: "1.0",
592	+		Provider:      schema.ProviderInfo{ID: "claude-code", Name: "Claude Code", Version: "x"},
593	+		SessionID:     "orig-claude-123",
594	+		CreatedAt:     "2026-06-17T10:00:00.000Z",
595	+		WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp/proj",
596	+		Exchanges: []schema.Exchange{
597	+			{
598	+				ExchangeID: "orig-claude-123:0",
599	+				Messages: []schema.Message{
600	+					{Role: schema.RoleUser, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "Create a Hello World in C."}}},
601	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Model: "claude-x", Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "I'll create it."}}},
602	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeThinking, Text: "Need a main function."}}},
603	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Tool: &schema.ToolInfo{Name: "Write", Type: schema.ToolTypeWrite, FormattedMarkdown: strptr(fm)}},
604	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "Done."}}},
605	+				},
606	+			},
607	+		},
608	+	}
609	+}
610	+
611	+func parseClaudeJSONL(t *testing.T, content []byte) []JSONLRecord {
612	+	t.Helper()
613	+	var records []JSONLRecord
614	+	scanner := bufio.NewScanner(bytes.NewReader(content))
615	+	scanner.Buffer(make([]byte, 0, 64*1024), 16*1024*1024)
616	+	for scanner.Scan() {
617	+		line := strings.TrimSpace(scanner.Text())
618	+		if line == "" {
619	+			continue
620	+		}
621	+		var m map[string]interface{}
622	+		if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &m); err != nil {
623	+			t.Fatalf("invalid JSONL line %q: %v", line, err)
624	+		}
625	+		records = append(records, JSONLRecord{Data: m})
626	+	}
627	+	if err := scanner.Err(); err != nil {
628	+		t.Fatalf("scanning content: %v", err)
629	+	}
630	+	return records
631	+}
632	+
633	+// TestReconstructSession_RoundTrip reconstructs a session to native Claude JSONL,
634	+// re-parses it through the forward path, and asserts the flattened transcript is
635	+// preserved.
636	+func TestReconstructSession_RoundTrip(t *testing.T) {
637	+	data := reconstructSampleData()
638	+	expected := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, "")
639	+
640	+	out, err := NewProvider().ReconstructSession(data, spi.ReconstructOptions{WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp/proj"})
641	+	if err != nil {
642	+		t.Fatalf("ReconstructSession: %v", err)
643	+	}
644	+	if out.SessionID == "" {
645	+		t.Fatal("expected a fresh session ID")
646	+	}
647	+	if !strings.HasSuffix(out.Filename, ".jsonl") {
648	+		t.Errorf("filename %q should end in .jsonl", out.Filename)
649	+	}
650	+
651	+	records := parseClaudeJSONL(t, out.Content)
652	+	regenerated, err := GenerateAgentSession(Session{SessionUuid: out.SessionID, Records: records}, "/tmp/proj")
653	+	if err != nil {
654	+		t.Fatalf("GenerateAgentSession: %v", err)
655	+	}
656	+
657	+	actual := spi.FlattenSessionData(regenerated, "")
658	+	if len(actual) != len(expected) {
659	+		t.Fatalf("round-trip produced %d turns, want %d\n got: %+v\nwant: %+v", len(actual), len(expected), actual, expected)
660	+	}
661	+	for i := range expected {
662	+		if actual[i] != expected[i] {
663	+			t.Errorf("turn %d mismatch:\n got: %+v\nwant: %+v", i, actual[i], expected[i])
664	+		}
665	+	}
666	+}
667	+
668	+// TestReconstructSession_Chain verifies the records form a valid linear parentUuid
669	+// chain rooted at null, all sharing the fresh session ID.
670	+func TestReconstructSession_Chain(t *testing.T) {
671	+	out, err := NewProvider().ReconstructSession(reconstructSampleData(), spi.ReconstructOptions{WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp/proj"})
672	+	if err != nil {
673	+		t.Fatalf("ReconstructSession: %v", err)
674	+	}
675	+
676	+	records := parseClaudeJSONL(t, out.Content)
677	+	if len(records) == 0 {
678	+		t.Fatal("no records produced")
679	+	}
680	+
681	+	var prev string
682	+	for i, r := range records {
683	+		if sid, _ := r.Data["sessionId"].(string); sid != out.SessionID {
684	+			t.Errorf("record %d sessionId = %q, want %q", i, sid, out.SessionID)
685	+		}
686	+		parent := r.Data["parentUuid"]
687	+		if i == 0 {
688	+			if parent != nil {
689	+				t.Errorf("first record parentUuid = %v, want nil", parent)
690	+			}
691	+			if src, _ := r.Data["specstorySourceSessionId"].(string); src != "orig-claude-123" {
692	+				t.Errorf("first record provenance = %q, want orig-claude-123", src)
693	+			}
694	+		} else if ps, _ := parent.(string); ps != prev {
695	+			t.Errorf("record %d parentUuid = %v, want %q", i, parent, prev)
696	+		}
697	+		prev, _ = r.Data["uuid"].(string)
698	+	}
699	+}

705	+	}

707	+		t.Errorf("path %q should be under .claude/projects", path)

710	+		t.Errorf("path %q should end with the filename", path)
711	+	}
712	+	// The project segment encodes the cwd (non-alphanumerics become dashes).
713	+	if !strings.Contains(path, "-tmp-some-claude-proj") {
714	+		t.Errorf("path %q should contain the encoded project dir", path)
715	+	}
716	+}

742	+// GetAgentChatSessionByPath parses a single Codex session directly from its native file
743	+// path, implementing spi.PathSessionReader. It skips findCodexSessions' whole-tree walk —
744	+// which is what makes the by-id GetAgentChatSession O(N) per call, and a reindex of every
745	+// session O(N²) — by reading session_meta from the known file and handing off to the same
746	+// processSessionToAgentChat the by-id path uses. originCwd is the workspace root, matching
747	+// what GetAgentChatSession passes as projectPath.
748	+func (p *Provider) GetAgentChatSessionByPath(nativePath string, originCwd string, debugRaw bool) (*spi.AgentChatSession, error) {
749	+	meta, err := loadCodexSessionMeta(nativePath)
750	+	if err != nil {
751	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to load codex session meta: %w", err)
752	+	}
753	+	// debugRaw is only set when a caller wants provider-specific raw dumps + RawData; reindex
754	+	// (the only caller) never does. Honor it via the full path so that behavior is unchanged.
755	+	if debugRaw {
756	+		info := codexSessionInfo{
757	+			SessionID:   strings.TrimSpace(meta.Payload.ID),
758	+			SessionPath: nativePath,
759	+			Meta:        meta,
760	+		}
761	+		return processSessionToAgentChat(&info, originCwd, debugRaw)
762	+	}
763	+	// Index fast path: read records only (skip the whole-file rawData string reindex never
764	+	// reads) and skip slug derivation (reindex uses the slug from enumeration). The SessionData
765	+	// produced here is identical to the full path's.
766	+	records, err := readSessionRecords(nativePath)
767	+	if err != nil {
768	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to read session data: %w", err)
769	+	}
770	+	if len(records) == 0 {
771	+		return nil, nil
772	+	}
773	+	sessionData, err := GenerateAgentSession(records, originCwd)
774	+	if err != nil {
775	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to generate SessionData: %w", err)
776	+	}
777	+	return &spi.AgentChatSession{
778	+		SessionID:   strings.TrimSpace(meta.Payload.ID),
779	+		CreatedAt:   meta.Timestamp,
780	+		SessionData: sessionData,
781	+	}, nil
782	+}

797	+
798	+// readSessionRecords reads only the parsed JSONL records, skipping construction of the raw
799	+// JSONL string. reindex parses on this path: the raw content (up to the full file size — some
800	+// Codex sessions are hundreds of MB) is never read when indexing, so building it is pure waste.

806	+// readCodexJSONL reads a Codex session's JSONL records, and — when collectRaw is true — the raw

832	+
833	+// codexSessionHeader is the minimal metadata enumeration needs from a session file: the
834	+// session_meta fields plus the first user message (for slug/name), produced in a SINGLE pass.
835	+type codexSessionHeader struct {
836	+	sessionID        string
837	+	cwd              string
838	+	createdAt        string
839	+	firstUserMessage string
840	+}
841	+
842	+// userMessageMarker is the cheap substring screen for a Codex user_message record. Every real
843	+// user_message record contains it (it is the payload "type" value), so screening on it before a
844	+// full JSON parse never misses one; a rare false positive (the literal text inside an injected
845	+// context record) just costs one extra parse that codexUserMessageText then rejects.
846	+const userMessageMarker = `"user_message"`
847	+
848	+// scanCodexSessionHeader reads a Codex session file ONCE and extracts the session_meta
849	+// (id/cwd/timestamp from line 1) and the first user message. It deliberately avoids fully
850	+// parsing the large injected-context records (environment, instructions, AGENTS.md — often MB
851	+// each) that precede the first user message: each line is first cheaply screened for the
852	+// user_message marker, and only a candidate line is JSON-decoded. The result is identical to the
853	+// prior loadCodexSessionMeta + extractCodexSessionMetadata pair, in one pass instead of two opens.
854	+// Returns (nil, nil) for a session with no user message — treated as empty, as before.
855	+func scanCodexSessionHeader(sessionPath string) (*codexSessionHeader, error) {
856	+	file, err := os.Open(sessionPath)
857	+	if err != nil {
858	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to open session file: %w", err)
859	+	}
860	+	defer func() { _ = file.Close() }()
861	+
862	+	reader := bufio.NewReader(file)
863	+	h := &codexSessionHeader{}
864	+	lineNum := 0
865	+	for {
866	+		line, readErr := reader.ReadString('\n')
867	+		if readErr != nil && readErr != io.EOF {
868	+			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to read line: %w", readErr)
869	+		}
870	+		lineNum++
871	+		trimmed := strings.TrimSpace(line)
872	+
873	+		if trimmed != "" {
874	+			if lineNum == 1 {
875	+				// Line 1 is session_meta. Keep loadCodexSessionMeta's 64 KB first-line limit so
876	+				// the set of indexed sessions is unchanged from the Scanner-based path.
877	+				if len(trimmed) > bufio.MaxScanTokenSize {
878	+					return nil, errors.New("codex session meta exceeds line limit")
879	+				}
880	+				var meta codexSessionMeta
881	+				if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(trimmed), &meta); err != nil {
882	+					return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to parse codex session meta: %w", err)
883	+				}
884	+				if meta.Type != "session_meta" {
885	+					return nil, fmt.Errorf("unexpected codex session record type: %s", meta.Type)
886	+				}
887	+				h.sessionID = strings.TrimSpace(meta.Payload.ID)
888	+				h.cwd = meta.Payload.CWD
889	+				h.createdAt = meta.Timestamp
890	+			} else if strings.Contains(trimmed, userMessageMarker) {
891	+				// Cheap screen passed: only now pay for a full parse. The big context records
892	+				// before the first user message lack the marker and are skipped without a parse.
893	+				if msg := codexUserMessageText(trimmed); msg != "" {
894	+					h.firstUserMessage = msg
895	+					break
896	+				}
897	+			}
898	+		}
899	+
900	+		if readErr == io.EOF {
901	+			break
902	+		}
903	+	}
904	+
905	+	if h.sessionID == "" {
906	+		return nil, errors.New("codex session meta not found")
907	+	}
908	+	if h.firstUserMessage == "" {
909	+		return nil, nil // no user message → empty session, excluded from enumeration
910	+	}
911	+	return h, nil
912	+}
913	+
914	+// codexUserMessageText returns the message text when line is an event_msg user_message with a
915	+// non-empty message, else "". It is only called for lines that passed the userMessageMarker
916	+// screen, and applies the same structural checks extractCodexSessionMetadata used.
917	+func codexUserMessageText(line string) string {
918	+	var record map[string]interface{}
919	+	if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &record); err != nil {
920	+		return ""
921	+	}
922	+	if recordType, _ := record["type"].(string); recordType != "event_msg" {
923	+		return ""
924	+	}
925	+	payload, ok := record["payload"].(map[string]interface{})
926	+	if !ok {
927	+		return ""
928	+	}
929	+	if payloadType, _ := payload["type"].(string); payloadType != "user_message" {
930	+		return ""
931	+	}
932	+	message, _ := payload["message"].(string)
933	+	return message
934	+}
935	+
936	+// ListAllAgentChatSessions enumerates every Codex CLI session across all projects. It is the
937	+// no-progress form of ListAllAgentChatSessionsProgress.
938	+func (p *Provider) ListAllAgentChatSessions() ([]spi.GlobalSessionRef, error) {
939	+	return p.ListAllAgentChatSessionsProgress(nil)
940	+}
941	+
942	+// ListAllAgentChatSessionsProgress enumerates every Codex CLI session across all projects by
943	+// walking ~/.codex/sessions for *.jsonl rollouts (Codex sessions are not project-keyed by
944	+// directory; the originating cwd lives in the session_meta record), reporting scan progress into r
945	+// (nil-safe). The per-file header read is the dominant cost of `specstory reindex` (parsing each
946	+// file's injected context), so headers are scanned in parallel across CPUs; output order is
947	+// irrelevant (reindex dedups and sorts later). Implements spi.ProgressEnumerator. See
948	+// docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
949	+func (p *Provider) ListAllAgentChatSessionsProgress(r *spi.ScanReporter) ([]spi.GlobalSessionRef, error) {
950	+	homeDir, err := osUserHomeDir()
951	+	if err != nil {
952	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to determine home directory: %w", err)
953	+	}
954	+	sessionsRoot := codexSessionsRoot(homeDir)
955	+	if _, err := os.Stat(sessionsRoot); err != nil {
956	+		// No sessions directory yet → nothing to enumerate (not an error).
957	+		return []spi.GlobalSessionRef{}, nil
958	+	}
959	+
960	+	// Phase 1: collect file paths (dirents only — no opens).
961	+	var paths []string
962	+	walkErr := filepath.WalkDir(sessionsRoot, func(path string, d os.DirEntry, err error) error {
963	+		if err != nil {
964	+			return nil // skip unreadable entries rather than abort the sweep
965	+		}
966	+		if d.IsDir() || filepath.Ext(path) != ".jsonl" {
967	+			return nil
968	+		}
969	+		paths = append(paths, path)
970	+		return nil
971	+	})
972	+	if walkErr != nil {
973	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to walk codex sessions: %w", walkErr)
974	+	}
975	+
976	+	// Phase 2: scan headers in parallel. Each file's single-pass read is CPU-bound (JSON) and the
977	+	// files are independent, so this scales across cores.
978	+	workers := min(runtime.NumCPU(), 12)
979	+	if workers < 1 {
980	+		workers = 1
981	+	}
982	+	pathCh := make(chan string, workers)
983	+	refs := make([]spi.GlobalSessionRef, 0, len(paths))
984	+	var mu sync.Mutex
985	+	var wg sync.WaitGroup
986	+	for i := 0; i < workers; i++ {
987	+		wg.Add(1)
988	+		go func() {
989	+			defer wg.Done()
990	+			for path := range pathCh {
991	+				h, err := scanCodexSessionHeader(path)
992	+				if err != nil {
993	+					slog.Debug("reindex: failed to scan codex session header", "path", path, "error", err)
994	+					continue
995	+				}
996	+				if h == nil {
997	+					continue // empty session (no user message)
998	+				}
999	+				ref := spi.GlobalSessionRef{
1000	+					SessionID:  h.sessionID,
1001	+					CreatedAt:  h.createdAt,
1002	+					Slug:       spi.GenerateFilenameFromUserMessage(h.firstUserMessage),
1003	+					Name:       spi.GenerateReadableName(h.firstUserMessage),
1004	+					NativePath: path,
1005	+					OriginCwd:  h.cwd,
1006	+				}
1007	+				mu.Lock()
1008	+				refs = append(refs, ref)
1009	+				mu.Unlock()
1010	+				r.Add(1) // count sessions found, not files scanned (warmup/empty files yield none)
1011	+			}
1012	+		}()
1013	+	}
1014	+	for _, path := range paths {
1015	+		pathCh <- path
1016	+	}
1017	+	close(pathCh)
1018	+	wg.Wait()
1019	+	return refs, nil
1020	+}

1022	new file mode 100644
1023	index 0000000..b52bf14
1024	--- /dev/null
1025	+++ b/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/codexcli/reconstruct.go
1026	@@ -0,0 +1,160 @@
1027	+package codexcli
1028	+
1029	+import (
1030	+	"bytes"
1031	+	"encoding/json"
1032	+	"fmt"
1033	+	"os"
1034	+	"path/filepath"
1035	+	"time"
1036	+
1037	+	"github.com/google/uuid"
1038	+
1039	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
1040	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
1041	+)
1042	+
1043	+// reconstructedCodexCliVersion mirrors the `cli_version` Codex writes into
1044	+// session_meta. A concrete value matching observed sessions maximizes the chance
1045	+// the loader accepts a synthesized file.
1046	+const reconstructedCodexCliVersion = "0.139.0"
1047	+
1048	+// codexTimestamp formats a time the way Codex records do (RFC3339 millis, Z).
1049	+func codexTimestamp(t time.Time) string {
1050	+	return t.Format("2006-01-02T15:04:05.000Z")
1051	+}
1052	+
1053	+// ReconstructSession rebuilds a Codex CLI native rollout from the neutral
1054	+// SessionData so `codex resume <id>` can continue the conversation.
1055	+//
1056	+// Codex resume replays the response_item model transcript, while the forward
1057	+// parser reads the event_msg UI stream — so reconstruction emits BOTH for each
1058	+// turn: a response_item message (what the agent replays) and a matching event_msg
1059	+// (what the UI and our own re-parse read). Tool calls and thinking are already
1060	+// flattened into agent text by FlattenSessionData.
1061	+//
1062	+// We do NOT synthesize an environment_context block: Codex injects its own fresh
1063	+// environment context on every resume, consistent with the cross-provider policy
1064	+// that the target supplies its own preamble. See docs/SESSION-PORTABILITY.md.
1065	+func (p *Provider) ReconstructSession(data *schema.SessionData, opts spi.ReconstructOptions) (*spi.ReconstructedSession, error) {
1066	+	if data == nil {
1067	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("cannot reconstruct nil session data")
1068	+	}
1069	+
1070	+	cwd := opts.WorkspaceRoot
1071	+	if cwd == "" {
1072	+		cwd = data.WorkspaceRoot
1073	+	}
1074	+
1075	+	turns := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, opts.MigrationNote)
1076	+	if len(turns) == 0 {
1077	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("session has no content to reconstruct")
1078	+	}
1079	+
1080	+	// Codex session IDs are UUIDv7. Fall back to v4 if v7 generation fails.
1081	+	newID := uuid.NewString()
1082	+	if v7, err := uuid.NewV7(); err == nil {
1083	+		newID = v7.String()
1084	+	}
1085	+
1086	+	base := time.Now().UTC()
1087	+
1088	+	var buf bytes.Buffer
1089	+	enc := json.NewEncoder(&buf)
1090	+	enc.SetEscapeHTML(false)
1091	+
1092	+	encode := func(rec map[string]interface{}) error {
1093	+		return enc.Encode(rec)
1094	+	}
1095	+
1096	+	// session_meta must be the first record; it also carries provenance.
1097	+	metaTS := codexTimestamp(base)
1098	+	if err := encode(map[string]interface{}{
1099	+		"timestamp": metaTS,
1100	+		"type":      "session_meta",
1101	+		"payload": map[string]interface{}{
1102	+			"id":                       newID,
1103	+			"timestamp":                metaTS,
1104	+			"cwd":                      cwd,
1105	+			"originator":               "codex-tui",
1106	+			"cli_version":              reconstructedCodexCliVersion,
1107	+			"source":                   "cli",
1108	+			"model_provider":           "openai",
1109	+			"specstorySourceSessionId": data.SessionID,
1110	+		},
1111	+	}); err != nil {
1112	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode session_meta: %w", err)
1113	+	}
1114	+
1115	+	for i, turn := range turns {
1116	+		ts := codexTimestamp(base.Add(time.Duration(i+1) * time.Second))
1117	+
1118	+		var role, contentType, eventType string
1119	+		if turn.Role == schema.RoleUser {
1120	+			role, contentType, eventType = "user", "input_text", "user_message"
1121	+		} else {
1122	+			role, contentType, eventType = "assistant", "output_text", "agent_message"
1123	+		}
1124	+
1125	+		// response_item: the model-facing transcript the agent replays on resume.
1126	+		if err := encode(codexResponseItemMessage(ts, role, contentType, turn.Text)); err != nil {
1127	+			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode response_item: %w", err)
1128	+		}
1129	+		// event_msg: the UI/history stream the forward parser reads.
1130	+		if err := encode(codexEventMessage(ts, eventType, turn.Text)); err != nil {
1131	+			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode event_msg: %w", err)
1132	+		}
1133	+	}
1134	+
1135	+	filename := fmt.Sprintf("rollout-%s-%s.jsonl", base.Format("2006-01-02T15-04-05"), newID)
1136	+
1137	+	return &spi.ReconstructedSession{
1138	+		SessionID: newID,
1139	+		Filename:  filename,
1140	+		Content:   buf.Bytes(),
1141	+	}, nil
1142	+}
1143	+
1144	+// NativeSessionPath returns where a reconstructed rollout belongs in Codex's
1145	+// store: ~/.codex/sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/<filename>, dated today. Codex sessions are
1146	+// not project-scoped by directory, so projectPath is unused. The directory is not
1147	+// required to exist (the caller creates it).
1148	+func (p *Provider) NativeSessionPath(projectPath string, filename string) (string, error) {
1149	+	homeDir, err := os.UserHomeDir()
1150	+	if err != nil {
1151	+		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get user home directory: %w", err)
1152	+	}
1153	+	now := time.Now()
1154	+	dir := filepath.Join(
1155	+		codexSessionsRoot(homeDir),
1156	+		fmt.Sprintf("%04d", now.Year()),
1157	+		fmt.Sprintf("%02d", int(now.Month())),
1158	+		fmt.Sprintf("%02d", now.Day()),
1159	+	)
1160	+	return filepath.Join(dir, filename), nil
1161	+}
1162	+
1163	+// codexResponseItemMessage builds a response_item "message" record.
1164	+func codexResponseItemMessage(ts, role, contentType, text string) map[string]interface{} {
1165	+	return map[string]interface{}{
1166	+		"timestamp": ts,
1167	+		"type":      "response_item",
1168	+		"payload": map[string]interface{}{
1169	+			"type":    "message",
1170	+			"role":    role,
1171	+			"content": []map[string]interface{}{{"type": contentType, "text": text}},
1172	+		},
1173	+	}
1174	+}
1175	+
1176	+// codexEventMessage builds an event_msg record (user_message / agent_message).
1177	+func codexEventMessage(ts, eventType, message string) map[string]interface{} {
1178	+	return map[string]interface{}{
1179	+		"timestamp": ts,
1180	+		"type":      "event_msg",
1181	+		"payload": map[string]interface{}{
1182	+			"type":    eventType,
1183	+			"message": message,
1184	+		},
1185	+	}
1186	+}

1209	+// reconstructSampleData exercises user text, agent text, thinking, and a tool
1210	+// call carrying both a summary and formatted markdown.
1211	+func reconstructSampleData() *schema.SessionData {
1212	+	summary := "Tool use: **exec_command** `ls`"
1213	+	fm := "```\nhello.c\nhello\n```"
1214	+	return &schema.SessionData{
1215	+		SchemaVersion: "1.0",
1216	+		Provider:      schema.ProviderInfo{ID: "codex-cli", Name: "Codex CLI", Version: "x"},
1217	+		SessionID:     "orig-codex-123",
1218	+		CreatedAt:     "2026-06-17T10:00:00.000Z",
1219	+		WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp/proj",
1220	+		Exchanges: []schema.Exchange{
1221	+			{
1222	+				ExchangeID: "orig-codex-123:0",
1223	+				Messages: []schema.Message{
1224	+					{Role: schema.RoleUser, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "Create a hello world in D."}}},
1225	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "Adding hello.d."}}},
1226	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeThinking, Text: "Check for a compiler."}}},
1227	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Tool: &schema.ToolInfo{Name: "exec_command", Type: schema.ToolTypeShell, Summary: strptr(summary), FormattedMarkdown: strptr(fm)}},
1228	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "Created hello.d."}}},
1229	+				},
1230	+			},
1231	+		},
1232	+	}
1233	+}
1234	+
1235	+func parseCodexJSONL(t *testing.T, content []byte) []map[string]interface{} {
1236	+	t.Helper()
1237	+	var records []map[string]interface{}
1238	+	scanner := bufio.NewScanner(bytes.NewReader(content))
1239	+	scanner.Buffer(make([]byte, 0, 64*1024), 16*1024*1024)
1240	+	for scanner.Scan() {
1241	+		line := strings.TrimSpace(scanner.Text())
1242	+		if line == "" {
1243	+			continue
1244	+		}
1245	+		var m map[string]interface{}
1246	+		if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &m); err != nil {
1247	+			t.Fatalf("invalid JSONL line %q: %v", line, err)
1248	+		}
1249	+		records = append(records, m)
1250	+	}
1251	+	if err := scanner.Err(); err != nil {
1252	+		t.Fatalf("scanning content: %v", err)
1253	+	}
1254	+	return records
1255	+}
1256	+
1257	+// TestReconstructSession_RoundTrip reconstructs to native Codex rollout format,
1258	+// re-parses through the forward path, and asserts the flattened transcript is
1259	+// preserved.
1260	+func TestReconstructSession_RoundTrip(t *testing.T) {
1261	+	data := reconstructSampleData()
1262	+	expected := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, "")
1263	+
1264	+	out, err := NewProvider().ReconstructSession(data, spi.ReconstructOptions{WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp/proj"})
1265	+	if err != nil {
1266	+		t.Fatalf("ReconstructSession: %v", err)
1267	+	}
1268	+	if out.SessionID == "" {
1269	+		t.Fatal("expected a fresh session ID")
1270	+	}
1271	+	if !strings.HasPrefix(out.Filename, "rollout-") || !strings.HasSuffix(out.Filename, ".jsonl") {
1272	+		t.Errorf("filename %q should look like rollout-<ts>-<id>.jsonl", out.Filename)
1273	+	}
1274	+
1275	+	records := parseCodexJSONL(t, out.Content)
1276	+	regenerated, err := GenerateAgentSession(records, "/tmp/proj")
1277	+	if err != nil {
1278	+		t.Fatalf("GenerateAgentSession: %v", err)
1279	+	}
1280	+
1281	+	actual := spi.FlattenSessionData(regenerated, "")
1282	+	if len(actual) != len(expected) {
1283	+		t.Fatalf("round-trip produced %d turns, want %d\n got: %+v\nwant: %+v", len(actual), len(expected), actual, expected)
1284	+	}
1285	+	for i := range expected {
1286	+		if actual[i] != expected[i] {
1287	+			t.Errorf("turn %d mismatch:\n got: %+v\nwant: %+v", i, actual[i], expected[i])
1288	+		}
1289	+	}
1290	+}
1291	+
1292	+// TestReconstructSession_Structure checks the first record is session_meta with a
1293	+// matching id and provenance, that there is exactly one user_message event, and
1294	+// that no synthesized environment_context is emitted (Codex injects its own).
1295	+func TestReconstructSession_Structure(t *testing.T) {
1296	+	out, err := NewProvider().ReconstructSession(reconstructSampleData(), spi.ReconstructOptions{WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp/proj"})
1297	+	if err != nil {
1298	+		t.Fatalf("ReconstructSession: %v", err)
1299	+	}
1300	+
1301	+	records := parseCodexJSONL(t, out.Content)
1302	+	if len(records) == 0 {
1303	+		t.Fatal("no records produced")
1304	+	}
1305	+
1306	+	meta := records[0]
1307	+	if meta["type"] != "session_meta" {
1308	+		t.Fatalf("first record type = %v, want session_meta", meta["type"])
1309	+	}
1310	+	payload, _ := meta["payload"].(map[string]interface{})
1311	+	if payload["id"] != out.SessionID {
1312	+		t.Errorf("session_meta id = %v, want %q", payload["id"], out.SessionID)
1313	+	}
1314	+	if payload["specstorySourceSessionId"] != "orig-codex-123" {
1315	+		t.Errorf("provenance = %v, want orig-codex-123", payload["specstorySourceSessionId"])
1316	+	}
1317	+
1318	+	// Exactly one user_message event per user turn; no synthesized environment_context anywhere.
1319	+	userEvents := 0
1320	+	for _, r := range records {
1321	+		if strings.Contains(toJSON(t, r), "environment_context") {
1322	+			t.Error("reconstruction should not synthesize an environment_context block")
1323	+		}
1324	+		if r["type"] != "event_msg" {
1325	+			continue
1326	+		}
1327	+		p, _ := r["payload"].(map[string]interface{})
1328	+		if p["type"] == "user_message" {
1329	+			userEvents++
1330	+		}
1331	+	}
1332	+	if userEvents != 1 {
1333	+		t.Errorf("got %d user_message events, want 1", userEvents)
1334	+	}
1335	+}
1336	+
1337	+func toJSON(t *testing.T, v map[string]interface{}) string {
1338	+	t.Helper()
1339	+	b, err := json.Marshal(v)
1340	+	if err != nil {
1341	+		t.Fatalf("marshal: %v", err)
1342	+	}
1343	+	return string(b)
1344	+}
1345	+
1346	+func TestNativeSessionPath(t *testing.T) {
1347	+	path, err := NewProvider().NativeSessionPath("/tmp/some-codex-proj", "rollout-x.jsonl")
1348	+	if err != nil {

1356	+	}

10	+// ListAllAgentChatSessions enumerates every session in this provider's native store,
11	+// regardless of project. See docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
12	+//
13	+// Cursor stores sessions at ~/.cursor/chats/<projectHash>/<sessionID>/store.db, where
14	+// projectHash = md5(canonical project path). Cursor records NO workspace path inside
15	+// the store (the meta blob has only agent/model fields), and md5 is one-way — so
16	+// OriginCwd cannot be filled from the store alone and is left empty here. The hash is
17	+// embedded in NativePath, so `specstory reindex` recovers the cwd by reverse-matching
18	+// that hash against the project paths the other providers surface.
19	+func (p *Provider) ListAllAgentChatSessions() ([]spi.GlobalSessionRef, error) {
20	+	chatsDir, err := GetCursorChatsDir()
21	+	if err != nil {
22	+		return nil, err
23	+	}
24	+	hashEntries, err := os.ReadDir(chatsDir)
25	+	if err != nil {
26	+		if os.IsNotExist(err) {
27	+			return []spi.GlobalSessionRef{}, nil
28	+		}
29	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to read cursor chats directory: %w", err)
30	+	}
31	+
32	+	var refs []spi.GlobalSessionRef
33	+	for _, hashEntry := range hashEntries {
34	+		if !hashEntry.IsDir() {
35	+			continue
36	+		}
37	+		hashDir := filepath.Join(chatsDir, hashEntry.Name())
38	+		sessionIDs, err := GetCursorSessionDirs(hashDir)
39	+		if err != nil {
40	+			continue
41	+		}
42	+		for _, sessionID := range sessionIDs {
43	+			if !HasStoreDB(hashDir, sessionID) {
44	+				continue
45	+			}
46	+			sessionPath := filepath.Join(hashDir, sessionID)
47	+			metadata, err := extractCursorSessionMetadata(sessionPath, sessionID)
48	+			if err != nil {
49	+				slog.Warn("reindex: failed to extract cursor session metadata", "sessionID", sessionID, "error", err)
50	+				continue
51	+			}
52	+			if metadata == nil {
53	+				continue // empty session
54	+			}
55	+			refs = append(refs, spi.GlobalSessionRef{
56	+				SessionID:  metadata.SessionID,
57	+				CreatedAt:  metadata.CreatedAt,
58	+				Slug:       metadata.Slug,
59	+				Name:       metadata.Name,
60	+				NativePath: filepath.Join(sessionPath, "store.db"),
61	+				OriginCwd:  "", // not stored by Cursor; recovered at reindex via the hash in NativePath
62	+			})
63	+		}
64	+	}
65	+	return refs, nil
66	+}
67	diff --git a/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/cursorcli/reconstruct.go b/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/cursorcli/reconstruct.go
68	new file mode 100644
69	index 0000000..af0c03a
70	--- /dev/null
71	+++ b/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/cursorcli/reconstruct.go
72	@@ -0,0 +1,222 @@
73	+package cursorcli
74	+
75	+import (
76	+	"bytes"
77	+	"crypto/sha256"
78	+	"database/sql"
79	+	"encoding/binary"
80	+	"encoding/hex"
81	+	"encoding/json"
82	+	"fmt"
83	+	"os"
84	+	"path/filepath"
85	+	"time"
86	+
87	+	"github.com/google/uuid"
88	+
89	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
90	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
91	+)
92	+
93	+// Cursor stores a session as a SQLite store.db: a content-addressed Merkle-DAG of
94	+// blobs where each blob's id == sha256(data). Conversation messages are PLAIN JSON
95	+// blobs; small protobuf "index" nodes thread them into the DAG; `meta['0']` carries
96	+// the head pointer (`latestRootBlobId`). Tool calls and thinking are already
97	+// flattened into agent text by FlattenSessionData, so reconstruction emits only
98	+// user/assistant text-message blobs. The structure below (messages + root + a
99	+// field-2 metadata subtree of title/empty/preview nodes, with NO system message)
100	+// was validated against `cursor-agent --resume`. See docs/SESSION-PORTABILITY.md.
101	+
102	+// blobID returns the content-addressed id Cursor uses: sha256(data), hex-encoded.
103	+func blobID(data []byte) string {
104	+	sum := sha256.Sum256(data)
105	+	return hex.EncodeToString(sum[:])
106	+}
107	+
108	+// pbField encodes a protobuf length-delimited field (wire type 2). fieldNum must be < 16.
109	+func pbField(fieldNum int, payload []byte) []byte {
110	+	out := []byte{byte(fieldNum<<3 | 2)}
111	+	var lenBuf [binary.MaxVarintLen64]byte
112	+	n := binary.PutUvarint(lenBuf[:], uint64(len(payload)))
113	+	out = append(out, lenBuf[:n]...)
114	+	return append(out, payload...)
115	+}
116	+
117	+// pbRef encodes a length-delimited field carrying a 32-byte blob id reference.
118	+func pbRef(fieldNum int, idHex string) []byte {
119	+	raw, _ := hex.DecodeString(idHex)
120	+	return pbField(fieldNum, raw)
121	+}
122	+
123	+// cursorTextPart / message structs marshal to Cursor's plain-JSON message shape.
124	+type cursorTextPart struct {
125	+	Type string `json:"type"`
126	+	Text string `json:"text"`
127	+}
128	+type cursorUserMsg struct {
129	+	Role    string           `json:"role"`
130	+	Content []cursorTextPart `json:"content"`
131	+}
132	+type cursorAsstMsg struct {
133	+	ID      string           `json:"id"`
134	+	Role    string           `json:"role"`
135	+	Content []cursorTextPart `json:"content"`
136	+}
137	+
138	+// marshalCompact JSON-encodes v without HTML escaping and without a trailing newline.
139	+func marshalCompact(v interface{}) ([]byte, error) {
140	+	var buf bytes.Buffer
141	+	enc := json.NewEncoder(&buf)
142	+	enc.SetEscapeHTML(false)
143	+	if err := enc.Encode(v); err != nil {
144	+		return nil, err
145	+	}
146	+	return bytes.TrimRight(buf.Bytes(), "\n"), nil
147	+}
148	+
149	+// ReconstructSession rebuilds a Cursor CLI native store.db from the neutral SessionData.
150	+func (p *Provider) ReconstructSession(data *schema.SessionData, opts spi.ReconstructOptions) (*spi.ReconstructedSession, error) {
151	+	if data == nil {
152	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("cannot reconstruct nil session data")
153	+	}
154	+
155	+	turns := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, opts.MigrationNote)
156	+	if len(turns) == 0 {
157	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("session has no content to reconstruct")
158	+	}
159	+
160	+	newID := uuid.NewString()
161	+	name := data.Slug
162	+	if name == "" {
163	+		name = "Resumed session"
164	+	}
165	+
166	+	// blobs accumulates every blob (id -> data); helper interns by content address.
167	+	blobs := map[string][]byte{}
168	+	put := func(b []byte) string { id := blobID(b); blobs[id] = b; return id }
169	+
170	+	// 1. Message blobs (plain JSON), in order. No system message.
171	+	var msgIDs []string
172	+	var lastAssistantText string
173	+	asstSeq := 0
174	+	for _, turn := range turns {
175	+		var raw []byte
176	+		var err error
177	+		if turn.Role == schema.RoleUser {
178	+			raw, err = marshalCompact(cursorUserMsg{Role: "user", Content: []cursorTextPart{{Type: "text", Text: turn.Text}}})
179	+		} else {
180	+			asstSeq++
181	+			raw, err = marshalCompact(cursorAsstMsg{ID: fmt.Sprintf("%d", asstSeq), Role: "assistant", Content: []cursorTextPart{{Type: "text", Text: turn.Text}}})
182	+			lastAssistantText = turn.Text
183	+		}
184	+		if err != nil {
185	+			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode message: %w", err)
186	+		}
187	+		msgIDs = append(msgIDs, put(raw))
188	+	}
189	+
190	+	// 2. Field-2 metadata subtree: title leaf, empty placeholder, preview node.
191	+	titleID := put(append(pbField(1, []byte(name)), pbField(2, []byte(uuid.NewString()))...))
192	+	emptyID := put(pbField(3, nil))                                     // {f3:""}
193	+	previewID := put(pbField(1, pbField(1, []byte(lastAssistantText)))) // {f1:{f1:text}}
194	+	metaNodeID := put(concat(pbRef(1, titleID), pbRef(2, emptyID), pbRef(2, previewID)))
195	+
196	+	// 3. Root node: field 1 = ordered message refs, field 2 = metadata node.
197	+	var root []byte
198	+	for _, mid := range msgIDs {
199	+		root = append(root, pbRef(1, mid)...)
200	+	}
201	+	root = append(root, pbRef(2, metaNodeID)...)
202	+	rootID := put(root)
203	+
204	+	// 4. meta['0'] head record (hex-encoded JSON).
205	+	metaJSON, err := marshalCompact(map[string]interface{}{
206	+		"agentId":          newID,
207	+		"latestRootBlobId": rootID,
208	+		"name":             name,
209	+		"mode":             "default",
210	+		"createdAt":        time.Now().UTC().UnixMilli(),
211	+	})
212	+	if err != nil {
213	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode meta: %w", err)
214	+	}
215	+
216	+	// 5. Materialize the SQLite store.db and return its bytes. SQLite needs a file
217	+	// handle, so we build in a temp file (created and removed within this call).
218	+	content, err := buildStoreDB(blobs, hex.EncodeToString(metaJSON))
219	+	if err != nil {
220	+		return nil, err
221	+	}
222	+
223	+	return &spi.ReconstructedSession{
224	+		SessionID: newID,
225	+		Filename:  filepath.Join(newID, "store.db"),
226	+		Content:   content,
227	+	}, nil
228	+}
229	+
230	+// concat joins byte slices.
231	+func concat(parts ...[]byte) []byte {
232	+	var out []byte
233	+	for _, p := range parts {
234	+		out = append(out, p...)
235	+	}
236	+	return out
237	+}
238	+
239	+// buildStoreDB writes the blobs + meta into a SQLite database and returns its bytes.
240	+func buildStoreDB(blobs map[string][]byte, metaHex string) ([]byte, error) {
241	+	tmp, err := os.CreateTemp("", "specstory-cursor-*.db")
242	+	if err != nil {
243	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to create temp db: %w", err)
244	+	}
245	+	tmpPath := tmp.Name()
246	+	_ = tmp.Close()
247	+	defer func() { _ = os.Remove(tmpPath) }()
248	+
249	+	db, err := sql.Open("sqlite", tmpPath)
250	+	if err != nil {
251	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to open temp db: %w", err)
252	+	}
253	+	if _, err := db.Exec("CREATE TABLE blobs (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, data BLOB)"); err != nil {
254	+		_ = db.Close()
255	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to create blobs table: %w", err)
256	+	}
257	+	if _, err := db.Exec("CREATE TABLE meta (key TEXT PRIMARY KEY, value TEXT)"); err != nil {
258	+		_ = db.Close()
259	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to create meta table: %w", err)
260	+	}
261	+	for id, data := range blobs {
262	+		if _, err := db.Exec("INSERT INTO blobs (id, data) VALUES (?, ?)", id, data); err != nil {
263	+			_ = db.Close()
264	+			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to insert blob: %w", err)
265	+		}
266	+	}
267	+	if _, err := db.Exec("INSERT INTO meta (key, value) VALUES ('0', ?)", metaHex); err != nil {
268	+		_ = db.Close()
269	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to insert meta: %w", err)
270	+	}
271	+	if err := db.Close(); err != nil {
272	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to close temp db: %w", err)
273	+	}
274	+
275	+	return os.ReadFile(tmpPath)
276	+}
277	+
278	+// NativeSessionPath returns where a reconstructed store.db belongs in Cursor's store:
279	+// ~/.cursor/chats/<md5(canonical cwd)>/<session-id>/store.db. The filename already
280	+// includes the session-id subdir, so this joins it under the project hash dir.
281	+func (p *Provider) NativeSessionPath(projectPath string, filename string) (string, error) {
282	+	if projectPath == "" {
283	+		var err error
284	+		projectPath, err = os.Getwd()
285	+		if err != nil {
286	+			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get current working directory: %w", err)
287	+		}
288	+	}
289	+	hashDir, err := GetProjectHashDir(projectPath)
290	+	if err != nil {
291	+		return "", err
292	+	}
293	+	return filepath.Join(hashDir, filename), nil
294	+}

298	--- /dev/null
299	+++ b/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/cursorcli/reconstruct_test.go
300	@@ -0,0 +1,183 @@
301	+package cursorcli
302	+
303	+import (
304	+	"crypto/sha256"
305	+	"database/sql"
306	+	"encoding/hex"
307	+	"encoding/json"
308	+	"os"
309	+	"path/filepath"
310	+	"strings"
311	+	"testing"
312	+
313	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
314	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
315	+)
316	+
317	+func strptr(s string) *string { return &s }
318	+
319	+func reconstructSampleData() *schema.SessionData {
320	+	fm := "Created `hello.rs`:\n\n```rust\nfn main() { println!(\"Hello, World!\"); }\n```"
321	+	return &schema.SessionData{
322	+		SchemaVersion: "1.0",
323	+		Provider:      schema.ProviderInfo{ID: "cursor", Name: "Cursor CLI", Version: "x"},
324	+		SessionID:     "orig-cursor-123",
325	+		CreatedAt:     "2026-06-18T10:00:00.000Z",
326	+		WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp/proj",
327	+		Slug:          "hello world in rust",
328	+		Exchanges: []schema.Exchange{
329	+			{
330	+				ExchangeID: "orig-cursor-123:0",
331	+				Messages: []schema.Message{
332	+					{Role: schema.RoleUser, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "Create a hello world in Rust."}}},
333	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "I'll create it."}}},
334	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Tool: &schema.ToolInfo{Name: "write", Type: schema.ToolTypeWrite, FormattedMarkdown: strptr(fm)}},
335	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "Done."}}},
336	+				},
337	+			},
338	+		},
339	+	}
340	+}
341	+
342	+// openStoreBytes writes the reconstructed store.db bytes to a temp file and reads
343	+// back its blobs + meta head record.
344	+func openStoreBytes(t *testing.T, content []byte) (map[string][]byte, map[string]interface{}) {
345	+	t.Helper()
346	+	path := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "store.db")
347	+	if err := os.WriteFile(path, content, 0o644); err != nil {
348	+		t.Fatalf("write store: %v", err)
349	+	}
350	+	db, err := sql.Open("sqlite", path+"?mode=ro")
351	+	if err != nil {
352	+		t.Fatalf("open store: %v", err)
353	+	}
354	+	defer func() { _ = db.Close() }()
355	+
356	+	blobs := map[string][]byte{}
357	+	rows, err := db.Query("SELECT id, data FROM blobs")
358	+	if err != nil {
359	+		t.Fatalf("query blobs: %v", err)
360	+	}
361	+	for rows.Next() {
362	+		var id string
363	+		var data []byte
364	+		if err := rows.Scan(&id, &data); err != nil {
365	+			t.Fatalf("scan: %v", err)
366	+		}
367	+		blobs[id] = data
368	+	}
369	+	_ = rows.Close()
370	+
371	+	var metaHex string
372	+	if err := db.QueryRow("SELECT value FROM meta WHERE key='0'").Scan(&metaHex); err != nil {
373	+		t.Fatalf("meta: %v", err)
374	+	}
375	+	metaBytes, err := hex.DecodeString(metaHex)
376	+	if err != nil {
377	+		t.Fatalf("meta hex: %v", err)
378	+	}
379	+	var meta map[string]interface{}
380	+	if err := json.Unmarshal(metaBytes, &meta); err != nil {
381	+		t.Fatalf("meta json: %v", err)
382	+	}
383	+	return blobs, meta
384	+}
385	+
386	+// refsOf scans a protobuf node for 32-byte blob-id references in the given field.
387	+func refsOf(node []byte, fieldNum int) []string {
388	+	tag := byte(fieldNum<<3 | 2)
389	+	var refs []string
390	+	for i := 0; i+34 <= len(node); i++ {
391	+		if node[i] == tag && node[i+1] == 0x20 {
392	+			refs = append(refs, hex.EncodeToString(node[i+2:i+34]))
393	+		}
394	+	}
395	+	return refs
396	+}
397	+
398	+// TestReconstructSession_Structure validates the store.db has the content-addressed
399	+// shape Cursor requires: sha256 ids, a head pointer, and a root whose field-1 lists
400	+// the message blobs in order with a field-2 metadata node.
401	+func TestReconstructSession_Structure(t *testing.T) {
402	+	data := reconstructSampleData()
403	+	expected := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, "") // user + 3 agent turns
404	+
405	+	out, err := NewProvider().ReconstructSession(data, spi.ReconstructOptions{WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp/proj"})
406	+	if err != nil {
407	+		t.Fatalf("ReconstructSession: %v", err)
408	+	}
409	+	if out.SessionID == "" || filepath.Base(out.Filename) != "store.db" {
410	+		t.Fatalf("unexpected id/filename: %q / %q", out.SessionID, out.Filename)
411	+	}
412	+
413	+	blobs, meta := openStoreBytes(t, out.Content)
414	+
415	+	// Every blob is content-addressed by sha256.
416	+	for id, d := range blobs {
417	+		if sum := sha256.Sum256(d); hex.EncodeToString(sum[:]) != id {
418	+			t.Errorf("blob id %s != sha256(data)", id)
419	+		}
420	+	}
421	+
422	+	head, _ := meta["latestRootBlobId"].(string)
423	+	root, ok := blobs[head]
424	+	if !ok {
425	+		t.Fatalf("head %q not present in blobs", head)
426	+	}
427	+	if meta["agentId"] != out.SessionID {
428	+		t.Errorf("meta agentId %v != session id %q", meta["agentId"], out.SessionID)
429	+	}
430	+
431	+	// Root field 1 = message blobs in order; verify roles + text match the turns.
432	+	msgRefs := refsOf(root, 1)
433	+	if len(msgRefs) != len(expected) {
434	+		t.Fatalf("root references %d messages, want %d", len(msgRefs), len(expected))
435	+	}
436	+	for i, ref := range msgRefs {
437	+		var m struct {
438	+			Role    string `json:"role"`
439	+			Content []struct {
440	+				Text string `json:"text"`
441	+			} `json:"content"`
442	+		}
443	+		if err := json.Unmarshal(blobs[ref], &m); err != nil {
444	+			t.Fatalf("message %d not JSON: %v", i, err)
445	+		}
446	+		wantRole := "assistant"
447	+		if expected[i].Role == schema.RoleUser {
448	+			wantRole = "user"
449	+		}
450	+		if m.Role != wantRole {
451	+			t.Errorf("message %d role = %q, want %q", i, m.Role, wantRole)
452	+		}
453	+		if len(m.Content) == 0 || m.Content[0].Text != expected[i].Text {
454	+			t.Errorf("message %d text mismatch:\n got: %q\nwant: %q", i, m.Content, expected[i].Text)
455	+		}
456	+	}
457	+
458	+	// Root field 2 = metadata node, which must resolve.
459	+	metaRefs := refsOf(root, 2)
460	+	if len(metaRefs) != 1 || blobs[metaRefs[0]] == nil {
461	+		t.Errorf("root should reference exactly one metadata node; got %v", metaRefs)
462	+	}
463	+}
464	+
465	+func TestNativeSessionPath(t *testing.T) {
466	+	path, err := NewProvider().NativeSessionPath(t.TempDir(), filepath.Join("sess-id", "store.db"))
467	+	if err != nil {
468	+		t.Fatalf("NativeSessionPath: %v", err)
469	+	}
470	+	if !strings.Contains(path, filepath.Join(".cursor", "chats")) {
471	+		t.Errorf("path %q should be under .cursor/chats", path)
472	+	}
473	+	if !strings.HasSuffix(path, filepath.Join("sess-id", "store.db")) {

493	+// ListAllAgentChatSessions enumerates every DeepSeek TUI session in
494	+// ~/.deepseek/sessions/. DeepSeek sessions are not project-scoped by directory; the
495	+// originating workspace is recorded in each session's metadata.workspace field. See
496	+// docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
497	+func (p *Provider) ListAllAgentChatSessions() ([]spi.GlobalSessionRef, error) {
498	+	files, err := listSessionFiles()
499	+	if err != nil {
500	+		if os.IsNotExist(err) {
501	+			return []spi.GlobalSessionRef{}, nil
502	+		}
503	+		return nil, err
504	+	}
505	+
506	+	var refs []spi.GlobalSessionRef
507	+	for _, file := range files {
508	+		metadata, err := extractSessionMetadata(file.Path)
509	+		if err != nil {
510	+			slog.Debug("reindex: failed to extract deepseek session metadata", "path", file.Path, "error", err)
511	+			continue
512	+		}
513	+		if metadata == nil {
514	+			continue // empty session
515	+		}
516	+		refs = append(refs, spi.GlobalSessionRef{
517	+			SessionID:  metadata.SessionID,
518	+			CreatedAt:  metadata.CreatedAt,
519	+			Slug:       metadata.Slug,
520	+			Name:       metadata.Name,
521	+			NativePath: file.Path,
522	+			OriginCwd:  extractWorkspaceFast(file.Path),
523	+		})
524	+	}
525	+	return refs, nil
526	+}
527	diff --git a/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/deepseektui/reconstruct.go b/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/deepseektui/reconstruct.go
528	new file mode 100644
529	index 0000000..e1f5a12
530	--- /dev/null
531	+++ b/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/deepseektui/reconstruct.go
532	@@ -0,0 +1,108 @@
533	+package deepseektui
534	+
535	+import (
536	+	"bytes"
537	+	"encoding/json"
538	+	"fmt"
539	+	"path/filepath"
540	+	"strings"
541	+	"time"
542	+
543	+	"github.com/google/uuid"
544	+
545	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
546	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
547	+)
548	+
549	+// deepseekTimestamp formats a time the way DeepSeek records do (RFC3339 micros, Z).
550	+func deepseekTimestamp(t time.Time) string {
551	+	return t.Format("2006-01-02T15:04:05.000000Z")
552	+}
553	+
554	+// ReconstructSession rebuilds a DeepSeek TUI native session from the neutral
555	+// SessionData so `deepseek --resume <id>` can continue the conversation.
556	+//
557	+// DeepSeek stores a session as a single JSON document
558	+// `{schema_version, system_prompt, metadata, messages}` where each message is
559	+// `{role, content:[{type:"text",text}]}`. Tool calls and thinking are already
560	+// flattened into agent text by FlattenSessionData, so reconstruction emits only
561	+// `user`/`assistant` text messages. The session is associated with a project via
562	+// metadata.workspace. See docs/SESSION-PORTABILITY.md.
563	+func (p *Provider) ReconstructSession(data *schema.SessionData, opts spi.ReconstructOptions) (*spi.ReconstructedSession, error) {
564	+	if data == nil {
565	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("cannot reconstruct nil session data")
566	+	}
567	+
568	+	cwd := opts.WorkspaceRoot
569	+	if cwd == "" {
570	+		cwd = data.WorkspaceRoot
571	+	}
572	+
573	+	turns := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, opts.MigrationNote)
574	+	if len(turns) == 0 {
575	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("session has no content to reconstruct")
576	+	}
577	+
578	+	newID := uuid.NewString()
579	+	now := deepseekTimestamp(time.Now().UTC())
580	+
581	+	title := strings.TrimSpace(data.Slug)
582	+	if title == "" {
583	+		title = "Resumed session"
584	+	}
585	+
586	+	messages := make([]map[string]interface{}, 0, len(turns))
587	+	for _, turn := range turns {
588	+		role := "assistant"
589	+		if turn.Role == schema.RoleUser {
590	+			role = "user"
591	+		}
592	+		messages = append(messages, map[string]interface{}{
593	+			"role":    role,
594	+			"content": []map[string]interface{}{{"type": "text", "text": turn.Text}},
595	+		})
596	+	}
597	+
598	+	session := map[string]interface{}{
599	+		"schema_version": 1,
600	+		"system_prompt":  "",
601	+		"metadata": map[string]interface{}{
602	+			"id":            newID,
603	+			"title":         title,
604	+			"created_at":    now,
605	+			"updated_at":    now,
606	+			"message_count": len(messages),
607	+			"total_tokens":  0,
608	+			"model":         "",
609	+			"workspace":     cwd,
610	+			"mode":          "agent",
611	+		},
612	+		"messages": messages,
613	+	}
614	+
615	+	var buf bytes.Buffer
616	+	enc := json.NewEncoder(&buf)
617	+	enc.SetEscapeHTML(false)
618	+	enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
619	+	if err := enc.Encode(session); err != nil {
620	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode deepseek session: %w", err)
621	+	}
622	+
623	+	return &spi.ReconstructedSession{
624	+		SessionID: newID,
625	+		Filename:  newID + ".json",
626	+		Content:   buf.Bytes(),
627	+	}, nil
628	+}
629	+
630	+// NativeSessionPath returns where a reconstructed session belongs in DeepSeek's
631	+// store: ~/.deepseek/sessions/<filename>. DeepSeek sessions are not project-scoped
632	+// by directory (the project is recorded in metadata.workspace), so projectPath is
633	+// unused. The directory is not required to exist (the caller creates it).
634	+func (p *Provider) NativeSessionPath(projectPath string, filename string) (string, error) {
635	+	dir, err := resolveSessionsDir()
636	+	if err != nil {
637	+		return "", err
638	+	}
639	+	return filepath.Join(dir, filename), nil
640	+}

659	+func strptr(s string) *string { return &s }
660	+
661	+func reconstructSampleData() *schema.SessionData {
662	+	fm := "Ran `ls`:\n\n```\nfile.txt\n```"
663	+	return &schema.SessionData{
664	+		SchemaVersion: "1.0",
665	+		Provider:      schema.ProviderInfo{ID: "deepseek-tui", Name: "DeepSeek TUI", Version: "x"},
666	+		SessionID:     "orig-deepseek-123",
667	+		CreatedAt:     "2026-06-17T10:00:00.000Z",
668	+		WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp/proj",
669	+		Exchanges: []schema.Exchange{
670	+			{
671	+				ExchangeID: "orig-deepseek-123:0",
672	+				Messages: []schema.Message{
673	+					{Role: schema.RoleUser, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "List the files here."}}},
674	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "Listing files."}}},
675	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeThinking, Text: "Use ls."}}},
676	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Tool: &schema.ToolInfo{Name: "shell", Type: schema.ToolTypeShell, FormattedMarkdown: strptr(fm)}},
677	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "There is one file."}}},
678	+				},
679	+			},
680	+		},
681	+	}
682	+}

684	+// TestReconstructSession_RoundTrip reconstructs to native DeepSeek JSON, re-parses
685	+// it through the forward path, and asserts the flattened transcript is preserved.
686	+func TestReconstructSession_RoundTrip(t *testing.T) {
687	+	data := reconstructSampleData()
688	+	expected := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, "")
689	+
690	+	out, err := NewProvider().ReconstructSession(data, spi.ReconstructOptions{WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp/proj"})
691	+	if err != nil {
692	+		t.Fatalf("ReconstructSession: %v", err)
693	+	}
694	+	if out.SessionID == "" || !strings.HasSuffix(out.Filename, ".json") {
695	+		t.Fatalf("unexpected id/filename: %q / %q", out.SessionID, out.Filename)
696	+	}
697	+
698	+	var session dsSession
699	+	if err := json.Unmarshal(out.Content, &session); err != nil {
700	+		t.Fatalf("reconstructed content is not valid dsSession JSON: %v", err)
701	+	}
702	+	if session.Metadata.ID != out.SessionID {
703	+		t.Errorf("metadata.id = %q, want %q", session.Metadata.ID, out.SessionID)
704	+	}
705	+	if session.Metadata.Workspace != "/tmp/proj" {
706	+		t.Errorf("metadata.workspace = %q, want /tmp/proj", session.Metadata.Workspace)
707	+	}
708	+
709	+	regenerated, err := generateAgentSession(&session, "/tmp/proj")
710	+	if err != nil {
711	+		t.Fatalf("generateAgentSession: %v", err)
712	+	}
713	+
714	+	actual := spi.FlattenSessionData(regenerated, "")
715	+	if len(actual) != len(expected) {
716	+		t.Fatalf("round-trip produced %d turns, want %d\n got: %+v\nwant: %+v", len(actual), len(expected), actual, expected)
717	+	}
718	+	for i := range expected {
719	+		if actual[i] != expected[i] {
720	+			t.Errorf("turn %d mismatch:\n got: %+v\nwant: %+v", i, actual[i], expected[i])
721	+		}
722	+	}
723	+}
724	+

727	+	if err != nil {
728	+		t.Fatalf("NativeSessionPath: %v", err)
729	+	}
730	+	if !strings.Contains(path, filepath.Join(".deepseek", "sessions")) {
731	+		t.Errorf("path %q should be under .deepseek/sessions", path)
732	+	}
733	+	if !strings.HasSuffix(path, "abc.json") {
734	+		t.Errorf("path %q should end with the filename", path)
735	+	}
736	+}

742	+	}

753	+// ListAllAgentChatSessions enumerates every Factory Droid session across all projects
754	+// in ~/.factory/sessions/. The originating cwd is read from inside each session
755	+// (parseFactorySession resolves it into WorkspaceRoot, preferring the recorded cwd).
756	+// See docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
757	+func (p *Provider) ListAllAgentChatSessions() ([]spi.GlobalSessionRef, error) {
758	+	files, err := listSessionFiles()
759	+	if err != nil {
760	+		if os.IsNotExist(err) {
761	+			return []spi.GlobalSessionRef{}, nil
762	+		}
763	+		return nil, err
764	+	}
765	+
766	+	var refs []spi.GlobalSessionRef
767	+	for _, file := range files {
768	+		session, err := parseFactorySession(file.Path)
769	+		if err != nil {
770	+			slog.Debug("reindex: failed to parse droid session", "path", file.Path, "error", err)
771	+			continue
772	+		}
773	+		if session == nil || len(session.Blocks) == 0 {
774	+			continue // empty session
775	+		}
776	+		refs = append(refs, spi.GlobalSessionRef{
777	+			SessionID:  session.ID,
778	+			CreatedAt:  session.CreatedAt,
779	+			Slug:       session.Slug,
780	+			Name:       generateDroidSessionName(session),
781	+			NativePath: file.Path,
782	+			OriginCwd:  strings.TrimSpace(session.WorkspaceRoot),
783	+		})
784	+	}
785	+	return refs, nil
786	+}
787	diff --git a/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/droidcli/reconstruct.go b/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/droidcli/reconstruct.go
788	new file mode 100644
789	index 0000000..a9a32b0
790	--- /dev/null
791	+++ b/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/droidcli/reconstruct.go
792	@@ -0,0 +1,129 @@
793	+package droidcli
794	+
795	+import (
796	+	"bytes"
797	+	"encoding/json"
798	+	"fmt"
799	+	"os"
800	+	"path/filepath"
801	+	"strings"
802	+	"time"
803	+
804	+	"github.com/google/uuid"
805	+
806	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
807	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
808	+)
809	+
810	+// droidTimestamp formats a time the way Factory Droid records do (RFC3339 millis, Z).
811	+func droidTimestamp(t time.Time) string {
812	+	return t.Format("2006-01-02T15:04:05.000Z")
813	+}
814	+
815	+// ReconstructSession rebuilds a Factory Droid native JSONL session from the neutral
816	+// SessionData so `droid --resume <id>` can continue the conversation.
817	+//
818	+// Droid stores a session as a `session_start` header followed by `parentId`-chained
819	+// `message` records, each `{message:{role, content:[{type:"text",text}]}}`. Tool
820	+// calls and thinking are already flattened into agent text by FlattenSessionData,
821	+// so reconstruction emits only `user`/`assistant` text messages. The store is
822	+// project-scoped (see NativeSessionPath). See docs/SESSION-PORTABILITY.md.
823	+func (p *Provider) ReconstructSession(data *schema.SessionData, opts spi.ReconstructOptions) (*spi.ReconstructedSession, error) {
824	+	if data == nil {
825	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("cannot reconstruct nil session data")
826	+	}
827	+
828	+	cwd := opts.WorkspaceRoot
829	+	if cwd == "" {
830	+		cwd = data.WorkspaceRoot
831	+	}
832	+
833	+	turns := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, opts.MigrationNote)
834	+	if len(turns) == 0 {
835	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("session has no content to reconstruct")
836	+	}
837	+
838	+	newID := uuid.NewString()
839	+	base := time.Now().UTC()
840	+
841	+	title := strings.TrimSpace(data.Slug)
842	+	if title == "" {
843	+		title = "Resumed session"
844	+	}
845	+
846	+	var buf bytes.Buffer
847	+	enc := json.NewEncoder(&buf)
848	+	enc.SetEscapeHTML(false)
849	+
850	+	// session_start header (also carries provenance).
851	+	if err := enc.Encode(map[string]interface{}{
852	+		"type":                      "session_start",
853	+		"id":                        newID,
854	+		"title":                     title,
855	+		"sessionTitle":              title,
856	+		"owner":                     os.Getenv("USER"),
857	+		"version":                   2,
858	+		"cwd":                       cwd,
859	+		"isSessionTitleManuallySet": false,
860	+		"sessionTitleAutoStage":     "first_message",
861	+		"specstorySourceSessionId":  data.SessionID,
862	+	}); err != nil {
863	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode session_start: %w", err)
864	+	}
865	+
866	+	var prevID string
867	+	for i, turn := range turns {
868	+		msgID := uuid.NewString()
869	+		role := "assistant"
870	+		if turn.Role == schema.RoleUser {
871	+			role = "user"
872	+		}
873	+
874	+		rec := map[string]interface{}{
875	+			"type":      "message",
876	+			"id":        msgID,
877	+			"timestamp": droidTimestamp(base.Add(time.Duration(i) * time.Second)),
878	+			"message": map[string]interface{}{
879	+				"role":    role,
880	+				"content": []map[string]interface{}{{"type": "text", "text": turn.Text}},
881	+			},
882	+		}
883	+		// Linear chain: the first message roots the thread (no parentId).
884	+		if prevID != "" {
885	+			rec["parentId"] = prevID
886	+		}
887	+
888	+		if err := enc.Encode(rec); err != nil {
889	+			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode message: %w", err)
890	+		}
891	+		prevID = msgID
892	+	}
893	+
894	+	return &spi.ReconstructedSession{
895	+		SessionID: newID,
896	+		Filename:  newID + ".jsonl",
897	+		Content:   buf.Bytes(),
898	+	}, nil
899	+}
900	+
901	+// NativeSessionPath returns where a reconstructed session belongs in Droid's store:
902	+// ~/.factory/sessions/<cwd-encoded>/<filename>. The directory is not required to
903	+// exist (the caller creates it).
904	+func (p *Provider) NativeSessionPath(projectPath string, filename string) (string, error) {
905	+	if projectPath == "" {
906	+		var err error
907	+		projectPath, err = os.Getwd()
908	+		if err != nil {
909	+			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get current working directory: %w", err)
910	+		}
911	+	}
912	+
913	+	dir, err := resolveProjectSessionDir(projectPath)
914	+	if err != nil {
915	+		return "", err
916	+	}
917	+	if dir == "" {
918	+		return "", fmt.Errorf("droidcli: cannot resolve session directory for project path")
919	+	}
920	+	return filepath.Join(dir, filename), nil
921	+}

956	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeThinking, Text: "Need a main function."}}},
957	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Tool: &schema.ToolInfo{Name: "Create", Type: schema.ToolTypeWrite, FormattedMarkdown: strptr(fm)}},

965	+// TestReconstructSession_RoundTrip reconstructs to native Droid JSONL, re-parses it
966	+// through the forward path, and asserts the flattened transcript is preserved.
967	+func TestReconstructSession_RoundTrip(t *testing.T) {
968	+	data := reconstructSampleData()
969	+	expected := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, "")
970	+
971	+	out, err := NewProvider().ReconstructSession(data, spi.ReconstructOptions{WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp/proj"})
972	+	if err != nil {
973	+		t.Fatalf("ReconstructSession: %v", err)
974	+	}
975	+	if out.SessionID == "" || !strings.HasSuffix(out.Filename, ".jsonl") {
976	+		t.Fatalf("unexpected id/filename: %q / %q", out.SessionID, out.Filename)
977	+	}
978	+
979	+	tmp := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), out.Filename)
980	+	if err := os.WriteFile(tmp, out.Content, 0o644); err != nil {
981	+		t.Fatalf("write temp: %v", err)
982	+	}
983	+	session, err := parseFactorySession(tmp)
984	+	if err != nil {
985	+		t.Fatalf("parseFactorySession: %v", err)
986	+	}
987	+	regenerated, err := GenerateAgentSession(session, "/tmp/proj")
988	+	if err != nil {
989	+		t.Fatalf("GenerateAgentSession: %v", err)
990	+	}
991	+
992	+	actual := spi.FlattenSessionData(regenerated, "")
993	+	if len(actual) != len(expected) {
994	+		t.Fatalf("round-trip produced %d turns, want %d\n got: %+v\nwant: %+v", len(actual), len(expected), actual, expected)
995	+	}
996	+	for i := range expected {
997	+		if actual[i] != expected[i] {
998	+			t.Errorf("turn %d mismatch:\n got: %+v\nwant: %+v", i, actual[i], expected[i])
999	+		}
1000	+	}
1001	+}

1027	--- a/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/geminicli/provider.go

1033	+
1034	+// ListAllAgentChatSessions enumerates every session in this provider's native store,
1035	+// regardless of project. See docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
1036	+//
1037	+// Gemini associates a tmp directory (~/.gemini/tmp/<hash>/) with a project via a
1038	+// .project_root marker file holding the project path, which is the originating cwd
1039	+// for every session in that directory.
1040	+func (p *Provider) ListAllAgentChatSessions() ([]spi.GlobalSessionRef, error) {
1041	+	tmpDir, err := GetGeminiTmpDir()
1042	+	if err != nil {
1043	+		return nil, err
1044	+	}
1045	+	entries, err := os.ReadDir(tmpDir)
1046	+	if err != nil {
1047	+		if os.IsNotExist(err) {
1048	+			return []spi.GlobalSessionRef{}, nil
1049	+		}
1050	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to read Gemini tmp directory: %w", err)
1051	+	}
1052	+
1053	+	var refs []spi.GlobalSessionRef
1054	+	for _, entry := range entries {
1055	+		if !entry.IsDir() {
1056	+			continue
1057	+		}
1058	+		dir := filepath.Join(tmpDir, entry.Name())
1059	+
1060	+		// The .project_root marker holds the project path — the originating cwd shared
1061	+		// by every session in this tmp directory.
1062	+		var cwd string
1063	+		if content, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(dir, ".project_root")); err == nil {
1064	+			cwd = strings.TrimSpace(string(content))
1065	+		}
1066	+
1067	+		sessions, err := FindSessions(dir)
1068	+		if err != nil {
1069	+			slog.Debug("reindex: failed to find gemini sessions", "dir", dir, "error", err)
1070	+			continue
1071	+		}
1072	+		for _, session := range sessions {
1073	+			metadata := extractGeminiSessionMetadata(session)
1074	+			if metadata == nil {
1075	+				continue // empty session
1076	+			}
1077	+			refs = append(refs, spi.GlobalSessionRef{
1078	+				SessionID:  metadata.SessionID,
1079	+				CreatedAt:  metadata.CreatedAt,
1080	+				Slug:       metadata.Slug,
1081	+				Name:       metadata.Name,
1082	+				NativePath: session.FilePath,
1083	+				OriginCwd:  cwd,
1084	+			})
1085	+		}
1086	+	}
1087	+	return refs, nil
1088	+}
1089	diff --git a/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/geminicli/reconstruct.go b/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/geminicli/reconstruct.go
1090	new file mode 100644
1091	index 0000000..97537e3
1092	--- /dev/null
1093	+++ b/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/geminicli/reconstruct.go
1094	@@ -0,0 +1,158 @@
1095	+package geminicli
1096	+
1097	+import (
1098	+	"bytes"
1099	+	"encoding/json"
1100	+	"fmt"
1101	+	"os"
1102	+	"path/filepath"
1103	+	"time"
1104	+
1105	+	"github.com/google/uuid"
1106	+
1107	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
1108	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
1109	+)
1110	+
1111	+// geminiTimestamp formats a time the way Gemini chat records do (RFC3339 millis, Z).
1112	+func geminiTimestamp(t time.Time) string {
1113	+	return t.Format("2006-01-02T15:04:05.000Z")
1114	+}
1115	+
1116	+// ReconstructSession rebuilds a Gemini CLI native chat session from the neutral
1117	+// SessionData so `gemini --resume <id>` can continue the conversation.
1118	+//
1119	+// Gemini stores a session as a single JSON document: a top-level object with
1120	+// `sessionId`/`projectHash`/`startTime`/`lastUpdated`/`kind` and a `messages`
1121	+// array. Each message is `{id, timestamp, type, content}` where a user message's
1122	+// content is `[{text}]` and a gemini message's content is a plain string. Tool
1123	+// calls and thinking are already flattened into agent text by FlattenSessionData,
1124	+// so reconstruction emits only `user`/`gemini` text messages. See
1125	+// docs/SESSION-PORTABILITY.md.
1126	+func (p *Provider) ReconstructSession(data *schema.SessionData, opts spi.ReconstructOptions) (*spi.ReconstructedSession, error) {
1127	+	if data == nil {
1128	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("cannot reconstruct nil session data")
1129	+	}
1130	+
1131	+	cwd := opts.WorkspaceRoot
1132	+	if cwd == "" {
1133	+		cwd = data.WorkspaceRoot
1134	+	}
1135	+
1136	+	turns := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, opts.MigrationNote)
1137	+	if len(turns) == 0 {
1138	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("session has no content to reconstruct")
1139	+	}
1140	+
1141	+	newID := uuid.NewString()
1142	+	base := time.Now().UTC()
1143	+
1144	+	// Best-effort project hash; Gemini stores it but resume keys off the project dir.
1145	+	projectHash, _ := HashProjectPath(cwd)
1146	+
1147	+	messages := make([]map[string]interface{}, 0, len(turns))
1148	+	for i, turn := range turns {
1149	+		msg := map[string]interface{}{
1150	+			"id":        uuid.NewString(),
1151	+			"timestamp": geminiTimestamp(base.Add(time.Duration(i) * time.Second)),
1152	+		}
1153	+		if turn.Role == schema.RoleUser {
1154	+			msg["type"] = "user"
1155	+			msg["content"] = []map[string]interface{}{{"text": turn.Text}}
1156	+		} else {
1157	+			msg["type"] = "gemini"
1158	+			msg["content"] = turn.Text
1159	+		}
1160	+		messages = append(messages, msg)
1161	+	}
1162	+
1163	+	session := map[string]interface{}{
1164	+		"sessionId":   newID,
1165	+		"projectHash": projectHash,
1166	+		"startTime":   geminiTimestamp(base),
1167	+		"lastUpdated": geminiTimestamp(base.Add(time.Duration(len(turns)) * time.Second)),
1168	+		"kind":        "main",
1169	+		"messages":    messages,
1170	+	}
1171	+
1172	+	var buf bytes.Buffer
1173	+	enc := json.NewEncoder(&buf)
1174	+	enc.SetEscapeHTML(false)
1175	+	enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
1176	+	if err := enc.Encode(session); err != nil {
1177	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode gemini session: %w", err)
1178	+	}
1179	+
1180	+	// Filename: session-<YYYY-MM-DDTHH-MM>-<first 8 of id>.json (Gemini convention).
1181	+	filename := fmt.Sprintf("session-%s-%s.json", base.Format("2006-01-02T15-04"), newID[:8])
1182	+
1183	+	return &spi.ReconstructedSession{
1184	+		SessionID: newID,
1185	+		Filename:  filename,
1186	+		Content:   buf.Bytes(),
1187	+	}, nil
1188	+}
1189	+
1190	+// NativeSessionPath returns where a reconstructed chat belongs in Gemini's store
1191	+// and, unlike the Claude/Codex resolvers, prepares the project directory: Gemini
1192	+// associates a tmp dir with a project via a `.project_root` marker, so for a
1193	+// project Gemini has never seen we create `~/.gemini/tmp/<name>/` with that marker
1194	+// and a `chats/` subdir. Returns `<projectDir>/chats/<filename>`.
1195	+func (p *Provider) NativeSessionPath(projectPath string, filename string) (string, error) {
1196	+	if projectPath == "" {
1197	+		var err error
1198	+		projectPath, err = osGetwd()
1199	+		if err != nil {
1200	+			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get current working directory: %w", err)
1201	+		}
1202	+	}
1203	+
1204	+	// Reuse the project dir Gemini already associates with this project, if any.
1205	+	dir, err := ResolveGeminiProjectDir(projectPath)
1206	+	if err != nil {
1207	+		dir, err = prepareGeminiProjectDir(projectPath)
1208	+		if err != nil {
1209	+			return "", err
1210	+		}
1211	+	}
1212	+
1213	+	return filepath.Join(dir, "chats", filename), nil
1214	+}
1215	+
1216	+// prepareGeminiProjectDir creates a Gemini project directory for a project that
1217	+// Gemini has not seen yet: ~/.gemini/tmp/<basename>/ (falling back to the hash dir
1218	+// if that basename is taken by a different project), with a `.project_root` marker
1219	+// and a `chats/` subdirectory.
1220	+func prepareGeminiProjectDir(projectPath string) (string, error) {
1221	+	tmpDir, err := GetGeminiTmpDir()
1222	+	if err != nil {
1223	+		return "", err
1224	+	}
1225	+	if err := os.MkdirAll(tmpDir, 0o755); err != nil {
1226	+		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to create gemini tmp dir: %w", err)
1227	+	}
1228	+
1229	+	canonical := canonicalizeProjectPath(projectPath)
1230	+	dir := filepath.Join(tmpDir, filepath.Base(projectPath))
1231	+
1232	+	// If the basename dir exists but belongs to a different project, key off the hash instead.
1233	+	if info, statErr := osStat(dir); statErr == nil && info.IsDir() && !matchesProjectRoot(dir, canonical) {
1234	+		if dir, err = GetGeminiProjectDir(projectPath); err != nil {
1235	+			return "", err
1236	+		}
1237	+	}
1238	+
1239	+	if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(dir, "chats"), 0o755); err != nil {
1240	+		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to create gemini chats dir: %w", err)
1241	+	}
1242	+
1243	+	// Write the project-root marker so Gemini maps this dir to the project on resume.
1244	+	rootFile := filepath.Join(dir, ".project_root")
1245	+	if _, statErr := osStat(rootFile); os.IsNotExist(statErr) {
1246	+		if err := os.WriteFile(rootFile, []byte(canonical), 0o644); err != nil {
1247	+			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to write gemini .project_root: %w", err)
1248	+		}
1249	+	}
1250	+
1251	+	return dir, nil
1252	+}

1273	+

1276	+	return &schema.SessionData{
1277	+		SchemaVersion: "1.0",
1278	+		Provider:      schema.ProviderInfo{ID: "gemini-cli", Name: "Gemini CLI", Version: "x"},
1279	+		SessionID:     "orig-gemini-123",
1280	+		CreatedAt:     "2026-06-17T10:00:00.000Z",
1281	+		WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp/proj",

1286	+					{Role: schema.RoleUser, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "Write hello world in Forth."}}},
1287	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "I'll create hello.fs."}}},
1288	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeThinking, Text: "Forth uses dot-quote for strings."}}},
1289	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Tool: &schema.ToolInfo{Name: "write_file", Type: schema.ToolTypeWrite, FormattedMarkdown: strptr(fm)}},
1290	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "Created hello.fs."}}},
1291	+				},
1292	+			},
1293	+		},
1294	+	}
1295	+}

1297	+// TestReconstructSession_RoundTrip reconstructs to native Gemini chat JSON, re-parses
1298	+// it through the forward path, and asserts the flattened transcript is preserved.
1299	+func TestReconstructSession_RoundTrip(t *testing.T) {
1300	+	data := reconstructSampleData()
1301	+	expected := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, "")
1302	+
1303	+	out, err := NewProvider().ReconstructSession(data, spi.ReconstructOptions{WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp/proj"})
1304	+	if err != nil {
1305	+		t.Fatalf("ReconstructSession: %v", err)
1306	+	}
1307	+	if out.SessionID == "" {
1308	+		t.Fatal("expected a fresh session ID")
1309	+	}
1310	+	if !strings.HasPrefix(out.Filename, "session-") || !strings.HasSuffix(out.Filename, ".json") {
1311	+		t.Errorf("filename %q should look like session-<ts>-<id>.json", out.Filename)
1312	+	}
1313	+
1314	+	var session GeminiSession
1315	+	if err := json.Unmarshal(out.Content, &session); err != nil {
1316	+		t.Fatalf("reconstructed content is not valid GeminiSession JSON: %v", err)
1317	+	}
1318	+	if session.ID != out.SessionID {
1319	+		t.Errorf("session sessionId = %q, want %q", session.ID, out.SessionID)
1320	+	}
1321	+
1322	+	regenerated, err := GenerateAgentSession(&session, "/tmp/proj")
1323	+	if err != nil {
1324	+		t.Fatalf("GenerateAgentSession: %v", err)
1325	+	}
1326	+
1327	+	actual := spi.FlattenSessionData(regenerated, "")
1328	+	if len(actual) != len(expected) {
1329	+		t.Fatalf("round-trip produced %d turns, want %d\n got: %+v\nwant: %+v", len(actual), len(expected), actual, expected)
1330	+	}
1331	+	for i := range expected {
1332	+		if actual[i] != expected[i] {
1333	+			t.Errorf("turn %d mismatch:\n got: %+v\nwant: %+v", i, actual[i], expected[i])
1334	+		}
1335	+	}
1336	+}
1337	+
1338	+// TestNativeSessionPath_CreatesProjectDir verifies that, for a project Gemini has
1339	+// not seen, NativeSessionPath creates the tmp project dir with a .project_root
1340	+// marker and returns a path under chats/.
1341	+func TestNativeSessionPath_CreatesProjectDir(t *testing.T) {
1342	+	home := t.TempDir()
1343	+	project := t.TempDir()
1344	+
1345	+	originalHome := osUserHomeDir
1346	+	osUserHomeDir = func() (string, error) { return home, nil }
1347	+	t.Cleanup(func() { osUserHomeDir = originalHome })
1348	+
1349	+	path, err := NewProvider().NativeSessionPath(project, "session-x.json")
1350	+	if err != nil {
1351	+		t.Fatalf("NativeSessionPath: %v", err)
1352	+	}
1353	+	if filepath.Base(path) != "session-x.json" {
1354	+		t.Errorf("path %q should end with the filename", path)
1355	+	}
1356	+	if filepath.Base(filepath.Dir(path)) != "chats" {
1357	+		t.Errorf("path %q should be under a chats/ dir", path)
1358	+	}
1359	+	// The .project_root marker should exist alongside chats/ and point at the project.
1360	+	rootFile := filepath.Join(filepath.Dir(filepath.Dir(path)), ".project_root")
1361	+	content, err := os.ReadFile(rootFile)
1362	+	if err != nil {
1363	+		t.Fatalf("expected .project_root marker: %v", err)
1364	+	}
1365	+	if !strings.Contains(string(content), filepath.Base(project)) {
1366	+		t.Errorf(".project_root %q should reference the project path", string(content))
1367	+	}
1368	+}
1369	+
1370	+func TestReconstructSession_Empty(t *testing.T) {
1371	+	_, err := NewProvider().ReconstructSession(&schema.SessionData{SessionID: "x", WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp"}, spi.ReconstructOptions{})
1372	+	if err == nil {
1373	+		t.Fatal("expected error reconstructing a session with no content")
1374	+	}
1375	+}
