33	+// sessionTUIResult is what the picker hands back: the chosen session and target agent,
34	+// or a cancel. It is read off the final model after the program exits.
35	+type sessionTUIResult struct {
36	+	session   *sessionindex.Session
37	+	targetID  string
38	+	cancelled bool
39	+}
40	+
41	+// tuiMode is the picker's top-level screen.
42	+type tuiMode int
43	+
44	+const (
45	+	modeList     tuiMode = iota // browsing the session list (current or a drilled-in project)
46	+	modeTarget                  // choosing which agent to resume into
47	+	modeProjects                // the all-projects browser (Stage B)
48	+)
49	+
50	+// agentMeta carries an agent's display name + accent color for the list.
51	+type agentMeta struct {
52	+	name   string
53	+	accent color.Color
54	+}
55	+
56	+// sessionTUI is the shared model behind BOTH `specstory resume` and `specstory search`.
57	+// The two commands are the same UI with different entry points: resume opens on the current
58	+// project's session list; search opens straight into the all-projects FTS with the input
59	+// focused. Everything else — keys, preview, agent filter, dense/sparse, the target-agent
60	+// step — is identical by construction. See docs/RESUME-TUI.md and docs/SESSION-SEARCH.md.
61	+type sessionTUI struct {
62	+	store       *sessionindex.Store
63	+	registry    *factory.Registry // re-parses a session for the glamour preview
64	+	title       string            // header title, e.g. "SpecStory Resume" / "SpecStory Search"
65	+	projectID   string
66	+	projectName string
67	+	agents      map[string]agentMeta // provider id -> display meta
68	+	installed   []agentChoice        // installed agents, for the target step
69	+	presetTo    string               // pre-selected target (from `resume <agent>`), or ""
70	+	lastAgent   string               // default target (last resumed), or ""
71	+
72	+	// homeProjectID/Name + homeSessions are the current directory's project; the picker
73	+	// can drill into other projects via the browser and toggle back to "home" with tab.
74	+	homeProjectID   string
75	+	homeProjectName string
76	+	homeSessions    []sessionindex.Session
77	+
78	+	all              []sessionindex.Session // sessions for the active project (projectID), newest first
79	+	filtered         []sessionindex.Session // after agent filter + search
80	+	filteredSnippets map[string]string      // match snippets for visible filtered rows, keyed by agent/session
81	+	cursor           int                    // index into filtered
82	+	top              int                    // first visible row (scroll)
83	+	inBrowser        bool                   // the active session list was reached via the browser
84	+
85	+	agentCycle  []string // "" (all) followed by each present agent id
86	+	agentFilter string   // "" = all
87	+	viewMode    string   // "dense" | "sparse"
88	+
89	+	// all-projects browser (Stage B)
90	+	projects         []sessionindex.ProjectSummary // all projects, most recent first
91	+	projFiltered     []sessionindex.ProjectSummary // after project-name search
92	+	projCursor       int
93	+	projTop          int
94	+	projectsLoaded   bool
95	+	startedInBrowser bool // launched straight into the browser (empty home project)
96	+	startedInSearch  bool // launched straight into the all-projects search (`specstory search`)
97	+	projSearching    bool
98	+	projSearch       textinput.Model
99	+	projSearchQuery  string
100	+
101	+	// global session search: FTS across all projects, or scoped to a single project with tab
102	+	// (the highlighted hit's project, else the current directory's). Opened with / in the
103	+	// browser or as `search`.
104	+	globalActive    bool
105	+	globalSearching bool
106	+	globalScopeID   string // "" = all projects; else the project id the search is scoped to
107	+	globalScopeName string // display name for the scoped project
108	+	globalInput     textinput.Model
109	+	globalQuery     string
110	+	globalResults   []sessionindex.Session
111	+	globalSnippets  map[string]string
112	+	globalCursor    int
113	+	globalTop       int
114	+
115	+	searching   bool
116	+	search      textinput.Model
117	+	searchQuery string
118	+	searchSeq   int // bumped per search keystroke; debounced FTS results must match it
119	+	snippetSeq  int // bumped per lazy snippet request; stale snippet results are discarded
120	+	// searchCancel aborts the in-flight FTS query when a newer keystroke supersedes it,
121	+	// freeing the database connection (a broad prefix query can rank the whole corpus).
122	+	searchCancel context.CancelFunc
123	+
124	+	// previewing shows a glamour-rendered, scrollable reader for the highlighted session,
125	+	// identical to search's reader (see openPreview / renderPreview).
126	+	previewing    bool
127	+	reader        viewport.Model
128	+	readerSession *sessionindex.Session
129	+
130	+	mode         tuiMode

133	+

137	+
138	+// sessionTUIOpts carries the per-command entry configuration. The only real differences
139	+// between `resume` and `search` live here: the header title, what the positional arg means
140	+// (presetTo vs. initialQuery), and whether to open in the all-projects search.
141	+type sessionTUIOpts struct {
142	+	title         string // header title ("SpecStory Resume" / "SpecStory Search")
143	+	presetTo      string // resume: pre-selected target agent (from `resume <agent>`)
144	+	lastAgent     string // default target (last resumed), or ""
145	+	viewMode      string // "dense" | "sparse"
146	+	initialQuery  string // search: pre-seed the all-projects query
147	+	startInSearch bool   // search: open in the all-projects FTS with the input focused
148	+}
149	+
150	+func newSessionTUI(store *sessionindex.Store, registry *factory.Registry, projectID, projectName string,
151	+	sessions []sessionindex.Session, agents map[string]agentMeta, installed []agentChoice, opts sessionTUIOpts) sessionTUI {
152	+
153	+	ti := textinput.New()
154	+	ti.Prompt = "/ "
155	+	pi := textinput.New()
156	+	pi.Prompt = "p "
157	+	gi := textinput.New()
158	+	gi.Prompt = "/ "
159	+
160	+	m := sessionTUI{
161	+		store:           store,
162	+		registry:        registry,
163	+		title:           opts.title,
164	+		reader:          viewport.New(),
165	+		projectID:       projectID,
166	+		projectName:     projectName,
167	+		homeProjectID:   projectID,
168	+		homeProjectName: projectName,
169	+		homeSessions:    sessions,
170	+		agents:          agents,
171	+		installed:       installed,
172	+		presetTo:        opts.presetTo,
173	+		lastAgent:       opts.lastAgent,
174	+		all:             sessions,
175	+		viewMode:        opts.viewMode,
176	+		search:          ti,
177	+		projSearch:      pi,
178	+		globalInput:     gi,
179	+	}
180	+	m.rebuildAgentCycle()
181	+	m.applyFilter()
182	+
183	+	switch {
184	+	case opts.startInSearch:
185	+		// `search`: land directly in the all-projects FTS, input focused (so the user types
186	+		// immediately, exactly like before). Init fires the pre-seeded query, if any. The
187	+		// search IS the root view here, so esc quits rather than unwinding into the browser.
188	+		m.startedInSearch = true
189	+		m.enterBrowser()
190	+		m.globalActive = true
191	+		m.globalSearching = true
192	+		m.globalQuery = opts.initialQuery
193	+		m.globalInput.SetValue(opts.initialQuery)
194	+		m.globalInput.Focus() // focus state persists on the stored model; Init returns the blink+query cmds
195	+		if queryReady(m.globalQuery) {
196	+			m.searchSeq = 1
197	+		}
198	+	case len(sessions) == 0:
199	+		// `resume` with an empty current project → open straight into the all-projects browser.
200	+		m.startedInBrowser = true
201	+		m.enterBrowser()
202	+	}
203	+	return m
204	+}
205	+
206	+func (m sessionTUI) Init() tea.Cmd {
207	+	// Search starts focused in the all-projects input; kick the blink and any pre-seeded query.
208	+	if m.globalSearching {
209	+		cmds := []tea.Cmd{m.globalInput.Focus()}
210	+		if queryReady(m.globalQuery) {
211	+			cmds = append(cmds, searchDebounce(m.searchSeq, modeProjects))
212	+		}
213	+		return tea.Batch(cmds...)
214	+	}
215	+	return nil
216	+}
217	+
218	+func (m sessionTUI) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
219	+	switch msg := msg.(type) {
220	+	case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
221	+		m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
222	+		m.reader.SetWidth(m.width)
223	+		m.reader.SetHeight(m.previewHeight())
224	+		return m, nil
225	+	case indexWarmedMsg:
226	+		return m.refreshAfterWarm()
227	+	case searchDebounceMsg:
228	+		// Fire the actual query only if no newer keystroke has arrived (debounce).
229	+		if msg.seq == m.searchSeq {
230	+			if m.searchCancel != nil {
231	+				m.searchCancel() // abort any prior in-flight query, freeing its connection
232	+			}
233	+			ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
234	+			m.searchCancel = cancel
235	+			return m, m.runSearch(msg.seq, msg.kind, ctx)
236	+		}
237	+		return m, nil
238	+	case searchResultMsg:
239	+		// Apply only the latest query's results (discard stale async results).
240	+		if msg.seq == m.searchSeq {
241	+			m.applySearchResults(msg.kind, msg.sessions)
242	+			return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(msg.kind)
243	+		}
244	+		return m, nil
245	+	case snippetResultMsg:
246	+		if msg.seq == m.snippetSeq {
247	+			if msg.kind == modeProjects {
248	+				if m.globalSnippets == nil {
249	+					m.globalSnippets = map[string]string{}
250	+				}
251	+				for key, snip := range msg.snippets {
252	+					m.globalSnippets[key] = snip
253	+				}
254	+			} else {
255	+				if m.filteredSnippets == nil {
256	+					m.filteredSnippets = map[string]string{}
257	+				}
258	+				for key, snip := range msg.snippets {
259	+					m.filteredSnippets[key] = snip
260	+				}
261	+			}
262	+		}
263	+		return m, nil
264	+	case tea.KeyPressMsg:
265	+		switch {
266	+		case m.previewing:
267	+			// The preview is a top-level overlay: it opens over the list OR the global
268	+			// results, so it must be checked before any mode-specific routing.
269	+			return m.updatePreview(msg)
270	+		case m.mode == modeTarget:
271	+			return m.updateTarget(msg)
272	+		case m.mode == modeProjects:
273	+			switch {
274	+			case m.globalSearching:
275	+				return m.updateGlobalSearch(msg)
276	+			case m.globalActive:
277	+				return m.updateGlobalResults(msg)
278	+			case m.projSearching:
279	+				return m.updateProjectSearch(msg)
280	+			default:
281	+				return m.updateProjects(msg)
282	+			}
283	+		case m.searching:
284	+			return m.updateSearch(msg)
285	+		default:
286	+			return m.updateList(msg)
287	+		}
288	+	}
289	+	return m, nil
290	+}
291	+
292	+// refreshAfterWarm re-queries the index after the background current-project warm pass lands,
293	+// so newly created or changed sessions for THIS project appear without a restart. It refreshes
294	+// in place only when the home project's session list is what's on screen (preserving the
295	+// cursor by session id), or re-runs a settled cross-project search to fold in the fresh rows.
296	+// In any other view it just caches the refreshed sessions for when the user returns home.
297	+func (m sessionTUI) refreshAfterWarm() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
298	+	sessions, err := m.store.ListByProject(m.homeProjectID)
299	+	if err != nil {
300	+		slog.Debug("resume: refresh after warm failed", "error", err)
301	+		return m, nil
302	+	}
303	+	m.homeSessions = sessions
304	+
305	+	// In-place refresh when the home project's list is the active view.
306	+	if m.mode == modeList && !m.inBrowser && m.projectID == m.homeProjectID {
307	+		selID := ""
308	+		if sel := m.selected(); sel != nil {
309	+			selID = sel.SessionID
310	+		}
311	+		m.all = sessions
312	+		m.rebuildAgentCycle()
313	+		m.applyFilter()
314	+		m.cursor = indexOfSession(m.filtered, selID)
315	+		m.clampScroll()
316	+		return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeList)
317	+	}
318	+
319	+	// A settled cross-project search (not mid-typing): re-run it so refreshed rows show up.
320	+	// While typing, the user's own debounced queries already see the fresh data.
321	+	if m.mode == modeProjects && m.globalActive && !m.globalSearching && queryReady(m.globalQuery) {
322	+		m.searchSeq++
323	+		return m, searchDebounce(m.searchSeq, modeProjects)
324	+	}
325	+	return m, nil
326	+}
327	+
328	+// indexOfSession returns the position of the session with id in list, or 0 when not present
329	+// (so a refreshed list with a deleted selection lands safely at the top).
330	+func indexOfSession(list []sessionindex.Session, id string) int {
331	+	if id == "" {
332	+		return 0
333	+	}
334	+	for i := range list {
335	+		if list[i].SessionID == id {
336	+			return i
337	+		}
338	+	}
339	+	return 0
340	+}
341	+
342	+// updateList handles keys while browsing the session list.
343	+func (m sessionTUI) updateList(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
344	+	switch msg.String() {
345	+	case "q", "ctrl+c":
346	+		m.result = sessionTUIResult{cancelled: true}
347	+		return m, tea.Quit
348	+	case "esc":
349	+		// In a drilled-in project, esc returns to the browser; at home it quits.
350	+		if m.inBrowser {
351	+			m.enterBrowser()
352	+			return m, nil
353	+		}
354	+		m.result = sessionTUIResult{cancelled: true}
355	+		return m, tea.Quit
356	+	case "tab":
357	+		// Toggle scope: home list ↔ all-projects browser.
358	+		m.enterBrowser()
359	+		return m, nil
360	+	case "up", "k":
361	+		m.moveCursor(-1)
362	+		return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeList)
363	+	case "down", "j":
364	+		m.moveCursor(1)
365	+		return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeList)
366	+	case "pgup":
367	+		m.moveCursor(-m.listHeight())
368	+		return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeList)
369	+	case "pgdown":
370	+		m.moveCursor(m.listHeight())
371	+		return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeList)
372	+	case "home", "g":
373	+		m.cursor, m.top = 0, 0
374	+		return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeList)
375	+	case "end", "G":
376	+		m.cursor = len(m.filtered) - 1
377	+		m.clampScroll()
378	+		return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeList)
379	+	case "r":
380	+		// Resume the highlighted session (enter is deliberately a no-op here, so a stray
381	+		// return can't accidentally launch an agent).
382	+		if sel := m.selected(); sel != nil {
383	+			return m.beginResume(sel)
384	+		}
385	+	case " ", "space":
386	+		if sel := m.selected(); sel != nil {
387	+			return m.openPreview(sel)
388	+		}
389	+	case "/":
390	+		m.searching = true
391	+		m.search.SetValue(m.searchQuery)
392	+		return m, m.search.Focus()
393	+	case "a":
394	+		return m, m.cycleAgent()
395	+	case "v":
396	+		m.toggleViewMode()
397	+		return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeList)
398	+	}
399	+	return m, nil
400	+}
401	+
402	+// updateSearch handles the full-text search input. The typed character is applied to the
403	+// input immediately (instant feedback); the FTS query runs async + debounced so a slow
404	+// query never blocks typing. See searchDebounceMsg / searchResultMsg.
405	+func (m sessionTUI) updateSearch(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
406	+	switch msg.String() {
407	+	case "esc":
408	+		m.searching = false
409	+		m.search.Blur()
410	+		m.searchQuery = ""
411	+		m.searchSeq++ // invalidate any in-flight search
412	+		m.snippetSeq++
413	+		m.applyFilter()
414	+		return m, nil
415	+	case "enter":
416	+		m.searching = false
417	+		m.search.Blur()
418	+		return m, nil
419	+	case "up", "down", "pgup", "pgdown":
420	+		// Arrows do nothing inside the one-line input, so commit the search and let the same
421	+		// key move the now-focused list — the user reaches a result without a separate enter.
422	+		m.searching = false
423	+		m.search.Blur()
424	+		return m.updateList(msg)
425	+	}
426	+	var cmd tea.Cmd
427	+	m.search, cmd = m.search.Update(msg)
428	+	m.searchQuery = m.search.Value()
429	+	m.searchSeq++
430	+	if !queryReady(m.searchQuery) {
431	+		// Too short to search: show the full (agent-filtered) list synchronously.
432	+		m.snippetSeq++
433	+		m.applyFilter()
434	+		return m, cmd
435	+	}
436	+	return m, tea.Batch(cmd, searchDebounce(m.searchSeq, modeList))
437	+}
438	+
439	+// updatePreview handles keys while the glamour preview reader is open. Close keys return to
440	+// the underlying screen (list or global results); `r` resumes the previewed session;
441	+// everything else (↑↓ pgup/pgdn) scrolls the viewport.
442	+func (m sessionTUI) updatePreview(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
443	+	switch msg.String() {
444	+	case " ", "space", "esc", "q":
445	+		m.previewing = false
446	+		m.readerSession = nil
447	+		return m, nil
448	+	case "r":
449	+		if m.readerSession != nil {
450	+			sess := m.readerSession
451	+			m.previewing = false
452	+			m.readerSession = nil
453	+			return m.beginResume(sess)
454	+		}
455	+		return m, nil
456	+	}
457	+	var cmd tea.Cmd
458	+	m.reader, cmd = m.reader.Update(msg)
459	+	return m, cmd
460	+}
461	+
462	+// openPreview loads the highlighted session into the glamour reader. Identical to search's
463	+// openReader so the two previews render the same way.
464	+func (m sessionTUI) openPreview(s *sessionindex.Session) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
465	+	m.readerSession = s
466	+	m.reader.SetWidth(m.width)
467	+	m.reader.SetHeight(m.previewHeight())
468	+	m.reader.SetContent(renderGlamour(sessionMarkdown(m.registry, m.store, s), m.width))
469	+	m.reader.GotoTop()
470	+	m.previewing = true
471	+	return m, nil
472	+}
473	+
474	+// previewHeight is the viewport height inside the preview chrome (title + two rules + footer).
475	+func (m sessionTUI) previewHeight() int {
476	+	const chrome = 4
477	+	h := m.height - chrome
478	+	if h < 1 {
479	+		h = 1
480	+	}
481	+	return h
482	+}
483	+
484	+// updateTarget handles the target-agent selection step.
485	+func (m sessionTUI) updateTarget(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
486	+	switch msg.String() {
487	+	case "esc", "q":
488	+		m.mode = modeList
489	+		m.chosen = nil
490	+	case "ctrl+c":
491	+		m.result = sessionTUIResult{cancelled: true}
492	+		return m, tea.Quit
493	+	case "up", "k":
494	+		if m.targetCursor > 0 {
495	+			m.targetCursor--
496	+		}
497	+	case "down", "j":
498	+		if m.targetCursor < len(m.installed)-1 {
499	+			m.targetCursor++
500	+		}
501	+	case "enter":
502	+		m.result = sessionTUIResult{session: m.chosen, targetID: m.installed[m.targetCursor].id}
503	+		return m, tea.Quit
504	+	}
505	+	return m, nil
506	+}
507	+
508	+// ---- list mechanics ----
509	+
510	+func (m *sessionTUI) moveCursor(delta int) {
511	+	if len(m.filtered) == 0 {
512	+		return
513	+	}
514	+	m.cursor += delta
515	+	if m.cursor < 0 {
516	+		m.cursor = 0
517	+	}
518	+	if m.cursor > len(m.filtered)-1 {
519	+		m.cursor = len(m.filtered) - 1
520	+	}
521	+	m.clampScroll()
522	+}
523	+
524	+func (m *sessionTUI) clampScroll() {
525	+	h := m.listHeight()
526	+	if m.cursor < m.top {
527	+		m.top = m.cursor
528	+	}
529	+	if m.cursor >= m.top+h {
530	+		m.top = m.cursor - h + 1
531	+	}
532	+	if m.top < 0 {
533	+		m.top = 0
534	+	}
535	+}
536	+
537	+func (m sessionTUI) selected() *sessionindex.Session {
538	+	if m.cursor < 0 || m.cursor >= len(m.filtered) {
539	+		return nil
540	+	}
541	+	return &m.filtered[m.cursor]
542	+}
543	+
544	+// rowsPerSession is how many terminal lines one list row occupies in each view mode.
545	+func (m sessionTUI) rowsPerSession() int {
546	+	if m.viewMode == "sparse" {
547	+		return 2
548	+	}
549	+	return 1
550	+}
551	+
552	+// listHeight is how many sessions fit in the list region (height minus chrome).
553	+func (m sessionTUI) listHeight() int {
554	+	const chrome = 5 // header(1) + two rules(2) + footer(1) + margin
555	+	avail := m.height - chrome
556	+	if avail < 1 {
557	+		avail = 1
558	+	}
559	+	n := avail / m.rowsPerSession()
560	+	if n < 1 {
561	+		n = 1
562	+	}
563	+	return n
564	+}
565	+
566	+func (m *sessionTUI) rebuildAgentCycle() {
567	+	present := map[string]bool{}
568	+	for _, s := range m.all {
569	+		present[s.Agent] = true
570	+	}
571	+	ids := make([]string, 0, len(present))
572	+	for id := range present {
573	+		ids = append(ids, id)
574	+	}
575	+	sort.Strings(ids)
576	+	m.agentCycle = append([]string{""}, ids...)
577	+}
578	+
579	+func (m *sessionTUI) cycleAgent() tea.Cmd {
580	+	// In the all-projects search, cycle over every known agent and re-run the query; in a
581	+	// session list, cycle over the agents actually present and re-filter in place.
582	+	if m.mode == modeProjects && m.globalActive {
583	+		m.agentFilter = nextInCycle(m.allAgentCycle(), m.agentFilter)
584	+		m.searchSeq++
585	+		return searchDebounce(m.searchSeq, modeProjects)
586	+	}
587	+	m.agentFilter = nextInCycle(m.agentCycle, m.agentFilter)
588	+	m.applyFilter()
589	+	return m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeList)
590	+}
591	+
592	+// allAgentCycle is the agent-filter ring for cross-project search: "" then every known agent.
593	+func (m sessionTUI) allAgentCycle() []string {
594	+	ids := make([]string, 0, len(m.agents))
595	+	for id := range m.agents {
596	+		ids = append(ids, id)
597	+	}
598	+	sort.Strings(ids)
599	+	return append([]string{""}, ids...)
600	+}
601	+
602	+// nextInCycle returns the element after cur in cycle, wrapping around.
603	+func nextInCycle(cycle []string, cur string) string {
604	+	idx := 0
605	+	for i, id := range cycle {
606	+		if id == cur {
607	+			idx = i
608	+			break
609	+		}
610	+	}
611	+	return cycle[(idx+1)%len(cycle)]
612	+}
613	+
614	+func (m *sessionTUI) toggleViewMode() {
615	+	if m.viewMode == "sparse" {
616	+		m.viewMode = "dense"
617	+	} else {
618	+		m.viewMode = "sparse"
619	+	}
620	+	m.clampScroll()
621	+}
622	+
623	+// applyFilter rebuilds the visible list from the agent filter and search query. When a
624	+// search is active, snippets are fetched lazily for the visible rows; otherwise the list
625	+// is the project's sessions filtered by agent and snippets are cleared.
626	+func (m *sessionTUI) applyFilter() {
627	+	m.filtered = m.filtered[:0]
628	+	m.filteredSnippets = nil
629	+
630	+	if queryReady(m.searchQuery) {
631	+		var out []sessionindex.Session
632	+		if sessions, err := m.store.Search(ftsQuery(m.searchQuery), m.projectID); err == nil {
633	+			for _, s := range sessions {
634	+				if m.agentFilter == "" || s.Agent == m.agentFilter {
635	+					out = append(out, s)
636	+				}
637	+			}
638	+		}
639	+		m.filtered = out
640	+		m.filteredSnippets = map[string]string{}
641	+	} else {
642	+		out := make([]sessionindex.Session, 0, len(m.all))
643	+		for _, s := range m.all {
644	+			if m.agentFilter == "" || s.Agent == m.agentFilter {
645	+				out = append(out, s)
646	+			}
647	+		}
648	+		m.filtered = out
649	+	}
650	+
651	+	if m.cursor > len(m.filtered)-1 {
652	+		m.cursor = len(m.filtered) - 1
653	+	}
654	+	if m.cursor < 0 {
655	+		m.cursor = 0
656	+	}
657	+	m.top = 0
658	+	m.clampScroll()
659	+}
660	+
661	+// beginResume advances from a chosen session toward launch. When a target agent was
662	+// pre-selected via `resume <agent>`, that choice is honored immediately and the picker exits
663	+// — the user is never asked to pick a target. Otherwise it moves to the target-selection step.
664	+func (m sessionTUI) beginResume(sess *sessionindex.Session) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
665	+	m.chosen = sess
666	+	if m.presetTo != "" {
667	+		m.result = sessionTUIResult{session: sess, targetID: m.presetTo}
668	+		return m, tea.Quit
669	+	}
670	+	m.mode = modeTarget
671	+	m.targetCursor = m.defaultTargetIndex()
672	+	return m, nil
673	+}
674	+
675	+func (m sessionTUI) defaultTargetIndex() int {
676	+	// Prefer the last-resumed agent, else the chosen session's own agent (same-agent resume).
677	+	// An explicit preset (`resume <agent>`) never reaches here — beginResume skips the
678	+	// target-selection step entirely in that case.
679	+	want := m.lastAgent
680	+	if want == "" && m.chosen != nil {
681	+		want = m.chosen.Agent
682	+	}
683	+	for i, a := range m.installed {
684	+		if a.id == want {
685	+			return i
686	+		}
687	+	}
688	+	return 0
689	+}
690	+
691	+// ftsQuery turns free-form input into a safe FTS5 prefix query (alnum tokens only).
692	+// minQueryLen is the shortest query we run a full-text search for. A single-character
693	+// prefix (e.g. "t*") matches almost the entire corpus, and ranking + snippet generation
694	+// over that set is pathologically slow (~80s on a ~800-session index), so we never fire
695	+// it. Shared by resume and search so both behave the same.
696	+const minQueryLen = 2
697	+
698	+// queryReady reports whether input has enough searchable characters (letters/digits, the
699	+// same runes ftsQuery keeps) to run an FTS query. Below minQueryLen we don't search.
700	+func queryReady(input string) bool {
701	+	n := 0
702	+	for _, r := range input {
703	+		if unicode.IsLetter(r) || unicode.IsDigit(r) {
704	+			n++
705	+			if n >= minQueryLen {
706	+				return true
707	+			}
708	+		}
709	+	}
710	+	return false
711	+}
712	+
713	+// ftsQuery turns free-form input into an FTS5 query string. Three shapes:
714	+//
715	+//   - Bare words become independent prefix terms, AND-ed (matched anywhere, any order) —
716	+//     a loose search:  thank you      -> thank* you*
717	+//   - A double-quoted span becomes a phrase: tokens adjacent and in order —
718	+//     an exact phrase:  "thank you"    -> "thank you"
719	+//   - A still-open quote (the user is mid-typing the phrase) keeps the final token a prefix,
720	+//     so results keep updating live:  "thank yo    -> thank + yo*
721	+//
722	+// A single typed word that contains punctuation (e.g. a filename, "poem.txt") is itself an
723	+// adjacency phrase. The index's unicode61 tokenizer splits on every non-alphanumeric run, so
724	+// "poem.txt" is stored as the two adjacent tokens poem, txt — never the fused "poemtxt". We
725	+// must tokenize the query the same way, or the term can't match what was indexed. So a bare
726	+// "poem.txt" becomes the phrase poem + txt* (adjacent, prefix the last token for type-ahead);
727	+// quoted, it is the committed phrase "poem txt" (adjacent, no prefix).
728	+//
729	+// Only letters and digits survive tokenization, so no FTS5 syntax character from the raw
730	+// input can reach MATCH — every query this builds is safe to execute.
731	+func ftsQuery(input string) string {
732	+	// Splitting on '"' yields alternating runs: even indexes are outside quotes, odd indexes
733	+	// are inside. An odd-indexed run that is also the LAST run means the closing quote hasn't
734	+	// been typed yet (an open phrase), so its final token stays a prefix for live type-ahead.
735	+	segs := strings.Split(input, `"`)
736	+	var parts []string
737	+	for i, seg := range segs {
738	+		switch {
739	+		case i%2 == 0: // outside quotes: each word is its own loose, prefix-terminated term
740	+			for _, f := range strings.Fields(seg) {
741	+				if toks := fieldTokens(f); len(toks) > 0 {
742	+					parts = append(parts, strings.Join(toks, " + ")+"*")
743	+				}
744	+			}
745	+		case i == len(segs)-1: // open phrase (still typing): adjacency across all tokens + prefix last
746	+			if toks := segmentTokens(seg); len(toks) > 0 {
747	+				parts = append(parts, strings.Join(toks, " + ")+"*")
748	+			}
749	+		default: // closed phrase: exact adjacency, no prefix (a committed phrase)
750	+			if toks := segmentTokens(seg); len(toks) > 0 {
751	+				parts = append(parts, `"`+strings.Join(toks, " ")+`"`)
752	+			}
753	+		}
754	+	}
755	+	return strings.Join(parts, " ")
756	+}
757	+
758	+// fieldTokens splits one whitespace-delimited field into its FTS5 tokens the way the index's
759	+// default unicode61 tokenizer does: every run of non-alphanumeric characters is a token
760	+// boundary. So "poem.txt" -> [poem, txt] and "max-cpu!" -> [max, cpu]. Mirroring the index
761	+// tokenizer here is the whole point — previously punctuation was stripped and the pieces
762	+// fused into one token ("poemtxt") that the index, holding poem and txt separately, never had.
763	+func fieldTokens(field string) []string {
764	+	var tokens []string
765	+	var b strings.Builder
766	+	flush := func() {
767	+		if b.Len() > 0 {
768	+			tokens = append(tokens, b.String())
769	+			b.Reset()
770	+		}
771	+	}
772	+	for _, r := range field {
773	+		if unicode.IsLetter(r) || unicode.IsDigit(r) {
774	+			b.WriteRune(r)
775	+		} else {
776	+			flush()
777	+		}
778	+	}
779	+	flush()
780	+	return tokens
781	+}
782	+
783	+// segmentTokens tokenizes a whole quoted segment into one flat, ordered token list — both the
784	+// whitespace between words and any punctuation within them are token boundaries (unicode61),
785	+// so the phrase matches the adjacency the index actually stored.
786	+func segmentTokens(seg string) []string {
787	+	var tokens []string
788	+	for _, f := range strings.Fields(seg) {
789	+		tokens = append(tokens, fieldTokens(f)...)
790	+	}
791	+	return tokens
792	+}
793	+
794	+// ---- rendering ----
795	+
796	+var (
797	+	styDim    = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("240"))
798	+	styFaint  = lipgloss.NewStyle().Faint(true)
799	+	styBold   = lipgloss.NewStyle().Bold(true)
800	+	styCursor = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("213")).Bold(true)
801	+	stySel    = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("255")).Bold(true)
802	+)
803	+
804	+func (m sessionTUI) View() tea.View {
805	+	var content string
806	+	switch {
807	+	case m.previewing:
808	+		content = m.renderPreview()
809	+	case m.mode == modeTarget:
810	+		content = m.renderTarget()
811	+	case m.mode == modeProjects && m.globalActive:
812	+		content = m.renderGlobalResults()
813	+	case m.mode == modeProjects:
814	+		content = m.renderProjects()
815	+	default:
816	+		content = m.renderListScreen()
817	+	}
818	+	v := tea.NewView(content)
819	+	v.AltScreen = true
820	+	return v
821	+}
822	+
823	+func (m sessionTUI) renderListScreen() string {
824	+	var b strings.Builder
825	+	b.WriteString(m.renderHeader())
826	+	b.WriteString("\n")
827	+	b.WriteString(strings.Repeat("─", m.lineWidth()))
828	+	b.WriteString("\n")
829	+	b.WriteString(m.renderRows())
830	+	b.WriteString("\n")
831	+	b.WriteString(strings.Repeat("─", m.lineWidth()))
832	+	b.WriteString("\n")
833	+	b.WriteString(m.renderFooter())
834	+	return b.String()
835	+}
836	+
837	+func (m sessionTUI) lineWidth() int {
838	+	if m.width < 20 {
839	+		return 80
840	+	}
841	+	return m.width
842	+}
843	+
844	+// headerLeft is the shared "<title> · <scope>" left segment used by every screen.
845	+func (m sessionTUI) headerLeft(scope string) string {
846	+	return styBold.Render(m.title) + styDim.Render(" · ") + scope
847	+}
848	+
849	+// projectScope labels the current project, e.g. "project: getspecstory" (name in white).
850	+func (m sessionTUI) projectScope() string {
851	+	return styDim.Render("project: ") + stySel.Render(m.projectName)
852	+}
853	+
854	+// agentScope mirrors the project-scope label: an unstyled "all agents" phrase (rendered like
855	+// "all projects") when unfiltered, else "agent: <name>" matching projectScope's "project: <name>".
856	+func (m sessionTUI) agentScope() string {
857	+	if m.agentFilter == "" {
858	+		return "all agents"
859	+	}
860	+	return styDim.Render("agent: ") + stySel.Render(m.agentName(m.agentFilter))
861	+}
862	+
863	+func (m sessionTUI) renderHeader() string {
864	+	// Agent filter sits left, right after the project, rather than tucked in the far corner.
865	+	left := m.headerLeft(m.projectScope()) + styDim.Render("  ·  ") + m.agentScope()
866	+	return headerRow(left, "", m.lineWidth())
867	+}
868	+
869	+func (m sessionTUI) renderRows() string {
870	+	if len(m.filtered) == 0 {
871	+		return styFaint.Render("  No sessions match.")
872	+	}
873	+	h := m.listHeight()
874	+	end := min(m.top+h, len(m.filtered))
875	+	var b strings.Builder
876	+	for i := m.top; i < end; i++ {
877	+		b.WriteString(m.sessionRow(m.filtered[i], i == m.cursor, m.snippetAt(i)))
878	+		if i < end-1 {
879	+			b.WriteString("\n")
880	+		}
881	+	}
882	+	return b.String()
883	+}
884	+
885	+// snippetAt returns the search snippet for filtered row i, or "" (no active search).
886	+func (m sessionTUI) snippetAt(i int) string {
887	+	if i >= 0 && i < len(m.filtered) {
888	+		s := m.filtered[i]
889	+		return m.filteredSnippets[sessionindex.FingerprintKey(s.Agent, s.SessionID)]
890	+	}
891	+	return ""
892	+}
893	+
894	+// sessionRow renders one list row. When snippet is non-empty (search active) the row
895	+// shows the highlighted match context instead of the session title.
896	+func (m sessionTUI) sessionRow(s sessionindex.Session, selected bool, snippet string) string {
897	+	cursor := "  "
898	+	if selected {
899	+		cursor = styCursor.Render("▸ ")
900	+	}
901	+	agent := m.agentTag(s.Agent)
902	+	when := fmt.Sprintf("%-4s", relativeTime(s.UpdatedAt))
903	+
904	+	if m.viewMode == "sparse" {
905	+		turns := styDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("%d prompts", s.UserTurns))
906	+		label := renderName(sessionTitle(s), selected, m.lineWidth()-24)
907	+		if snippet != "" {
908	+			label = renderSnippet(snippet, m.lineWidth()-26)
909	+		}
910	+		head := cursor + agent + "  " + label + "   " + turns
911	+		sub := "    " + styFaint.Render(fmt.Sprintf("%s ago · %s", relativeTime(s.UpdatedAt), shortID(s.SessionID)))
912	+		return head + "\n" + sub
913	+	}
914	+	turns := styDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("%4d", s.UserTurns))
915	+	// A year-stamped date ("Dec 31 '25") is wider than the 4-col slot; shrink the label by the
916	+	// overflow so the right-hand turns column can't get pushed off the line and wrap.
917	+	extra := len(when) - 4
918	+	if extra < 0 {
919	+		extra = 0
920	+	}
921	+	label := renderName(sessionTitle(s), selected, m.lineWidth()-22-extra)
922	+	if snippet != "" {
923	+		label = renderSnippet(snippet, m.lineWidth()-24-extra)
924	+	}
925	+	return cursor + agent + " " + styDim.Render(when) + "  " + label + "  " + turns
926	+}
927	+
928	+// renderSnippet renders a FTS snippet (matched terms wrapped in the control-char marks)
929	+// with the matches highlighted, collapsing whitespace and clipping to maxWidth columns.
930	+func renderSnippet(snip string, maxWidth int) string {
931	+	if maxWidth < 8 {
932	+		maxWidth = 8
933	+	}
934	+	snip = strings.NewReplacer("\n", " ", "\t", " ", "\r", " ").Replace(snip)
935	+	hl := lipgloss.NewStyle().Bold(true).Foreground(lipgloss.Color("232")).Background(lipgloss.Color("221"))
936	+
937	+	var b strings.Builder
938	+	var seg strings.Builder
939	+	matched := false
940	+	visible := 0
941	+	flush := func() {
942	+		if seg.Len() == 0 {
943	+			return
944	+		}
945	+		if matched {
946	+			b.WriteString(hl.Render(seg.String()))
947	+		} else {
948	+			b.WriteString(seg.String())
949	+		}
950	+		seg.Reset()
951	+	}
952	+	for _, r := range snip {
953	+		switch r {
954	+		case '\x02':
955	+			flush()
956	+			matched = true
957	+			continue
958	+		case '\x03':
959	+			flush()
960	+			matched = false
961	+			continue
962	+		}
963	+		if visible >= maxWidth {
964	+			flush()
965	+			b.WriteString("…")
966	+			return b.String()
967	+		}
968	+		seg.WriteRune(r)
969	+		visible++
970	+	}
971	+	flush()
972	+	return b.String()
973	+}
974	+
975	+func renderName(name string, selected bool, width int) string {
976	+	if width < 8 {
977	+		width = 8
978	+	}
979	+	t := truncate(name, width)
980	+	if selected {
981	+		return stySel.Render(t)
982	+	}
983	+	return t
984	+}
985	+
986	+func (m sessionTUI) renderFooter() string {
987	+	if m.searching {
988	+		return m.search.View() + "    " + styFaint.Render("esc clear · enter apply")
989	+	}
990	+	scopeKey := "tab all-projects"
991	+	if m.inBrowser {
992	+		scopeKey = "tab/esc back"
993	+	}
994	+	keys := []string{"↑↓ move", "r resume", "space preview", "/ search", "a agent", scopeKey, "v " + m.viewMode, "q quit"}
995	+	return styDim.Render(strings.Join(keys, " · "))
996	+}
997	+
998	+func (m sessionTUI) renderPreview() string {
999	+	var b strings.Builder
1000	+	left := styBold.Render("Preview")
1001	+	if s := m.readerSession; s != nil {
1002	+		left += styDim.Render(" · ") + m.agentTag(s.Agent) +
1003	+			styDim.Render(" · ") + sessionTitle(*s)
1004	+	}
1005	+	b.WriteString(left)
1006	+	b.WriteString("\n")
1007	+	b.WriteString(strings.Repeat("─", m.lineWidth()))
1008	+	b.WriteString("\n")
1009	+	b.WriteString(m.reader.View())
1010	+	b.WriteString("\n")
1011	+	b.WriteString(strings.Repeat("─", m.lineWidth()))
1012	+	b.WriteString("\n")
1013	+	b.WriteString(styDim.Render(strings.Join([]string{"↑↓ scroll", "pgup/pgdn page", "r resume", "space/esc close"}, " · ")))
1014	+	return b.String()
1015	+}
1016	+
1017	+func (m sessionTUI) renderTarget() string {
1018	+	var b strings.Builder
1019	+	b.WriteString(styBold.Render("Resume into which agent?"))
1020	+	if m.chosen != nil {
1021	+		b.WriteString(styDim.Render("   " + sessionTitle(*m.chosen)))
1022	+	}
1023	+	b.WriteString("\n\n")
1024	+	for i, a := range m.installed {
1025	+		cursor := "   "
1026	+		label := a.provider.Name()
1027	+		if m.chosen != nil && a.id == m.chosen.Agent {
1028	+			label += styFaint.Render(" (same agent — native resume)")
1029	+		}
1030	+		if i == m.targetCursor {
1031	+			cursor = styCursor.Render(" ▸ ")
1032	+			label = stySel.Render(a.provider.Name()) + label[len(a.provider.Name()):]
1033	+		}
1034	+		b.WriteString(cursor + label + "\n")
1035	+	}
1036	+	b.WriteString("\n")
1037	+	b.WriteString(styDim.Render(strings.Join([]string{"↑↓ move", "↵ resume", "esc back"}, " · ")))
1038	+	return b.String()
1039	+}
1040	+
1041	+// ---- helpers ----
1042	+
1043	+func (m sessionTUI) agentName(id string) string {
1044	+	if a, ok := m.agents[id]; ok {
1045	+		return a.name
1046	+	}
1047	+	return id
1048	+}
1049	+
1050	+func (m sessionTUI) agentTag(id string) string {
1051	+	label := fmt.Sprintf("%-8s", id)

1058	+// sessionTitle is the human label for a session: name, then slug, then short id.
1059	+func sessionTitle(s sessionindex.Session) string {
1060	+	switch {
1061	+	case strings.TrimSpace(s.Name) != "":
1062	+		return s.Name
1063	+	case strings.TrimSpace(s.Slug) != "":
1064	+		return s.Slug
1065	+	default:
1066	+		return shortID(s.SessionID)
1067	+	}
1068	+}
1069	+
1070	+// truncate shortens s to n runes with an ellipsis.
1071	+func truncate(s string, n int) string {
1072	+	r := []rune(s)
1073	+	if len(r) <= n {
1074	+		return s
1075	+	}
1076	+	if n < 1 {
1077	+		return ""
1078	+	}
1079	+	return string(r[:n-1]) + "…"
1080	+}
1081	+
1082	+// relativeTime renders an ISO 8601 timestamp as a compact "2m"/"3h"/"5d"/"Jun 2".
1083	+func relativeTime(iso string) string {
1084	+	t, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, iso)
1085	+	if err != nil {
1086	+		return ""
1087	+	}
1088	+	d := time.Since(t)
1089	+	switch {
1090	+	case d < time.Minute:
1091	+		return "now"
1092	+	case d < time.Hour:
1093	+		return fmt.Sprintf("%dm", int(d.Minutes()))
1094	+	case d < 24*time.Hour:
1095	+		return fmt.Sprintf("%dh", int(d.Hours()))
1096	+	case d < 7*24*time.Hour:
1097	+		return fmt.Sprintf("%dd", int(d.Hours()/24))
1098	+	default:
1099	+		lt := t.Local()
1100	+		if lt.Year() == time.Now().Year() {
1101	+			return lt.Format("Jan 2")
1102	+		}
1103	+		// Disambiguate prior years so "Dec 31" can't be mistaken for this year: "Dec 31 '25".
1104	+		return lt.Format("Jan 2 '06")
1105	+	}
1106	+}
1107	+
1108	+// headerRow lays out a left and right segment across width with a gap between.
1109	+func headerRow(left, right string, width int) string {
1110	+	gap := width - lipgloss.Width(left) - lipgloss.Width(right)
1111	+	if gap < 1 {
1112	+		gap = 1
1113	+	}
1114	+	return left + strings.Repeat(" ", gap) + right
1115	+}
1116	+
1117	+// renderGlamour renders markdown to styled terminal output for the preview reader.
1118	+func renderGlamour(md string, width int) string {
1119	+	if width < 20 {
1120	+		width = 80
1121	+	}
1122	+	r, err := glamour.NewTermRenderer(glamour.WithStandardStyle("dark"), glamour.WithWordWrap(width-2))
1123	+	if err != nil {
1124	+		return md
1125	+	}
1126	+	out, err := r.Render(md)
1127	+	if err != nil {
1128	+		return md
1129	+	}
1130	+	return out
1131	+}

1133	+// sessionMarkdown returns the session as markdown for the preview — the real specstory
1134	+// render when the session can be re-parsed (needs a cwd), else the plain FTS body.
1135	+func sessionMarkdown(registry *factory.Registry, store *sessionindex.Store, s *sessionindex.Session) string {
1136	+	if s.OriginCwd != "" {
1137	+		if prov, err := registry.Get(s.Agent); err == nil {
1138	+			if full, err := prov.GetAgentChatSession(s.OriginCwd, s.SessionID, false); err == nil &&
1139	+				full != nil && full.SessionData != nil {
1140	+				if md, err := session.GenerateMarkdownFromAgentSession(full.SessionData, false, true); err == nil {
1141	+					return md
1142	+				}
1143	+			}
1144	+		}
1145	+	}
1146	+	if body, _ := store.SessionBody(s.Agent, s.SessionID); strings.TrimSpace(body) != "" {
1147	+		return "```\n" + body + "\n```"
1148	+	}
1149	+	return "_(no readable content for this session)_"
1150	+}

1152	+// ---- integration with the resume command ----
1153	+
1154	+// openOrBuildResumeIndex opens sessions.db, building it first (with the normal reindex
1155	+// UI) when it is missing or empty, then proceeding straight into the picker. builtFresh
1156	+// reports whether a full foreground build just ran — when it did, the index is already
1157	+// fully current, so callers skip the background warm (it would be redundant).
1158	+func openOrBuildResumeIndex() (store *sessionindex.Store, builtFresh bool, err error) {
1159	+	dbPath, err := sessionindex.DefaultPath()
1160	+	if err != nil {
1161	+		return nil, false, err
1162	+	}
1163	+	if _, statErr := os.Stat(dbPath); statErr == nil {
1164	+		s, err := sessionindex.OpenReader(dbPath)
1165	+		if err != nil {
1166	+			return nil, false, err
1167	+		}
1168	+		if n, _ := s.Count(); n > 0 {
1169	+			return s, false, nil
1170	+		}
1171	+		_ = s.Close() // empty → rebuild below
1172	+	}
1173	+	if err := runReindex(false); err != nil {
1174	+		return nil, false, err
1175	+	}
1176	+	s, err := sessionindex.OpenReader(dbPath)
1177	+	return s, true, err
1178	+}
1179	+
1180	+// indexWarmedMsg is sent to the running TUI when the background warm finishes refreshing the
1181	+// CURRENT project (the first warm pass). The model re-queries so new/changed sessions for this
1182	+// project appear without a restart. The subsequent full pass is silent (no refresh).
1183	+type indexWarmedMsg struct{}
1184	+
1185	+// startIndexWarm launches the background two-pass index warm for projectID, refreshing the
1186	+// running TUI (via p.Send) when the current-project pass completes. The returned cancel func
1187	+// should be invoked when the TUI exits: the warm is best-effort and must never block process
1188	+// exit. When builtFresh is true the index was just rebuilt in the foreground, so there is
1189	+// nothing to warm and this only returns a cancel func.
1190	+func startIndexWarm(p *tea.Program, projectID string, builtFresh bool) context.CancelFunc {
1191	+	ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
1192	+	if builtFresh {
1193	+		return cancel
1194	+	}
1195	+	dbPath, err := sessionindex.DefaultPath()
1196	+	if err != nil {
1197	+		slog.Debug("warm: no db path; skipping", "error", err)
1198	+		return cancel
1199	+	}
1200	+	go warmIndexInBackground(ctx, dbPath, projectID, func() { p.Send(indexWarmedMsg{}) })
1201	+	return cancel
1202	+}
1203	+
1204	+// selectResumeViaTUI runs the picker for the current project and returns the chosen
1205	+// resume plan (or nil if the user cancelled). On a successful selection it persists the
1206	+// view-mode and target-agent preferences to the user config.
1207	+func selectResumeViaTUI(registry *factory.Registry, store *sessionindex.Store, projectID, projectName, presetTo string, builtFresh bool) (*resumePlan, error) {
1208	+	sessions, err := store.ListByProject(projectID)
1209	+	if err != nil {
1210	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("loading sessions: %w", err)
1211	+	}
1212	+	// An empty current project is fine — the picker opens in the all-projects browser.
1213	+	// Only bail when the whole index is empty (nothing to resume anywhere).
1214	+	if total, _ := store.Count(); total == 0 {
1215	+		fprintln(os.Stderr, "\nNo agent sessions indexed yet. Run an agent here, then try again (or `specstory reindex`).")
1216	+		return nil, nil
1217	+	}
1218	+
1219	+	agents := map[string]agentMeta{}
1220	+	var installed []agentChoice
1221	+	for _, id := range registry.ListIDs() {
1222	+		prov, err := registry.Get(id)
1223	+		if err != nil {
1224	+			continue
1225	+		}
1226	+		agents[id] = agentMeta{name: prov.Name(), accent: colorForAgent(id)}
1227	+		if prov.Check("").Success {
1228	+			installed = append(installed, agentChoice{id: id, provider: prov})
1229	+		}
1230	+	}
1231	+	if len(installed) == 0 {
1232	+		fprintln(os.Stderr, "\nNo installed agents found to resume into.")
1233	+		return nil, nil
1234	+	}
1235	+
1236	+	// A preset target (`resume <agent>`) must be installed to resume into. The command already
1237	+	// confirmed it is a known provider; here, with the installed set known, confirm it is
1238	+	// available and resolve it to its canonical ID. When set, the picker skips the
1239	+	// target-selection step entirely (see beginResume).
1240	+	if presetTo != "" {
1241	+		resolved := ""
1242	+		for _, a := range installed {
1243	+			if strings.EqualFold(a.id, presetTo) {
1244	+				resolved = a.id
1245	+				break
1246	+			}
1247	+		}
1248	+		if resolved == "" {
1249	+			return nil, utils.ValidationError{Message: fmt.Sprintf(
1250	+				"agent %q is not installed, so it can't be a resume target.", presetTo)}
1251	+		}
1252	+		presetTo = resolved
1253	+	}
1254	+
1255	+	viewMode, lastAgent := "dense", ""
1256	+	if cfg, _ := config.Load(nil); cfg != nil {
1257	+		viewMode = cfg.GetResumeViewMode()
1258	+		lastAgent = cfg.GetResumeLastAgent()
1259	+	}
1260	+
1261	+	model := newSessionTUI(store, registry, projectID, projectName, sessions, agents, installed, sessionTUIOpts{
1262	+		title:     "SpecStory Resume",
1263	+		presetTo:  presetTo,
1264	+		lastAgent: lastAgent,
1265	+		viewMode:  viewMode,
1266	+	})
1267	+	p := tea.NewProgram(model)
1268	+	cancelWarm := startIndexWarm(p, projectID, builtFresh)
1269	+	final, err := p.Run()
1270	+	cancelWarm() // stop warming the moment the picker exits (never block on it)
1271	+	if err != nil {
1272	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("resume picker failed: %w", err)
1273	+	}
1274	+	rm := final.(sessionTUI)
1275	+	if rm.result.cancelled || rm.result.session == nil {
1276	+		return nil, nil
1277	+	}
1278	+
1279	+	fromID := rm.result.session.Agent
1280	+	fromProv, err := registry.Get(fromID)
1281	+	if err != nil {
1282	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("unknown source agent %q: %w", fromID, err)
1283	+	}
1284	+	toProv, err := registry.Get(rm.result.targetID)
1285	+	if err != nil {
1286	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("unknown target agent %q: %w", rm.result.targetID, err)
1287	+	}
1288	+
1289	+	if err := config.SaveResumePrefs(rm.viewMode, rm.result.targetID); err != nil {
1290	+		slog.Debug("resume: could not save prefs", "error", err)
1291	+	}
1292	+
1293	+	return &resumePlan{
1294	+		from:      fromProv,
1295	+		fromID:    fromID,
1296	+		sessionID: rm.result.session.SessionID,
1297	+		fromCwd:   rm.result.session.OriginCwd,
1298	+		to:        toProv,
1299	+		toID:      rm.result.targetID,
1300	+	}, nil
1301	+}

1564	+	}
1565	+	if p.ProjectID == unknownProjectID || p.ProjectID == "" {
1566	+		return "(unknown project)"
1567	+	}
1568	+	return p.ProjectID
1569	+}
1570	+
1571	+// dateBucket groups a timestamp into a relative bucket for the browser rollup.
1572	+func dateBucket(iso string) string {
1573	+	t, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, iso)
1574	+	if err != nil {
1575	+		return "Older"
1576	+	}
1577	+	switch d := dayDiff(t.Local()); {
1578	+	case d <= 0:
1579	+		return "Today"
1580	+	case d == 1:
1581	+		return "Yesterday"
1582	+	case d < 7:
1583	+		return "Previous 7 days"
1584	+	case d < 30:
1585	+		return "Previous 30 days"
1586	+	default:
1587	+		return "Older"
1588	+	}
1589	+}
1590	+
1591	+// dayDiff returns whole calendar days between now and t (both local), now - t.
1592	+func dayDiff(t time.Time) int {
1593	+	now := time.Now()
1594	+	ny, nm, nd := now.Date()
1595	+	ty, tm, td := t.Date()
1596	+	a := time.Date(ny, nm, nd, 0, 0, 0, 0, now.Location())
1597	+	c := time.Date(ty, tm, td, 0, 0, 0, 0, now.Location())
1598	+	return int(a.Sub(c).Hours() / 24)
1599	+}
1600	+
1601	+// ---- global session search (FTS across all projects, from the browser) ----
1602	+
1603	+func (m sessionTUI) updateGlobalSearch(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
1604	+	switch msg.String() {
1605	+	case "esc":
1606	+		// In `search`, the search is the root → esc quits. In `resume` (reached via the
1607	+		// browser), esc backs out to the browser.
1608	+		if m.startedInSearch {
1609	+			m.result = sessionTUIResult{cancelled: true}
1610	+			return m, tea.Quit
1611	+		}
1612	+		m.searchSeq++
1613	+		m.exitGlobal()
1614	+		return m, nil
1615	+	case "tab":
1616	+		return m, m.toggleGlobalScope()
1617	+	case "enter":
1618	+		m.globalSearching = false // commit → browse the results
1619	+		m.globalInput.Blur()
1620	+		return m, nil
1621	+	case "up", "down", "pgup", "pgdown":
1622	+		// Arrows are useless in the one-line input, so commit and let the same key move into
1623	+		// the results — reach a hit without a separate enter.
1624	+		m.globalSearching = false
1625	+		m.globalInput.Blur()
1626	+		return m.updateGlobalResults(msg)
1627	+	}
1628	+	var cmd tea.Cmd
1629	+	m.globalInput, cmd = m.globalInput.Update(msg)
1630	+	m.globalQuery = m.globalInput.Value()
1631	+	m.searchSeq++
1632	+	if !queryReady(m.globalQuery) {
1633	+		// Too short to search the whole corpus: no results until the 2nd character.
1634	+		m.globalResults = nil
1635	+		m.globalSnippets = nil
1636	+		m.snippetSeq++
1637	+		return m, cmd
1638	+	}
1639	+	return m, tea.Batch(cmd, searchDebounce(m.searchSeq, modeProjects))
1640	+}
1641	+
1642	+func (m sessionTUI) updateGlobalResults(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
1643	+	switch msg.String() {
1644	+	case "q", "ctrl+c":
1645	+		m.result = sessionTUIResult{cancelled: true}
1646	+		return m, tea.Quit
1647	+	case "esc":
1648	+		// In `search`, the search is the root → esc quits. In `resume` (reached via the
1649	+		// browser), esc backs out to the browser.
1650	+		if m.startedInSearch {
1651	+			m.result = sessionTUIResult{cancelled: true}
1652	+			return m, tea.Quit
1653	+		}
1654	+		m.exitGlobal()
1655	+	case "tab":
1656	+		return m, m.toggleGlobalScope()
1657	+	case "/":
1658	+		m.globalSearching = true
1659	+		m.globalInput.SetValue(m.globalQuery)
1660	+		return m, m.globalInput.Focus()
1661	+	case "up", "k":
1662	+		m.moveGlobalCursor(-1)
1663	+		return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeProjects)
1664	+	case "down", "j":
1665	+		m.moveGlobalCursor(1)
1666	+		return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeProjects)
1667	+	case "pgup":
1668	+		m.moveGlobalCursor(-m.globalHeight())
1669	+		return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeProjects)
1670	+	case "pgdown":
1671	+		m.moveGlobalCursor(m.globalHeight())
1672	+		return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeProjects)
1673	+	case "home", "g":
1674	+		m.globalCursor, m.globalTop = 0, 0
1675	+		return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeProjects)
1676	+	case "end", "G":
1677	+		m.globalCursor = len(m.globalResults) - 1
1678	+		m.clampGlobalScroll()
1679	+		return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeProjects)
1680	+	case "r":
1681	+		// Resume the highlighted hit (enter is a no-op, mirroring the list).
1682	+		if sel := m.globalSelected(); sel != nil {
1683	+			return m.beginResume(sel)
1684	+		}
1685	+	case " ", "space":
1686	+		if sel := m.globalSelected(); sel != nil {
1687	+			return m.openPreview(sel)
1688	+		}
1689	+	case "a":
1690	+		return m, m.cycleAgent()
1691	+	case "v":
1692	+		m.toggleViewMode()
1693	+		m.clampGlobalScroll() // toggleViewMode clamps the list; the global list scrolls separately
1694	+		return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeProjects)
1695	+	}
1696	+	return m, nil
1697	+}
1698	+
1699	+// globalSelected returns the highlighted cross-project hit, or nil.
1700	+func (m sessionTUI) globalSelected() *sessionindex.Session {
1701	+	if m.globalCursor < 0 || m.globalCursor >= len(m.globalResults) {
1702	+		return nil
1703	+	}
1704	+	return &m.globalResults[m.globalCursor]
1705	+}
1706	+
1707	+// toggleGlobalScope flips the cross-project search between all projects and a single project
1708	+// (tab): the highlighted hit's project when a result is selected, else the current directory's
1709	+// project (nothing to point at). A second tab widens back to all projects. It then re-runs the
1710	+// query from the top against the new scope.
1711	+func (m *sessionTUI) toggleGlobalScope() tea.Cmd {
1712	+	switch {
1713	+	case m.globalScopeID != "":
1714	+		// Already scoped → widen back to all projects.
1715	+		m.globalScopeID, m.globalScopeName = "", ""
1716	+	case m.globalSelected() != nil:
1717	+		// A result is highlighted → scope to its project.
1718	+		sel := m.globalSelected()
1719	+		m.globalScopeID, m.globalScopeName = sel.ProjectID, sessionProject(*sel)
1720	+	default:
1721	+		// No results to point at → scope to the current directory's project.
1722	+		m.globalScopeID, m.globalScopeName = m.homeProjectID, m.homeProjectName
1723	+	}
1724	+	m.globalCursor, m.globalTop = 0, 0
1725	+	m.searchSeq++
1726	+	if !queryReady(m.globalQuery) {
1727	+		m.globalResults = nil
1728	+		m.globalSnippets = nil
1729	+		m.snippetSeq++
1730	+		return nil
1731	+	}
1732	+	return searchDebounce(m.searchSeq, modeProjects)
1733	+}
1734	+
1735	+func (m *sessionTUI) exitGlobal() {
1736	+	m.globalActive = false
1737	+	m.globalSearching = false
1738	+	m.globalInput.Blur()
1739	+	m.globalInput.SetValue("")
1740	+	m.globalQuery = ""
1741	+	m.globalScopeID, m.globalScopeName = "", "" // re-enter search at all-projects scope
1742	+	m.globalResults = nil
1743	+	m.globalSnippets = nil
1744	+	m.snippetSeq++
1745	+	m.globalCursor, m.globalTop = 0, 0
1746	+}
1747	+
1748	+func (m *sessionTUI) moveGlobalCursor(delta int) {
1749	+	if len(m.globalResults) == 0 {
1750	+		return
1751	+	}
1752	+	m.globalCursor += delta
1753	+	if m.globalCursor < 0 {
1754	+		m.globalCursor = 0
1755	+	}
1756	+	if m.globalCursor > len(m.globalResults)-1 {
1757	+		m.globalCursor = len(m.globalResults) - 1
1758	+	}
1759	+	m.clampGlobalScroll()
1760	+}
1761	+
1762	+func (m *sessionTUI) clampGlobalScroll() {
1763	+	h := m.globalHeight()
1764	+	if m.globalCursor < m.globalTop {
1765	+		m.globalTop = m.globalCursor
1766	+	}
1767	+	if m.globalCursor >= m.globalTop+h {
1768	+		m.globalTop = m.globalCursor - h + 1
1769	+	}
1770	+	if m.globalTop < 0 {
1771	+		m.globalTop = 0
1772	+	}
1773	+}
1774	+
1775	+func (m sessionTUI) globalHeight() int {
1776	+	const chrome = 6
1777	+	avail := m.height - chrome
1778	+	if avail < 1 {
1779	+		avail = 1
1780	+	}
1781	+	n := avail / m.rowsPerSession()
1782	+	if n < 1 {
1783	+		n = 1
1784	+	}
1785	+	return n
1786	+}

1788	+func (m sessionTUI) renderGlobalResults() string {
1789	+	var b strings.Builder
1790	+
1791	+	scope := "all projects"
1792	+	if m.globalScopeID != "" {
1793	+		scope = styDim.Render("project: ") + stySel.Render(m.globalScopeName)
1794	+	}
1795	+	left := m.headerLeft(scope) + styDim.Render("  ·  ") + m.agentScope()

1797	+		left += styDim.Render(" · ") + stySel.Render(q)
1798	+	}
1799	+	right := styDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("%d matches", len(m.globalResults)))
1800	+	b.WriteString(headerRow(left, right, m.lineWidth()) + "\n")
1801	+	b.WriteString(strings.Repeat("─", m.lineWidth()) + "\n")
1802	+
1803	+	scopeWord := "all projects"
1804	+	if m.globalScopeID != "" {
1805	+		scopeWord = "this project"
1806	+	}
1807	+	switch {
1808	+	case strings.TrimSpace(m.globalQuery) == "":

1810	+	case len(m.globalResults) == 0:
1811	+		b.WriteString(styFaint.Render("  No matches."))
1812	+	default:
1813	+		h := m.globalHeight()
1814	+		end := min(m.globalTop+h, len(m.globalResults))
1815	+		for i := m.globalTop; i < end; i++ {
1816	+			s := m.globalResults[i]
1817	+			snip := m.globalSnippets[sessionindex.FingerprintKey(s.Agent, s.SessionID)]
1818	+			b.WriteString(m.globalRow(s, i == m.globalCursor, snip))
1819	+			if i < end-1 {
1820	+				b.WriteString("\n")
1821	+			}
1822	+		}
1823	+	}
1824	+
1825	+	b.WriteString("\n" + strings.Repeat("─", m.lineWidth()) + "\n")
1826	+	// esc quits when search is the root (`specstory search`); otherwise it backs out to the
1827	+	// browser it was opened from (`specstory resume`).
1828	+	escHint := "esc back"
1829	+	if m.startedInSearch {
1830	+		escHint = "esc quit"
1831	+	}
1832	+	scopeKey := "tab this project"
1833	+	if m.globalScopeID != "" {
1834	+		scopeKey = "tab all projects"
1835	+	}
1836	+	if m.globalSearching {
1837	+		// While typing: esc quits (search root) or cancels back to the browser (resume). Only
1838	+		// offer "enter browse results" once there's actually something to browse.
1839	+		inputHint := "esc quit"
1840	+		if !m.startedInSearch {
1841	+			inputHint = "esc cancel"
1842	+		}
1843	+		inputHint += " · " + scopeKey
1844	+		if len(m.globalResults) > 0 {
1845	+			inputHint += " · enter browse results"
1846	+		}
1847	+		b.WriteString(m.globalInput.View() + "    " + styFaint.Render(inputHint))

1855	+// globalRow renders a cross-project search hit: agent · time · project · highlighted match
1856	+// snippet. Honors the dense/sparse view mode, matching the project session list.
1857	+func (m sessionTUI) globalRow(s sessionindex.Session, selected bool, snippet string) string {
1858	+	cursor := "  "
1859	+	if selected {
1860	+		cursor = styCursor.Render("▸ ")
1861	+	}
1862	+	agent := m.agentTag(s.Agent)
1863	+	proj := fmt.Sprintf("%-18s", truncate(sessionProject(s), 18))
1864	+
1865	+	if m.viewMode == "sparse" {
1866	+		label := renderName(sessionTitle(s), selected, m.lineWidth()-30)
1867	+		if snippet != "" {
1868	+			label = renderSnippet(snippet, m.lineWidth()-32)
1869	+		}
1870	+		head := cursor + agent + "  " + styFaint.Render(proj) + "  " + label
1871	+		sub := "    " + styFaint.Render(fmt.Sprintf("%s ago · %s", relativeTime(s.UpdatedAt), shortID(s.SessionID)))
1872	+		return head + "\n" + sub
1873	+	}
1874	+
1875	+	when := fmt.Sprintf("%-4s", relativeTime(s.UpdatedAt))
1876	+	label := renderName(sessionTitle(s), selected, m.lineWidth()-46)
1877	+	if snippet != "" {
1878	+		label = renderSnippet(snippet, m.lineWidth()-48)
1879	+	}
1880	+	return cursor + agent + " " + styDim.Render(when) + "  " + styFaint.Render(proj) + "  " + label
1881	+}
1882	+
1883	+func sessionProject(s sessionindex.Session) string {
1884	+	if strings.TrimSpace(s.ProjectName) != "" {
1885	+		return s.ProjectName
1886	+	}
1887	+	if s.ProjectID == unknownProjectID || s.ProjectID == "" {
1888	+		return "(unknown)"
1889	+	}
1890	+	return s.ProjectID
1891	+}
1892	+
1893	+// ---- async, debounced full-text search ----
1894	+
1895	+// searchDebounceMsg fires after a brief typing pause; the query runs only if its seq is
1896	+// still current. searchResultMsg carries the async FTS results back to the model. Both
1897	+// keep typing instant: the input updates synchronously while the query runs off-thread.
1898	+type searchDebounceMsg struct {
1899	+	seq  int
1900	+	kind tuiMode // modeList (project-scoped) or modeProjects (global)
1901	+}
1902	+
1903	+type searchResultMsg struct {
1904	+	seq      int
1905	+	kind     tuiMode
1906	+	sessions []sessionindex.Session
1907	+}
1908	+
1909	+type snippetResultMsg struct {
1910	+	seq      int
1911	+	kind     tuiMode
1912	+	snippets map[string]string
1913	+}
1914	+
1915	+const searchDebounceDelay = 50 * time.Millisecond
1916	+
1917	+// searchDebounce schedules a debounce tick. On fire, the model checks the seq and decides
1918	+// whether to actually run the query (so only the last keystroke in a burst queries).
1919	+func searchDebounce(seq int, kind tuiMode) tea.Cmd {
1920	+	return tea.Tick(searchDebounceDelay, func(time.Time) tea.Msg {
1921	+		return searchDebounceMsg{seq: seq, kind: kind}
1922	+	})
1923	+}
1924	+
1925	+// runSearch returns a command that performs the FTS query off the UI thread.
1926	+func (m sessionTUI) runSearch(seq int, kind tuiMode, ctx context.Context) tea.Cmd {
1927	+	store := m.store
1928	+	query, projectID := m.searchQuery, m.projectID
1929	+	if kind == modeProjects {
1930	+		query, projectID = m.globalQuery, m.globalScopeID // "" = all projects, else scoped
1931	+	}
1932	+	fq := ftsQuery(query)
1933	+	return func() tea.Msg {
1934	+		if !queryReady(query) {
1935	+			return searchResultMsg{seq: seq, kind: kind}
1936	+		}
1937	+		sessions, _ := store.SearchContext(ctx, fq, projectID)
1938	+		return searchResultMsg{seq: seq, kind: kind, sessions: sessions}
1939	+	}
1940	+}
1941	+
1942	+// applySearchResults installs async FTS results into the matching list (scoped or global).
1943	+func (m *sessionTUI) applySearchResults(kind tuiMode, sessions []sessionindex.Session) {
1944	+	if kind == modeProjects {
1945	+		m.globalSnippets = map[string]string{}
1946	+		var out []sessionindex.Session
1947	+		for _, s := range sessions {
1948	+			if m.agentFilter == "" || s.Agent == m.agentFilter {
1949	+				out = append(out, s)
1950	+			}
1951	+		}
1952	+		m.globalResults = out
1953	+		if m.globalCursor > len(m.globalResults)-1 {
1954	+			m.globalCursor = len(m.globalResults) - 1
1955	+		}
1956	+		if m.globalCursor < 0 {
1957	+			m.globalCursor = 0
1958	+		}
1959	+		m.globalTop = 0
1960	+		return
1961	+	}
1962	+
1963	+	var out []sessionindex.Session
1964	+	for _, s := range sessions {
1965	+		if m.agentFilter == "" || s.Agent == m.agentFilter {
1966	+			out = append(out, s)
1967	+		}
1968	+	}
1969	+	m.filtered = out
1970	+	m.filteredSnippets = map[string]string{}
1971	+	if m.cursor > len(m.filtered)-1 {
1972	+		m.cursor = len(m.filtered) - 1
1973	+	}
1974	+	if m.cursor < 0 {
1975	+		m.cursor = 0
1976	+	}
1977	+	m.top = 0
1978	+	m.clampScroll()
1979	+}
1980	+
1981	+func (m *sessionTUI) requestVisibleSnippets(kind tuiMode) tea.Cmd {
1982	+	var query string
1983	+	var rows []sessionindex.Session
1984	+	if kind == modeProjects {
1985	+		if !queryReady(m.globalQuery) || len(m.globalResults) == 0 {
1986	+			return nil
1987	+		}
1988	+		h := m.globalHeight()
1989	+		end := min(m.globalTop+h, len(m.globalResults))
1990	+		if m.globalTop >= end {
1991	+			return nil
1992	+		}
1993	+		query = m.globalQuery
1994	+		rows = append([]sessionindex.Session(nil), m.globalResults[m.globalTop:end]...)
1995	+	} else {
1996	+		if !queryReady(m.searchQuery) || len(m.filtered) == 0 {
1997	+			return nil
1998	+		}
1999	+		h := m.listHeight()
2000	+		end := min(m.top+h, len(m.filtered))
2001	+		if m.top >= end {
2002	+			return nil
2003	+		}
2004	+		query = m.searchQuery
2005	+		rows = append([]sessionindex.Session(nil), m.filtered[m.top:end]...)
2006	+	}
2007	+
2008	+	store := m.store
2009	+	fq := ftsQuery(query)
2010	+	m.snippetSeq++
2011	+	seq := m.snippetSeq
2012	+	return func() tea.Msg {
2013	+		snips, _ := store.SnippetsContext(context.Background(), fq, rows)
2014	+		return snippetResultMsg{seq: seq, kind: kind, snippets: snips}
2015	+	}
2016	+}
2017

package main

import "fmt"

type M struct{ seq int }
func (m *M) bump() int { m.seq++; return m.seq }
func ret(m M) (M, int) { return m, m.bump() }

func main() {
	m := M{seq: 0}
	rm, captured := ret(m)
	fmt.Printf("returned model seq=%d, closure-captured seq=%d, match=%v\n", rm.seq, captured, rm.seq==captured)
}
EOF
go run evalorder_test.go; rm -f evalorder_test.go

Verified: under the gc compiler, `return m, m.bump()` runs the mutating method *before* capturing `m`, so the values match — the TUI's `snippetSeq`/`searchSeq` pattern works correctly (though it leans on evaluation order the Go spec leaves unspecified). All tests pass. Here is the review.
